text.DRB: For of them thou shalt learn wisdom, and doctrine of understanding, and to serve great men without blame.
Let not the narration of the ancients escape thee: for they learned of their fathers:
text.NABRE: Do not give ground before scoundrels; it will set them in ambush against you.
text.KJV: 8:11 Rise not vp (in anger) at the presence of an iniurious person, least he lie in waite to entrap thee in thy words.

text.DRB: because of them thou shalt learn understanding, and in time of necessity to give answer.
text.NABRE: Do not lend to one more powerful than yourself; or if you lend, count it as lost.
text.KJV: 8:12 Lend not vnto him that is mightier then thy selfe; for if thou lendest him, count it but lost.

text.DRB: Kindle not the coals of sinners rebuking them, and be not kindled with the flame of the fire of their sins.
text.NABRE: Do not give collateral beyond your means; consider any collateral a debt you must pay.
text.KJV: 8:13 Be not surety aboue thy power: for if thou be surety, take care to pay it.

text.DRB: Stand not against the face of a contumelious person, lest he sit as a spy in wait for thy mouth.
text.NABRE: Do not go to court against a judge, for the case will be settled in his favor.
text.KJV: 8:14 Goe not to law with a iudge, for they will iudge for him according to his honour.
8:15 Trauaile not by the way with a bold fellow, least he become grieuous vnto thee: for he will doe according to his owne will, and thou shalt perish with him through his folly.
text.NABRE: Do not travel with the ruthless lest they weigh you down with calamity; For they will only go their own way, and through their folly you will also perish.
text.NRSV: Do not travel with a reckless person, or he will be a burden to you; for he will do as he pleases, and through his folly you will perish with him.
text.DRB: Lend not to a man mightier than thyself, and if thou doest lend, count it as lost.
Be not surety above thy power: and if thou be surety, think as if thou were to pay it.
text.NABRE: Do not defy the quick-tempered, or ride with them through the desert. For bloodshed is nothing to them; when there is no one to help, they will destroy you.
text.KJV: 8:16 Striue not with an angry man, and goe not with him into a solitary place: for blood is as nothing in his sight, and where there is no helpe, he will ouerthrow thee.

text.DRB: Judge not against a judge: because he judgeth according to that which is just.
text.NABRE: Do not take counsel with simpletons, for they cannot keep a confidence.
text.KJV: 8:17 Consult not with a foole; for he cannot keepe counsell.

text.DRB: With the audacious go not on the way, lest perhaps he burden thee with his evils: for he goeth according to his own will, and thou shalt perish together with his folly.
text.NABRE: Before a stranger do nothing that should be kept secret, for you do not know what it will produce later on.
text.KJV: 8:18 Doe no secret thing before a stranger, for thou knowest not what he will bring forth.

text.DRB: With an angry man make no brawl, and with the audacious go not into the desert: because blood is as nothing before him, and where there is no help, he will overthrow thee.
text.NABRE: Open your heart to no one, do not banish your happiness.
text.KJV: 8:19 Open not thine heart to euery man, least he requite thee with a shrewd turne.

text.DRB: Confer no counsel with fools, for they cannot love but such things as please them.
Before a stranger do no matter of counsel: for thou knowest not what he will bring forth.
Make not thy heart manifest to every man: lest perhaps he repay thee false kindness, and speak reproachfully to thee.

text.KJV: 9:1 Be not iealous ouer the wife of thy bosome, and teach her not an euil lesson against thy selfe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Advice Concerning Women Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, lest you teach her to do evil against you.
text.NRSV: Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, or you will teach her an evil lesson to your own hurt.
text.DRB: Be not jealous over the wife of thy bosom, lest she shew upon thee the malice of wicked doctrine.

text.KJV: 9:2 Giue not thy soule vnto a woman, to set her foot vpon thy substance.
text.NABRE: Do not give a woman power over you to trample on your dignity.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself to a woman and let her trample down your strength.
text.DRB: Give not to a woman the power of thy soul, lest she go in thy strength, and thou be confounded.

text.KJV: 9:3 Meete not with an harlot, least thou fall into her snares.
text.NABRE: Do not go near a strange woman, lest you fall into her snares.
text.NRSV: Do not go near a loose woman, or you will fall into her snares.
text.DRB: Look not upon a woman that is desirous of many: lest perhaps thou fall into her snares.
With her that is a dancer be not daily conversant, nor hear her, lest perhaps thou perish in her efficacy.
text.NABRE: Do not dally with a singer, lest you be captivated by her charms.
text.KJV: 9:4 Use not much the companie of a woman that is a singer, least thou be taken with her attempts.

text.DRB: Behold not a virgin, lest perhaps thou be scandalized in her beauty.
text.NABRE: Do not entertain any thoughts about a virgin, lest you be enmeshed in damages for her.
text.KJV: 9:5 Gaze not on a maide, that thou fall not by those things, that are pretious in her.

text.DRB: Give not thy soul to harlots in any point: lest thou destroy thyself, and thine inheritance.
text.NABRE: Do not give yourself to a prostitute lest you lose your inheritance.
text.KJV: 9:6 Giue not thy soule vnto harlots, that thou loose not thine inheritance.

text.DRB: Look not round about in the ways of the city, nor wander up and down in the streets thereof.
text.NABRE: Do not look around the streets of the city or wander through its squares.
text.KJV: 9:7 Looke not round about thee, in the streets of the citie, neither wander thou in the solitary places thereof.
9:8 Turne away thine eye from a beautifull woman, and looke not vpon anothers beautie: for many haue beene deceiued by the beautie of a woman, for heerewith loue is kindled as a fire.
text.NABRE: Avert your eyes from a shapely woman; do not gaze upon beauty that is not yours; Through woman’s beauty many have been ruined, for love of it burns like fire.
text.NRSV: Turn away your eyes from a shapely woman, and do not gaze at beauty belonging to another; many have been seduced by a woman’s beauty, and by it passion is kindled like a fire.
text.DRB: Turn away thy face from a trimmed woman, and gaze not about upon another's beauty.
By the beauty of a woman many have perished: and hereby concupiscence is inflamed as a fire.
text.NABRE: Never recline at table with a married woman, or drink intoxicants with her, Lest your heart be drawn to her and you go down in blood to the grave.
text.KJV: 9:9 Sit not at all with another mans wife, nor sit downe with her in thine armes, and spend not thy money with her at the wine, least thine heart incline vnto her, and so thorough thy desire thou fall into destruction.
9:10 Forsake not an old friend, for the new is not comparable to him: a new friend is as new wine: when it is old, thou shalt drinke it with pleasure.
text.NABRE: Choice of Friends Do not abandon old friends; new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine— when it has aged, you drink it with pleasure.
text.NRSV: Do not abandon old friends, for new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine; when it has aged, you can drink it with pleasure.
text.DRB: Every woman, that is an harlot, shall be trodden upon as dung in the way.

text.KJV: 9:11 Enuy not the glory of a sinner: for thou knowest not what shall be his end.
text.NABRE: Do not envy the wicked for you do not know when their day will come.
text.NRSV: Do not envy the success of sinners, for you do not know what their end will be.
text.DRB: Many having admired the beauty of another man's wife, have become reprobate, for her communication burneth as fire.
Sit not at all with another man's wife, nor repose upon the bed with her:
text.NABRE: Do not delight in the pleasures of the ungodly; remember, they will not die unpunished.
text.KJV: 9:12 Delight not in the thing that the vngodly haue pleasure in, but remember they shall not goe vnpunished vnto their graue.

9:13 Keepe thee farre from the man that hath power to kill, so shalt thou not doubt the feare of death: and if thou come vnto him, make no fault, least he take away thy life presently: remember that thou goest in the midst of snares, and that thou walkest vpon the battlements of the citie.
text.NABRE: Keep away from those who have power to kill, and you will not be filled with the dread of death. But if you come near them, do not offend them, lest they take away your life. Know that you are stepping among snares and walking over a net.
text.NRSV: Keep far from those who have power to kill, and you will not be haunted by the fear of death.
text.DRB: and strive not with her at the wine, lest perhaps thy heart decline toward her, & with thy blood thou fall into perdition.
Forsake not an old friend: for the new will not be like to him.
text.NABRE: As best you can, answer your neighbor, and associate with the wise.
text.KJV: 9:14 As neere as thou canst, ghesse at thy neighbour, and consult with the wise.
9:15 Let thy talke be with the wise, and all thy communication in the law of the most high.
text.NABRE: With the learned exchange ideas; and let all your conversation be about the law of the Most High.
text.NRSV: Reason with the intelligent, and let all your argument be about the law of the Most High.
text.DRB: Anew friend, is as new wine: it shall wax old, and thou shalt drink it with sweetness.
Do not zealously desire the glory and the riches of a sinner: for thou knowest not what his subversion shall be.
text.NABRE: Take the righteous for your table companions; and let your glory be in the fear of God.
text.KJV: 9:16 And let iust men eate and drinke with thee, and let thy glorying be in the feare of the Lord.
9:17 For the hand of the artificer, the worke shall be commended: and the wise ruler of the people, for his speech.
text.NABRE: Concerning Rulers Work by skilled hands will earn praise; but the people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.NRSV: A work is praised for the skill of the artisan, so a people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.DRB: Let not the injury of the unjust please thee, knowing that even to hell the impious shall not please.
Be far from the man that hath power to kill, and thou shalt not suspect the fear of death.
text.NABRE: Loud mouths are feared in their city, and whoever is reckless in speech is hated.
text.KJV: 9:18 A man of an ill tongue is dangerous in his citie, and he that is rash in his talke shall be hated.

text.DRB: And if thou come to him, commit nothing, lest perhaps he take away thy life.
Know it to be communication with death; because thou shalt go in the midst of snares, and shalt walk upon the weapons of the sorrowful.
According to thy power beware thee of thy neighbour, and treat with the wise and prudent.
Let just men be thy guests, and let thy gloriation be in the fear of God,
and let the cogitation of God be in thy understanding, & all thine enarration in the precepts of the Highest.
Works shall be praised in the hands of artificers, and the Prince of the people in the wisdom of his speech, but the word of the ancients in the sense.
Aman full of tongue is terrible in his city, and he that is rash in his word shall be odious.

text.KJV: 10:1 A wise iudge will instruct his people, & the gouernement of a prudent man is well ordered.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 A wise magistrate gives stability to his people, and government by the intelligent is well ordered.
text.NRSV: A wise magistrate educates his people, and the government of an intelligent person is well ordered.
text.DRB: A wise judge shall judge his people, and the principality of the wise shall be stable.

text.KJV: 10:2 As the iudge of the people is himselfe, so are his officers, and what maner of man the ruler of the citie is, such are all they that dwell therein.
text.NABRE: As the people’s judge, so the officials; as the head of a city, so the inhabitants.
text.NRSV: As the people’s judge is, so are his officials; and as the ruler of the city is, so are all its inhabitants.
text.DRB: According to the judge of the people, so also are his ministers: and what manner of man the ruler of a city is, such also are the inhabitants therein.

text.KJV: 10:3 An vnwise king destroyeth his people, but through the prudence of them which are in authoritie, the citie shalbe inhabited.
text.NABRE: A reckless king destroys his people, but a city grows through the intelligence of its princes.
text.NRSV: An untrained king ruins his people, but a city prospers through the intelligence of its rulers.
text.DRB: An unwise king shall destroy his people: and cities shall be inhabited by the understanding of the prudent.

text.KJV: 10:4 The power of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and in due time hee will set ouer it one that is profitable.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty over the earth is in the hand of God, who appoints the right person for the right time.
text.NRSV: The government of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and over it he will raise up the right leader for the time.
text.DRB: The power of the earth is in the hand of God, and he will raise up a profitable ruler for a time over it.
The prosperity of man is in the hand of God, & upon the face of the scribe he will put his honour.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty over everyone is in the hand of God, who imparts his majesty to the ruler.
text.KJV: 10:5 In the hand of God is the prosperitie of man: and vpon the person of the scribe shall he lay his honour.

text.DRB: Any injury of thy neighbour remember not, and do nothing by works of injury.
text.NABRE: The Sin of Pride No matter what the wrong, never harm your neighbor or go the way of arrogance.
text.KJV: 10:6 Beare not hatred to thy neighbour for euery wrong, and do nothing at all by iniurious practises.
10:7 Pride is hatefull before God, and man: and by both doeth one commit iniquitie.
text.NABRE: Odious to the Lord and to mortals is pride, and for both oppression is a crime.
text.NRSV: Arrogance is hateful to the Lord and to mortals, and injustice is outrageous to both.
text.DRB: Pride is odious before God and men: and all the iniquity of the nations is execrable.

text.KJV: 10:8 Because of vnrighteous dealings, iniuries, and riches got by deceit, the kingdome is translated from one people to another.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty is transferred from one people to another because of the lawlessness of the proud.
text.NRSV: Sovereignty passes from nation to nation because of injustice and insolence and wealth.
text.DRB: A kingdom is translated from nation unto nation, because of injustices, and injuries, and contumelies, and diverse deceits.

text.KJV: 10:9 Why is earth and ashes proude? There is not a more wicked thing, then a couetous man: for such an one setteth his owne soule to sale, because while he liueth, he casteth away his bowels.
text.NABRE: Why are dust and ashes proud? Even during life the body decays.
text.NRSV: How can dust and ashes be proud? Even in life the human body decays.
text.DRB: But nothing is more wicked than the covetous man. Why is earth and ashes proud?
Nothing is more wicked than to love money. For he hath his soul also to sell: because in his life he hath cast forth his most inward things:
text.NABRE: A slight illness—the doctor jests; a king today—tomorrow he is dead.
text.KJV: 10:10 The Phisition cutteth off a long disease, and he that is to day a King, to morrow shall die.

text.DRB: All power is of short life. Long sickness grieveth the Physician.
text.NABRE: When a people die, they inherit corruption and worms, gnats and maggots.
text.KJV: 10:11 For when a man is dead, hee shall inherite creeping things, beastes and wormes.
10:12 The beginning of pride is, when one departeth from God, and his heart is turned away from his maker.
text.NABRE: The beginning of pride is stubbornness in withdrawing the heart from one’s Maker.
text.NRSV: The beginning of human pride is to forsake the Lord; the heart has withdrawn from its Maker.
text.DRB: Short sickness the Physician cutteth off at the first: so also the king is today, & tomorrow he shall die.
For when a man shall die, he shall inherit serpents, and beasts, and worms.
text.NABRE: For sin is a reservoir of insolence, a source which runs over with vice; Because of it God sends unheard-of afflictions and strikes people with utter ruin.
text.KJV: 10:13 For pride is the beginning of sinne, and hee that hath it, shall powre out abomination: and therefore the Lord brought vpon them strange calamities, and ouerthrew them vtterly.
10:14 The Lord hath cast downe the thrones of proud Princes, and set vp the meeke in their stead.
text.NABRE: God overturns the thrones of the proud and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.NRSV: The Lord overthrows the thrones of rulers, and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.DRB: The beginning of the pride of man, is to apostatate from God:
because his heart is departed from him that made him, for pride is the beginning of all sin: he that holdeth it, shall be filled with curses, & it shall subvert him in the end.
text.NABRE: God plucks up the roots of the proud, and plants the lowly in their place.
text.KJV: 10:15 The Lord hath plucked vp the rootes of the proud nations: and planted the lowly in their place.

text.DRB: Therefore hath our Lord dishonoured the congregations of the evil, & hath destroyed them even to the end.
text.NABRE: The Lord lays waste the lands of the nations, and destroys them to the very foundations of the earth.
text.KJV: 10:16 The Lord ouerthrew countreys of the heathen: and destroyed them to the foundations of the earth.

text.DRB: God hath destroyed the seats of proud Princes, and hath made the meek sit in their stead.
text.NABRE: He removes them from the earth, destroying them, erasing their memory from the world.
text.KJV: 10:17 He tooke some of them away, and destroyed them, and hath made their memoriall to cease from the earth.
10:18 Pride was not made for men, nor furious anger for them that are borne of a woman.
text.NABRE: Insolence does not befit mortals, nor impudent anger those born of women.
text.NRSV: Pride was not created for human beings, or violent anger for those born of women.
text.DRB: God hath made the roots of the proud nations to wither, and hath planted the humble of the nations themselves.
Our Lord hath subverted the lands of the gentiles, and hath destroyed them even to the foundation.
text.NABRE: True Glory Whose offspring can be honorable? Human offspring. Those who fear the Lord are honorable offspring. Whose offspring can be disgraceful? Human offspring. Those who transgress the commandment are disgraceful offspring.
text.KJV: 10:19 They that feare the Lord are a sure seed, and they that loue him, an honourable plant: they that regard not the Law, are a dishonourable seed, they that transgresse the commandements, are a deceiuable seed.

text.DRB: He hath made of them to wither, and hath destroyed them, and hath made the memory of them to cease from the earth.
text.NABRE: Among relatives their leader is honored; but whoever fears God is honored among God’s people.
text.KJV: 10:20 Among brethren he that is chiefe is honourable, so are they that feare the Lord in his eyes.

text.DRB: God hath destroyed the memory of the proud, and hath left the memory of them that are humble in understanding.
text.KJV: 10:21 The feare of the Lord goeth before the obtayning of authoritie: but roughnesse and pride, is the loosing thereof.

text.DRB: Pride was not created to men: nor wrath to the nation of women.
text.KJV: 10:22 Whether hee bee rich, noble, or poore, their glorie is the feare of the Lord.

text.DRB: That seed of men shall be honoured, which feareth God: but that seed shall be dishonoured, which transgresseth the commandments of our Lord.
text.KJV: 10:23 It is not meet to despise the poore man that hath vnderstanding, neither is it conuenient to magnifie a sinnefull man.
10:24 Great men, and Iudges, and Potentates shall bee honoured, yet is there none of them greater then he that feareth the Lord.
text.NRSV: The great, the judge, and the ruler are honored, but none of them is greater than the one who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: In the midst of brethren their ruler shall be in honour: and they that fear our Lord, shall be in his eyes.
The glory of the rich, of the honourable, and of the poor, is the fear of God:
text.KJV: 10:25 Unto the seruant that is wise, shall they that are free doe seruice: and hee that hath knowledge, will not grudge when he is reformed.

text.DRB: Despise not the just man that is poor, and magnify not the sinful man that is rich.
text.KJV: 10:26 Be not ouerwise in doing thy busines, and boast not thy selfe in the time of thy distresse.
10:27 Better is he that laboureth and aboundeth in all things, then hee that boasteth himselfe, and wanteth bread.
text.NRSV: Better are those who work and have an abundance of everything, than those who go about boasting of their rank but lack food.
text.DRB: The great one, and the judge, and the mighty is in honour, and there is none greater than he that feareth God.

text.KJV: 10:28 My sonne, glorifie thy soule in meekenesse, and giue it honour according to the dignitie thereof.
text.NRSV: My child, honor yourself with humility, and give yourself the esteem you deserve.
text.DRB: Free men will serve a servant that is wise: and a man that is prudent and hath discipline will not murmur being rebuked, and the ignorant shall not be honoured.
Extol not thyself in doing thy work, and linger not in the time of distress:
text.KJV: 10:29 Who wil iustifie him that sinneth against his owne soule? and who will honour him that dishonoureth his owne life?

text.DRB: better is he that worketh, and aboundeth in all things, than he that glorieth, and lacketh bread.
text.KJV: 10:30 The poore man is honoured for his skill, and the rich man is honoured for his riches.

text.DRB: Son in mildness keep thy soul, and give him honour according to his desert.
text.KJV: 10:31 Hee that is honoured in pouertie, how much more in riches? And he that is dishonourable in riches, how much more in pouertie?

text.DRB: Him that sinneth against his own soul who shall justify? And who shall honour him that dishonoureth his own soul?
The poor man is glorified by his discipline and fear: & there is a man that is honoured for his substance.
But he that is glorified in poverty, how much more in substance? And he that is glorified in substance, let him fear poverty.

text.KJV: 11:1 Wisedome lifteth vp the head of him that is of low degree, and maketh him to sit among great men.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The wisdom of the poor lifts their head high and sets them among princes.
text.NRSV: The wisdom of the lowly lifts up their heads, and seats them among the great.
text.DRB: The wisdom of the humble shall exalt his head, & shall make him sit in the midst of great men.

text.KJV: 11:2 Commend not a man for his beautie, neither abhorre a man for his outward appearance.
text.NABRE: Do not praise anyone for good looks; or despise anyone because of appearance.
text.NRSV: Do not praise individuals for their good looks, or loathe anyone because of appearance.
text.DRB: Praise not a man in his beauty, neither despise a man by his look.

text.KJV: 11:3 The Bee is little among such as flie, but her fruite is the chiefe of sweete things.
text.NABRE: The bee is least among winged creatures, but it reaps the choicest of harvests.
text.NRSV: The bee is small among flying creatures, but her product is the best of sweet things.
text.DRB: The bee is small among fowls, and her fruit hath the beginning of sweetness.

text.KJV: 11:4 Boast not of thy cloathing and raiment, and exalt not thy selfe in the day of honour: for the workes of the Lord are wonderfull, and his workes among men are hidden.
text.NABRE: Do not mock the one who wears only a loin-cloth, or scoff at a person’s bitter day. For strange are the deeds of the Lord, hidden from mortals his work.
text.NRSV: Do not boast about your clothing, or lift yourself up on the day you are honored; for the works of the Lord are wonderful, and his activities are hidden from mortals.
text.DRB: In apparel do not glory at any time, nor be extolled in the day of thine honour, because the works of the Highest only be marvelous, and his works are glorious, and secret, and not seen.
Many tyrants have sit in the throne, and he whom no man would think hath worn the crown.
text.NABRE: Many are the oppressed who rise to the throne; some that none would consider wear a crown.
text.KJV: 11:5 Many kings haue sit downe vpon the ground, and one that was neuer thought of, hath worne the crowne.

text.DRB: Many mighty men have been greatly oppressed, and the glorious have been delivered into the hands of others.
text.NABRE: Many are the exalted who fall into utter disgrace, many the honored who are given into the power of the few.
text.KJV: 11:6 Many mightie men haue beene greatly disgraced: and the honourable deliuered into other mens hands.
11:7 Blame not before thou hast examined the trueth: vnderstand first, and then rebuke.
text.NABRE: Moderation and Patience Before investigating, do not find fault; examine first, then criticize.
text.NRSV: Do not find fault before you investigate; examine first, and then criticize.
text.DRB: Before thou inquire, blame no man: and when thou hast inquired, chasten justly.

text.KJV: 11:8 Answere not, before thou hast heard the cause: neither interrupt men in the midst of their talke.
text.NABRE: Before listening, do not say a word, interrupt no one in the midst of speaking.
text.NRSV: Do not answer before you listen, and do not interrupt when another is speaking.
text.DRB: Before thou hear, answer not a word, and in the midst of ancients add not to speak.

text.KJV: 11:9 Striue not in a matter that concerneth thee not: and sit not in iudgement with sinners.
text.NABRE: Do not dispute about what is not your concern; in the quarrels of the arrogant do not take part.
text.NRSV: Do not argue about a matter that does not concern you, and do not sit with sinners when they judge a case.
text.DRB: Strive not for that thing, which doth not molest thee: and consist not in the judgement of sinners.

text.KJV: 11:10 My sonne, meddle not with many matters: for if thou meddle much, thou shalt not be innocent: and if thou follow after, thou shalt not obtaine, neither shalt thou escape by flying.
text.NABRE: My son, why increase your anxiety, since whoever is greedy for wealth will not be blameless? Even if you chase after it, you will never overtake it; and by fleeing you will not escape.
text.NRSV: My child, do not busy yourself with many matters; if you multiply activities, you will not go unpunished.
text.DRB: Son let not thy doings be in many things: and if thou be rich, thou shalt not be free from sin: for if thou pursue, thou shalt not attain: and if thou run before, thou shalt not escape.
There is one that laboureth, and hasteneth, and is a sorrowful impious man, and so much the more he shall not abound.
text.NABRE: One may work and struggle and drive, and fall short all the same.
text.KJV: 11:11 There is one that laboureth and taketh paines, and maketh haste, and is so much the more behinde.

text.DRB: There is a lither man that wanteth recovery, more failing in strength, and abounding in poverty:
text.NABRE: Others go their way broken-down drifters, with little strength and great misery— Yet the eye of the Lord looks favorably upon them, shaking them free of the stinking mire.
text.KJV: 11:12 Againe, there is another that is slow, and hath neede of helpe, wanting abilitie, and full of pouertie, yet the eye of the Lord looked vpon him for good, and set him vp from his low estate,

text.DRB: and the eye of God hath respected him in good, and hath erected him from his low estate, and hath exalted his head: and many have marveled at him, and have honoured God.
text.NABRE: He lifts up their heads and exalts them to the amazement of the many.
text.KJV: 11:13 And lifted vp his head from miserie, so that many that saw it, marueiled at him.
11:14 Prosperitie and aduersitie, life and death, pouerty and riches, come of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Good and evil, life and death, poverty and riches—all are from the Lord.
text.NRSV: Good things and bad, life and death, poverty and wealth, come from the Lord.
text.DRB: Good things and evil, life and death, poverty and honesty are of God.
Wisdom and discipline, and the knowledge of the law are with God. Love, and the ways of good things are with him.
text.KJV: 11:15 Wisedome, knowledge, and vnderstanding of the Lawe, are of the Lord: loue, & the way of good workes, are from him.

text.DRB: Error & darkness are created with sinners: and they that rejoice in evils, wax old in evil.
text.KJV: 11:16 Errour and darkenesse had their beginning together with sinners: and euill shall waxe old with them that glory therein.

text.DRB: The gift of God is permanent to the just, and his prospering shall have success for ever.
text.KJV: 11:17 The gift of the Lord remaineth with the godly, and his fauour bringeth prosperitie for euer.

text.DRB: There is that is enriched by doing sparingly, and this is the portion of his reward,
text.KJV: 11:18 There is that waxeth rich by his warinesse, and pinching, and this is the portion of his reward:

text.DRB: in that he saith: I have found me rest, and now I will eat of my goods alone:
text.KJV: 11:19 Whereas he sayth, I haue found rest, and now will eate continually of my goods, and yet hee knoweth not what time shall come vpon him, and that hee must leaue those things to others, and die.

text.DRB: and he knoweth not that time passeth, & death approacheth, and he must leave all to others, and shall die.
text.KJV: 11:20 Be stedfast in thy couenant, and be conuersant therein, and waxe olde in thy worke.
11:21 Marueile not at the workes of sinners, but trust in the Lord, and abide in thy labour: for it is an easie thing in the sight of the Lord, on the sudden to make a poore man rich.
text.NRSV: Do not wonder at the works of a sinner, but trust in the Lord and keep at your job; for it is easy in the sight of the Lord to make the poor rich suddenly, in a moment.
text.DRB: Stand in thy covenant, and commune therein, and grow old in the work of thy commandments.
Abide not in the works of sinners. But trust in God, and tarry in thy place.
text.KJV: 11:22 The blessing of the Lord is in the reward of the godly, and suddenly he maketh his blessing to flourish.

text.DRB: For it is easy in the eyes of God suddenly to enrich the poor man.
text.KJV: 11:23 Say not, what profit is there of my seruice? and what good things shal I haue hereafter?

text.DRB: The blessing of God hasteneth to the reward of the just, and in a swift hour his prospering fructifieth.
text.KJV: 11:24 Againe, say not, I haue enough, and possesse many things; and what euill can come to me hereafter?

text.DRB: Say not: What need I, and what good shall I have by this?
text.KJV: 11:25 In the day of prosperitie, there is a forgetfulnesse of affliction: and in the day of affliction, there is no remembrance of prosperitie.
11:26 For it is an easie thing vnto the Lord in the day of death, to reward a man according to his wayes.
text.NRSV: It is easy for the Lord on the day of death to reward individuals according to their conduct.
text.DRB: Say not: I am sufficient for myself: and what shall I be made worse by this?
In the day of good things be not unmindful of evils: and in the day of evils be not unmindful of good things:
text.KJV: 11:27 The affliction of an houre, maketh a man forget pleasure: and in his end, his deeds shalbe discouered.
11:28 Iudge none blessed before his death: for a man shall bee knowen in his children.
text.NRSV: Call no one happy before his death; by how he ends, a person becomes known.
text.DRB: because it is easy before God in the day of death to reward every one according to his ways.
The malice of an hour maketh oblivion of great voluptuousness, and in the end of a man in the disclosing of his works.
text.KJV: 11:29 Bring not euery man into thine house, for the deceitfull man hath many traines.

text.DRB: Before death praise no man, because a man is known in his children.
text.KJV: 11:30 Like as a Partrich taken  in a cage, so is the heart of the proud; and like as a spie, watcheth hee for thy fall.

text.DRB: Bring not every man into thine house: for there be many trains of the deceitful man.
text.KJV: 11:31 For hee lieth in wait, and turneth good into euill, and in things worthy praise, will lay blame vpon thee.

text.DRB: For as the stomachs belch of stinking breaths, and as the partridge is brought in to the cage, and as the doe into the snare: so also the heart of the proud, and as a watch-man that seeth the fall of his neighbour.
text.KJV: 11:32 Of a sparke of fire, a heape of coales is kindled: and a sinnefull man layeth waite for blood.

text.DRB: For turning good things into evil he lieth in wait, and on the elect he will lay a blot.
text.KJV: 11:33 Take heed of a mischieuous man, (for hee worketh wickednesse) lest hee bring vpon thee a perpetuall blot.

text.DRB: For of one spark fire is increased, and of a deceitful man blood is increased: and a sinful man lieth in wait for blood.
text.KJV: 11:34 Receiue a stranger into thine house, and hee will disturbe thee, and turne thee out of thine owne.

text.DRB: Take heed of thyself of the pestiferous person, for he forgeth evils: lest perhaps he bring upon thee derision for ever.
Admit a stranger to thee, and he shall overthrow thee in an hurlwind, & shall make thee an alien from thine own.

text.KJV: 12:1 When thou wilt doe good, know to whō thou doest it, so shalt thou be thanked for thy benefites.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 If you do good, know for whom you are doing it, and your kindness will have its effect.
text.NRSV: If you do good, know to whom you do it, and you will be thanked for your good deeds.
text.DRB: If thou wilt do good, know to whom thou doest it, and there shall be much thank in thy good deeds.

text.KJV: 12:2 Do good to the godly man, and thou shalt find a recompence, and if not from him, yet from the most high.
text.NABRE: Do good to the righteous and reward will be yours, if not from them, from the Lord.
text.NRSV: Do good to the devout, and you will be repaid—if not by them, certainly by the Most High.
text.DRB: Do good to the just, and thou shalt find great reward: and if not of him, assuredly of our Lord.
For it is not well with him, that is ever occupied in evil things, and that giveth not alms: because the Highest both hateth sinners, and hath mercy on them that are penitent.
text.NABRE: No good comes to those who give comfort to the wicked, nor is it an act of mercy that they do.
text.KJV: 12:3 There can no good come to him that is alwayes occupied in euill: nor to him that giueth no almes.
12:4 Giue to the godly man, and helpe not a sinner.
text.NABRE: Give to the good but refuse the sinner;
text.NRSV: Give to the devout, but do not help the sinner.
text.DRB: Give to the merciful, and receive not the sinner: both to the impious & to sinners he will repay vengeance, keeping them unto the day of vengeance.
Give to the good, and receive not a sinner.
text.NABRE: refresh the downtrodden but give nothing to the proud. No arms for combat should you give them, lest they use these against you; Twofold evil you will obtain for every good deed you do for them.
text.KJV: 12:5 Doe well vnto him that is lowly, but giue not to the vngodly: hold backe thy bread, and giue it not vnto him, lest he ouermaster thee thereby. For  thou shalt receiue twice as much euill, for all the good thou shalt haue done vnto him.

text.DRB: Do good to the humble, and give not to the impious: prohibit to give him bread, lest therein he be mightier than thou:
text.NABRE: For God also hates sinners, and takes vengeance on evildoers.
text.KJV: 12:6 For the most High hateth sinners, and will repay vengeance vnto the vngodly, and keepeth them against the mightie day of their punishment.

text.DRB: for thou shalt find double evils in all the good, whatsoever thou shalt do to him: because the Highest hateth sinners, and will repay vengeance to the impious.
text.KJV: 12:7 Giue vnto the good, and helpe not the sinner.
12:8 A friend cannot be knowen in prosperitie, and an enemy cannot be hidden in aduersitie.
text.NRSV: A friend is not known in prosperity, nor is an enemy hidden in adversity.
text.DRB: Afriend shall not be known in prosperity, and an enemy shall not be hid in adversity.
In the prosperity of a man, his enemies are in sorrow, and in affliction a friend is known.
text.KJV: 12:9 In the prosperitie of a man, enemies will be grieued, but in his aduersitie, euen a friend will depart.
12:10 Neuer trust thine enemie: for like as yron rusteth, so is his wickednesse.
text.NRSV: Never trust your enemy, for like corrosion in copper, so is his wickedness.
text.DRB: Credit not thine enemy for ever: for as a brass pot his wickedness rusteth:
and if humbling himself he go crouching, be advised in thy mind, and beware of him.
text.KJV: 12:11 Though he humble himselfe, and goe crouching, yet take good heed, and beware of him, and thou shalt bee vnto him, as if thou hadst wiped a looking glasse, and thou shalt knowe that his rust hath not beene altogether wiped away.

text.DRB: Place him not by thee, neither let him sit on thy right hand, lest perhaps turning into thy place, he seek after thy seat: and at the last thou know my words, and be pricked in my sayings.
text.KJV: 12:12 Set him not by thee, lest when he hath ouerthrowen thee, he stand vp in thy place, neither let him sit at thy right hand, lest he seeke to take thy seat, and thou at the last remember my wordes, and be pricked therewith.
12:13 Who will pitie a charmer that is bitten with a serpent, or any such as come nigh wilde beasts?
text.NRSV: Who pities a snake charmer when he is bitten, or all those who go near wild animals?
text.DRB: Who will have pity upon the enchanter striking of a serpent, or of any that come near to beasts? So also he that keepeth company with a wicked man, and is wrapped in his sins.

text.KJV: 12:14 So one that goeth to a sinner, and is defiled with him in his sinnes, who will pitie?
text.NRSV: So no one pities a person who associates with a sinner and becomes involved in his sins.
text.DRB: For one hour he will tarry with thee: but if thou decline, he will not abide it.
In his lips the enemy speaketh sweetly, and in his heart he lieth in wait, that he may overthrow thee into the pit.
text.KJV: 12:15 For a while hee will abide with thee, but if thou begin to fall, he wil not tarie.

12:16 An enemie speaketh sweetly with his lippes, but in his heart he imagineth how to throw thee into a pit: hee will weepe with his eyes, but if he find opportunitie, hee will not be satisfied with blood.
text.NRSV: An enemy has sweetness on his lips, but in his heart he plans to throw you into a pit; an enemy may have tears in his eyes, but if he finds an opportunity he will never have enough of your blood.
text.DRB: In his eyes the enemy weepeth: and if he may find a time, he will not be satisfied with blood:
and if evils happen to thee, thou shalt find him there first.
text.KJV: 12:17 If aduersitie come vpon thee, thou shalt find him there first, & though he pretend to helpe thee, yet shal he vndermine thee.

text.DRB: In his eyes the enemy weepeth, and as it were helping thee, he will undermine thy feet.
text.KJV: 12:18 He will shake his head and clap his handes, and whisper much, and change his countenance.

text.DRB: He will shake his head, and clap his hand, and whispering many things he will change his countenance.

text.KJV: 13:1 He that toucheth pitch, shal be defiled therewith, and hee that hath fellowship with a proude man, shall be like vnto him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 Caution Regarding Associates Touch pitch and you blacken your hand; associate with scoundrels and you learn their ways.
text.NRSV: Whoever touches pitch will be defiled, and whoever associates with the proud will become like them.
text.DRB: He that toucheth pitch, shall be defiled with it: and he that communicateth with the proud, shall put on pride.

text.KJV: 13:2 Burthen not thy selfe aboue thy power, while thou liuest, and haue no fellowship with one that is mightier, and richer then thy selfe. For how agree the kettle and the earthen pot together? for if the one be smitten against the other, it shall be broken.
text.NABRE: Do not lift a weight too heavy for you, or associate with anyone wealthier than you. How can the clay pot go with the metal cauldron? When they knock together, the pot will be smashed:
text.NRSV: Do not lift a weight beyond your strength, or associate with one who is mightier and richer than you.
text.DRB: He shall take a burden upon him that communicateth with one more honourable than himself. And be not companion with one richer than thyself.

text.KJV: 13:3 The rich man hath done wrong, and yet he threatneth withall: the poore is wronged, and he must intreat also.
text.NABRE: The rich do wrong and boast of it, while the poor are wronged and beg forgiveness.
text.NRSV: A rich person does a wrong, and even adds insults; a poor person suffers a wrong, and must add apologies.
text.DRB: What society shall the cauldron have with the earthen pot? For when they shall knock one against the other, it shall be broken.
The rich man hath done unjustly, and he will fume: but the poor man being hurt will hold his peace.
text.NABRE: As long as the rich can use you they will enslave you, but when you are down and out they will abandon you.
text.KJV: 13:4 If thou be for his profit, he will vse thee: but if thou haue nothing, he will forsake thee.

text.DRB: If thou give, he will take thee: and if thou have not, he will forsake thee.
text.NABRE: As long as you have anything they will live with you, but they will drain you dry without remorse.
text.KJV: 13:5 If thou haue any thing, he will liue with thee, yea he will make thee bare, and will not be sorie for it.

text.DRB: If thou have, he will live with thee, and will empty thee, and he will not be sorry for thee.
text.NABRE: When they need you they will deceive you and smile at you and raise your hopes; they will speak kindly to you and say, “What do you need?”
text.KJV: 13:6 If he haue need of thee, hee will deceiue thee, and smile vpon thee, and put thee in hope, he will speake thee faire, and say, What wantest thou?

text.DRB: If thou be necessary for him, he will supplant thee, and smiling will put thee in hope, telling thee good things, and will say: What wantest thou?
text.NABRE: They will embarrass you at their dinner parties, and finally laugh at you. Afterwards, when they see you, they will pass you by, and shake their heads at you.
text.KJV: 13:7 And hee will shame thee by his meates, vntill he haue drawen thee drie twice or thrice, and at the last hee will laugh thee to scorne: afterward when he seeth thee, he will forsake thee, and shake his head at thee.

text.DRB: And he will confound thee in his meats, till he empty thee twice & thrice, and at the last he will mock thee: and afterward seeing he will forsake thee, and will shake his head at thee.
text.NABRE: Be on guard: do not act too boldly; do not be like those who lack sense.
text.KJV: 13:8 Beware that thou bee not deceiued, and brought downe in thy iolitie.

text.DRB: Humble thyself to God, and expect his hands.
text.NABRE: When the influential draw near, keep your distance; then they will urge you all the more.
text.KJV: 13:9 If thou be inuited of a mighty man, withdraw thy selfe, and so much the more will he inuite thee.
13:10 Presse thou not vpon him, lest thou be put backe, stand not farre off, lest thou be forgotten.
text.NABRE: Do not draw too close, lest you be rebuffed, but do not keep too far away lest you be regarded as an enemy.
text.NRSV: Do not push forward, or you may be repulsed; and do not keep too far away, or you may be forgotten.
text.DRB: Take heed lest seduced into folly thou be humbled.
Be not humble in thy wisdom, lest humbled thou be seduced into folly.
text.NABRE: Do not venture to be free with them, do not trust their many words; For by prolonged talk they will test you, and though smiling they will probe you.
text.KJV: 13:11 Affect not to be made equall vnto him in talke, and beleeue not his many words: for with much communication will he tempt thee, and smiling vpon thee will get out thy secrets.

text.DRB: Being called of the mightier, depart: for by this he will call thee the more.
text.NABRE: Mercilessly they will make you a laughingstock, and will not refrain from injury or chains.
text.KJV: 13:12 But cruelly he will lay vp thy words, and will not spare to doe thee hurt, and to put thee in prison.

text.DRB: Be not importune, lest thou be rejected: & be not far from him, lest thou go into oblivion.
text.NABRE: Be on your guard and take care never to accompany lawless people.
text.KJV: 13:13 Obserue and take good heed, for thou walkest in peril of thy ouerthrowing: when thou hearest these things, awake in thy sleepe.

text.DRB: Stay not to speak fellow-like with him: neither credit his many words. For by much talk he will prove thee, and smiling will examine thee of thy secrets.
text.KJV: 13:14 Loue the Lord all thy life, and call vpon him for thy saluation.
13:15 Euery beast loueth his like, and euery man loueth his neighbour.
text.NRSV: Every creature loves its like, and every person his neighbor.
text.DRB: His cruel mind will keep thy words: and he will not spare for malice, and for bands.

text.KJV: 13:16 All flesh consorteth according to kind, and a man will cleaue to his like:
text.NRSV: All living beings associate with their own kind, and people stick to those like themselves.
text.DRB: Take heed to thyself, and attend diligently to thine hearing: because thou walkest with thy subversion.
But hearing those things see as it were in sleep, and thou shalt watch.
text.KJV: 13:17 What fellowship hath the wolfe with the lambe? so the sinner with the godly.
13:18 What agreement is there betweene the Hyena and a dogge? and what peace betweene the rich and the poore?
text.NRSV: What peace is there between a hyena and a dog? And what peace between the rich and the poor?
text.DRB: Love God all thy life, and invocate him for thy salvation.

text.KJV: 13:19 As the wilde asse is the lyons pray in the wildernesse: so the rich eate vp the poore.
text.NRSV: Wild asses in the wilderness are the prey of lions; likewise the poor are feeding grounds for the rich.
text.DRB: Every beast loveth the like to it-self: so also every man the nearest to himself.
All flesh will march with the like to it-self, & every man will associate himself to his like.
text.KJV: 13:20 As the proud hate humilitie: so doth the rich abhorre the poore.

text.DRB: If the wolf shall at any time communicate with the lamb, so the sinner with the just.
text.KJV: 13:21 A rich man beginning to fall, is held vp of his friends: but a poore man being downe, is thrust also away by his friends.

text.DRB: What fellowship hath an holy man with a dog, or what part hath the rich with the poor?
text.KJV: 13:22 When a rich man is fallen, he hath many helpers: he speaketh things not to be spoken, and yet men iustifie him: the poore man slipt, and yet they rebuked him too: he spake wisely, and could haue no place.

text.DRB: The wild ass in the desert is the lion's prey: so the poor are also the pastures of the rich.
text.KJV: 13:23 When a rich man speaketh, euery man holdeth his tongue, and looke what hee sayeth, they extoll it to the clouds: but if the poore man speake, they say, What fellow is this? and if he stumble, they will helpe to ouerthrowe him.
13:24 Riches are good vnto him that hath no sinne, and pouerty is euill in the mouth of the vngodly.
text.NRSV: Wealth is good if it is free from sin; poverty is evil only in the opinion of the ungodly.
text.DRB: And as humility is abomination to the proud: so also the poor man is the execration of the rich.

text.KJV: 13:25 The heart of a man changeth his countenance, whether it be for good or euill: and a merry heart maketh a cheerefull countenance.
text.NRSV: The heart changes the face, either for good or for evil.
text.DRB: The rich man being moved is confirmed by his friends: but the humble when he is fallen, shall be thrust out even of his familiars.
To the rich deceived there are many recoverers: he hath spoken proud words, and they have justified him.
text.KJV: 13:26 A cheerefull countenance is a token of a heart that is in prosperity, and the finding out of parables, is a wearisome labour of the minde.

text.DRB: The humble was deceived, he moreover is rebuked also: he hath spoken wisely, and place was not given unto him.
The rich man spake, and all held their peace, and they will carry his word even to the clouds.
The poor man spake and they say: Who is this? And if he stumble, they will overthrow him.
Substance is good, to him that hath no sin in his conscience: and poverty is most wicked in the mouth of the impious.
The heart of a man altereth his countenance, either into good, or into evil.
The token of a good heart, and a good countenance thou shalt hardly find, and with labour.

text.KJV: 14:1 Blessed is the man that hath not slipt with his mouth, and is not pricked with the multitude of sinnes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Happy those whose mouth causes them no grief, those who are not stung by remorse for sin.
text.NRSV: Happy are those who do not blunder with their lips, and need not suffer remorse for sin.
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that hath not offended in a word out of his mouth, and is not pricked with the sorrow of sin.

text.KJV: 14:2 Blessed is hee whose conscience hath not condemned him, and who is not fallen from his hope in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Happy are those whose conscience does not reproach them, those who have not lost hope.
text.NRSV: Happy are those whose hearts do not condemn them, and who have not given up their hope.
text.DRB: Happy is he, that hath not had heaviness of his mind, and hath not fallen from his hope.

text.KJV: 14:3 Riches are not comely for a niggard: and what should an enuious man doe with money?
text.NABRE: The Use of Wealth Wealth is not appropriate for the mean-spirited; to misers, what use is gold?
text.NRSV: Riches are not appropriate for a small-minded person; and of what use is wealth to a miser?
text.DRB: Substance is without reason to the covetous man and niggard, and for the spiteful envious man to what purpose is gold?
He that heapeth together from his heart unjustly, gathereth for others, and in his goods another will keep riot.
text.NABRE: What they deny themselves they collect for someone else, and strangers will live sumptuously on their possessions.
text.KJV: 14:4 He that gathereth by defrauding his owne soule, gathereth for others, that shall spend his goods riotously.
14:5 Hee that is euill to himselfe, to whom will he be good? he shall not take pleasure in his goods.
text.NABRE: To whom will they be generous that are stingy with themselves and do not enjoy what is their own?
text.NRSV: He who is evil to himself, to whom will he be good? He will not enjoy his own riches.
text.DRB: He that is wicked to himself, to what other man will he be good? And he shall have no pleasure in his goods.
He that envieth himself, nothing is worse than he, and this is the reward of his malice:
text.NABRE: None are worse than those who are stingy with themselves; they punish their own avarice.
text.KJV: 14:6 There is none worse then he that enuieth himselfe; and this is a recompence of his wickednesse.

text.DRB: and if he do good, he doth it ignorantly, and not willing: and at the last he manifesteth his malice.
text.NABRE: If ever they do good, it is by mistake; in the end they reveal their meanness.
text.KJV: 14:7 And if he doth good, he doth it vnwillingly, and at the last he will declare his wickednesse.

text.DRB: The eye of the envious is wicked, and turneth away his face, and despiseth his own soul.
text.NABRE: Misers are evil people, they turn away and disregard others.
text.KJV: 14:8 The enuious man hath a wicked eye, he turneth away his face and despiseth men.

text.DRB: The eye of the covetous man insatiable in a portion of iniquity, will not be satisfied till he consume his own soul withering it.
text.NABRE: The greedy see their share as not enough; greedy injustice dries up the soul.
text.KJV: 14:9 A couetous mans eye is not satisfied with his portion, and the iniquity of the wicked dryeth vp his soule.

text.DRB: An evil eye is towards evil things: & he shall have his fill of bread, needy & in heaviness shall he be at his table.
text.NABRE: The eye of the miserly is rapacious for food, but there is none of it on their own table.
text.KJV: 14:10 A wicked eye enuieth  bread, and he is a niggard at his table.
14:11 My sonne, according to thy habilitie doe good to thy selfe, and giue the Lord his due offering.
text.NABRE: My son, if you have the means, treat yourself well, and enjoy life as best you can.
text.NRSV: My child, if you have anything, do good to yourself, and bring worthy offerings to the Lord.
text.DRB: Son if thou have it, do good to thyself, and offer to God worthy oblations.

text.KJV: 14:12 Remember that death will not be long in comming, and that the couenant of the graue is not shewed vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Remember that death does not delay, and you have not been told the grave’s appointed time.
text.NRSV: Remember that death does not tarry, and the decree of Hades has not been shown to you.
text.DRB: Be mindful that death slacked not, and that the covenant of hell hath been shewed thee: for the covenant of this world shall die the death.

text.KJV: 14:13 Doe good vnto thy friend before thou die, and according to thy abilitie, stretch out thy hand and giue to him.
text.NABRE: Before you die, be good to your friends; give them a share in what you possess.
text.NRSV: Do good to friends before you die, and reach out and give to them as much as you can.
text.DRB: Before death do good to thy friend, and according to thine ability stretching out thy hand, give to the poor.
Be not defrauded of thy good day, and let not a little portion of a good gift over-pass thee.
text.NABRE: Do not deprive yourself of good things now or let a choice portion escape you.
text.KJV: 14:14 Defraud not thy selfe of the good day, and let not the part of a good desire ouerpasse thee.

text.DRB: Shalt thou not leave to others thy sorrows, & labours in the division of the lot?
text.NABRE: Will you not leave your riches to others, and your earnings to be divided by lot?
text.KJV: 14:15 Shalt thou not leaue thy trauailes vnto another? and thy labours to be diuided by lot?

text.DRB: Give and take, and justify thy soul.
text.NABRE: Give and take, treat yourself well, for in Sheol there are no joys to seek.
text.KJV: 14:16 Giue, and take, and sanctifie thy soule, for there is no seeking of dainties in the graue.
14:17 All flesh waxeth old as a garment: for the couenant from the beginning is; thou shalt die the death.
text.NABRE: All flesh grows old like a garment; the age-old law is: everyone must die.
text.NRSV: All living beings grow old like a garment; for the decree from of old is, “You must surely die!”
text.DRB: Before thy death work justice: for in hell there cannot meat be found.

text.KJV: 14:18 As of the greene leaues on a thicke tree, some fall, and some grow; so is the generation of flesh and blood, one commeth to an end, and another is borne.
text.NABRE: As with the leaves growing on a luxuriant tree— one falls off and another sprouts— So with the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another flourishes.
text.NRSV: Like abundant leaves on a spreading tree that sheds some and puts forth others, so are the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another is born.
text.DRB: All flesh shall wax old as grass, and as the leaf fructifying on a green tree.
Some grow, and some are shaken off: so the generation of flesh and blood, one is ended, and another is born.
text.NABRE: All human deeds surely perish; the works they do follow after them.
text.KJV: 14:19 Euery worke rotteth and consumeth away, and the worker therof shal goe withall.
14:20 Blessed is the man that doeth meditate good things in wisdome, and that reasoneth of holy things by his vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: The Search for Wisdom and Her Blessings Happy those who meditate on Wisdom, and fix their gaze on knowledge;
text.NRSV: Happy is the person who meditates on wisdom and reasons with intelligent understanding,
text.DRB: All corruptible work shall fail in the end: and he that worketh it shall go therewith.
And every excellent work shall be justified: and he that worketh it, shall be honoured therein.
text.NABRE: Who ponder her ways in their heart, and understand her paths;
text.KJV: 14:21 He that considereth her wayes in his heart, shall also haue vnderstanding in her secrets.
14:22 Goe after her as one that traceth, and lie in wait in her wayes.
text.NABRE: Who pursue her like a scout, and watch at her entry way;
text.NRSV: who pursues her like a hunter, and lies in wait on her paths;
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that shall continue in wisdom, and that shall meditate in his justice, and in understanding shall consider the providence of God.
He that considereth her ways in his heart, and hath understanding in her secrets, going after her as a searcher, and consisting in her ways:
text.NABRE: Who peep through her windows, and listen at her doors;
text.KJV: 14:23 Hee that prieth in at her windowes, shal also hearken at her doores.

text.DRB: He that looketh through her windows, and heareth in her gates:
text.NABRE: Who encamp near her house and fasten their tent pegs next to her walls;
text.KJV: 14:24 Hee that doeth lodge neere her house, shall also fasten a pin in her walles.

text.DRB: He that resteth by her house, & in her walls fastening a stake will set up his cottage beside her hands: and good things shall rest in his cottage for ever.
text.NABRE: Who pitch their tent beside her, and dwell in a good place;
text.KJV: 14:25 He shall pitch his tent nigh vnto her, and shall lodge in a lodging where good things are.
14:26 He shal set his children vnder her shelter, and shall lodge vnder her branches.
text.NABRE: Who build their nest in her leaves, and lodge in her branches;
text.NRSV: he will place his children under her shelter, and will dwell under her branches;
text.DRB: He shall set his children under her covering, and shall abide under her boughs:

text.KJV: 14:27 By her he shall be couered from heat, and in her glory shall he dwell.
text.NABRE: Who take refuge from the heat in her shade and dwell in her home.
text.NRSV: he will be sheltered by her from the heat, and will dwell in the midst of her glory.
text.DRB: he shall be protected under her covering from the heat, and shall rest in her glory.

text.KJV: 15:1 He that feareth the Lord will doe good, and he that hath the knowledge of the Law shal obtaine her.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Whoever fears the Lord will do this; whoever is practiced in the Law will come to Wisdom.
text.NRSV: Whoever fears the Lord will do this, and whoever holds to the law will obtain wisdom.
text.DRB: He that feareth God, shall do good things, and he that hath justice shall apprehend her,

text.KJV: 15:2 And as a mother shall she meet him, and receiue him as a wife maried of a virgin.
text.NABRE: She will meet him like a mother; like a young bride she will receive him,
text.NRSV: She will come to meet him like a mother, and like a young bride she will welcome him.
text.DRB: and she will meet him as an honourable mother, & a wife from virginity she will receive him.

text.KJV: 15:3 With the bread of vnderstanding shall she feed him, and giue him the water of wisedome to drinke.
text.NABRE: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of understanding to drink.
text.NRSV: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.
text.DRB: She shall feed him with the bread of life and understanding, and give him the water of wholesome wisdom to drink: and she shall be made sure in him, and he shall not be bowed:
and she shall hold him fast, and he shall not be confounded: and she shall exalt him before his neighbours,
text.NABRE: He will lean upon her and not fall; he will trust in her and not be put to shame.
text.KJV: 15:4 Hee shall be stayed vpon her, and shall not be moued, and shall rely vpon her, and shall not be confounded.

text.DRB: and in the midst of the Church she shall open his mouth, and shall fill him with the spirit of wisdom and understanding, and shall clothe him with a stole of glory.
text.NABRE: She will exalt him above his neighbors, and in the assembly she will make him eloquent.
text.KJV: 15:5 Shee shall exalt him aboue his neighbours, and in the midst of the congregation shall she open his mouth.
15:6 He shall finde ioy, and a crowne of gladnesse, and she shall cause him to inherit an euerlasting name.
text.NABRE: Joy and gladness he will find, and an everlasting name he will inherit.
text.NRSV: He will find gladness and a crown of rejoicing, and will acquire an everlasting name.
text.DRB: Joy & exultation she shall heap upon him, & shall make him inherit an everlasting name.
Foolish men shall not apprehend her, & wise men shall meet her, foolish men shall not see her: for she is far from pride and deceit.
text.NABRE: The worthless will not attain her, and the haughty will not behold her.
text.KJV: 15:7 But foolish men shall not attaine vnto her, and sinners shall not see her.

text.DRB: Lying men shall not be mindful of her: and true men shall be found in her, and shall have success, even to the beholding of God.
text.NABRE: She is far from the impious; liars never think of her.
text.KJV: 15:8 For she is farre from pride, and men that are liers cannot remember her.

text.DRB: Praise is not comely in the mouth of a sinner:
text.NABRE: Praise is unseemly on the lips of sinners, for it has not been allotted to them by God.
text.KJV: 15:9 Praise is not seemly in the mouth of a sinner, for it was not sent him of the Lord:

text.DRB: because wisdom proceeded from God. For praise shall be with the wisdom of God, & shall abound in a faithful mouth, & the dominatour will give praise to it.
text.NABRE: But praise is uttered by the mouth of the wise, and its rightful owner teaches it.
text.KJV: 15:10 For praise shalbe vttered in wisdome, and the Lord wil prosper it.
15:11 Say not thou, It is through the Lord, that I fell away, for thou oughtest not to doe the things that he hateth.
text.NABRE: Free Will Do not say: “It was God’s doing that I fell away,” for what he hates he does not do.
text.NRSV: Do not say, “It was the Lord’s doing that I fell away”; for he does not do what he hates.
text.DRB: Say not: It is by God, that she is absent: for do not thou the things that he hateth.

text.KJV: 15:12 Say not thou, He hath caused mee to erre, for hee hath no need of the sinfull man.
text.NABRE: Do not say: “He himself has led me astray,” for he has no need of the wicked.
text.NRSV: Do not say, “It was he who led me astray”; for he has no need of the sinful.
text.DRB: Say not: He hath made me err: for impious men are not necessary for him.

text.KJV: 15:13 The Lord hateth all abomination, and they that feare God loue it not.
text.NABRE: Abominable wickedness the Lord hates and he does not let it happen to those who fear him.
text.NRSV: The Lord hates all abominations; such things are not loved by those who fear him.
text.DRB: Our Lord hateth all abomination of error, and it shall not be amiable to them, that fear him.

text.KJV: 15:14 Hee himselfe made man from the beginning, and left him in the hand of his counsell,
text.NABRE: God in the beginning created human beings and made them subject to their own free choice.
text.NRSV: It was he who created humankind in the beginning, and he left them in the power of their own free choice.
text.DRB: God from the beginning made man, and left him in the hand of his own counsel.

text.KJV: 15:15 If thou wilt, to keepe the Commandements, and to performe acceptable faithfulnesse.
text.NABRE: If you choose, you can keep the commandments; loyalty is doing the will of God.
text.NRSV: If you choose, you can keep the commandments, and to act faithfully is a matter of your own choice.
text.DRB: He added his commandments and precepts.

text.KJV: 15:16 He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thy hand vnto whether thou wilt.
text.NABRE: Set before you are fire and water; to whatever you choose, stretch out your hand.
text.NRSV: He has placed before you fire and water; stretch out your hand for whichever you choose.
text.DRB: If thou will keep the commandment, and keep acceptable fidelity for ever, they shall preserve thee.

text.KJV: 15:17 Before man is life and death, and whether him liketh shalbe giuen him.
text.NABRE: Before everyone are life and death, whichever they choose will be given them.
text.NRSV: Before each person are life and death, and whichever one chooses will be given.
text.DRB: He hath set before thee water and fire: to which thou wilt, stretch forth thine hand.

text.KJV: 15:18 For the wisedome of the Lord is great, and he is mighty in power, and beholdeth all things,
text.NABRE: Immense is the wisdom of the Lord; mighty in power, he sees all things.
text.NRSV: For great is the wisdom of the Lord; he is mighty in power and sees everything;
text.DRB: Before man there is life and death, good and evil: what pleaseth him shall be given him:

text.KJV: 15:19 And his eyes are vpon them that feare him, & hee knoweth euery worke of man.
text.NABRE: The eyes of God behold his works, and he understands every human deed.
text.NRSV: his eyes are on those who fear him, and he knows every human action.
text.DRB: Because the wisdom of God is much, and he is strong in might, seeing all men without intermission.

text.KJV: 15:20 Hee hath commanded no man to do wickedly, neither hath he giuen any man license to sinne.
text.NABRE: He never commands anyone to sin, nor shows leniency toward deceivers.
text.NRSV: He has not commanded anyone to be wicked, and he has not given anyone permission to sin.
text.DRB: The eyes of our Lord are towards them that fear him, and he knoweth all the work of man.
He hath commanded no man to do impiously, and he hath given no man space to sin:
for he desireth not a multitude of faithless and unprofitable children.

text.KJV: 16:1 Desire not a multitude of vnprofitable children, neither delight in vngodly sonnes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 God’s Punishment of Sinners Do not yearn for worthless children, or rejoice in wicked offspring.
text.NRSV: Do not desire a multitude of worthless children, and do not rejoice in ungodly offspring.
text.DRB: Rejoice not in impious children, if they be multiplied: neither be delighted upon them, if the fear of God be not in them.

text.KJV: 16:2 Though they multiply, reioyce not in them, except the feare of the Lord be with them.
text.NABRE: Even if they be many, do not rejoice in them if they do not have fear of the Lord.
text.NRSV: If they multiply, do not rejoice in them, unless the fear of the Lord is in them.
text.DRB: Credit not their life, and respect not their labours.
For better is one fearing God, than a thousand impious children.
text.NABRE: Do not count on long life for them, or have any hope for their future. For one can be better than a thousand; rather die childless than have impious children!
text.KJV: 16:3 Trust not thou in their life, neither respect their multitude: for one that is iust, is better then a thousand, and better it is to die without children, then to haue them that are vngodly.

text.DRB: And it is more profitable to die without children, rather than to leave impious children.
text.NABRE: Through one wise person a city can be peopled; but through a clan of rebels it becomes desolate.
text.KJV: 16:4 For by one that hath vnderstanding, shall the city be replenished, but the kindred of the wicked, shall speedily become desolate.

text.DRB: By one wise a country shall be inhabited, and the tribe of the impious shall be made desolate.
text.NABRE: Many such things my eye has seen, and even more than these my ear has heard.
text.KJV: 16:5 Many such things haue I seene with mine eyes, and mine eare hath heard greater things then these.
16:6 In the congregation of the vngodly, shall a fire be kindled, and in a rebellious nation, wrath is set on fire.
text.NABRE: Against a sinful band fire is kindled, upon a godless people wrath blazes.
text.NRSV: In a gathering of sinners a fire is kindled, and in a disobedient nation wrath blazes up.
text.DRB: Many such things hath mine eye seen, and mine ear hath heard things of more force than these.

text.KJV: 16:7 Hee was not pacified towards the olde giants, who fell away in the strength of their foolishnesse.
text.NABRE: He did not forgive the princes of old who rebelled long ago in their might.
text.NRSV: He did not forgive the ancient giants who revolted in their might.
text.DRB: In the synagogue of sinners a fire shall flame, and in an incredulous nation wrath shall wax hot.
The old giants did not obtain for their sins, who were destroyed trusting to their own strength:
text.NABRE: He did not spare the neighbors of Lot, abominable in their pride.
text.KJV: 16:8 Neither spared he the place where Lot soiourned, but abhorred them for their pride.

text.DRB: and he spared not the peregrination of Lot, and he detested them for the pride of their word.
text.NABRE: He did not spare the doomed people, dispossessed because of their sin;
text.KJV: 16:9 Hee pitied not the people of perdition, who were taken away in their sinnes.

text.DRB: He had not pity on them, destroying the whole nation, and extolling themselves in their sins.
text.NABRE: Nor the six hundred thousand foot soldiers, sent to their graves for the arrogance of their hearts.
text.KJV: 16:10 Nor the sixe hundreth thousand footmen, who were gathered together in the hardnesse of their hearts.
16:11 And if there be one stiffe-necked among the people, it is marueile, if he escape vnpunished; for mercy and wrath are with him, hee is mighty to forgiue, and to powre out displeasure.
text.NABRE: Had there been but one stiff-necked person, it would be a wonder had he gone unpunished. For mercy and anger alike are with him; he remits and forgives, but also pours out wrath.
text.NRSV: And if there is even one stubborn person, it is a wonder if he could go unpunished. For mercy and wrath are with the Lord; he is mighty to forgive, and he pours out wrath.
text.DRB: And as the six hundred thousand foot men, which were gathered together in the hardness of their heart: and if one had been stiff necked, it is marvel if he had been unpunished.

text.KJV: 16:12 As his mercy is great, so is his correction also: he iudgeth a man according to his workes.
text.NABRE: Great as his mercy is his punishment; he judges people, each according to their deeds.
text.NRSV: As great as is his mercy, so also is his chastisement; he judges a person according to one’s deeds.
text.DRB: For there is mercy and wrath with him. Mighty exoration, & pouring out wrath:
according to his mercy, so his chastisement judgeth a man according to his works.
text.NABRE: Criminals do not escape with their plunder; the hope of the righteous, God never leaves unfulfilled.
text.KJV: 16:13 The sinner shall not escape with his spoiles, and the patience of the godly shall not be frustrate.

text.DRB: The sinner shall not escape in robbery, and the sufferance of him that doth mercy shall not slack.
text.NABRE: Whoever does good has a reward; each receives according to their deeds.
text.KJV: 16:14 Make way for euery worke of mercy: for euery man shall finde according to his workes.

text.DRB: All mercy shall make a place to every man according to the merit of his works, & according to the understanding of his peregrination.
text.KJV: 16:15 The Lord hardened Pharaoh, that hee should not know him, that his powerfull workes might be knowen to the world.

text.DRB: Say not: I shall be hid from God, and from on high who shall remember me?
text.KJV: 16:16 His mercy is manifest to euery creature, and hee hath separated his light from the darkenesse with an Adamant.

16:17 Say not thou, I will hide my selfe from the Lord: shall any remember me from aboue? I shall not be remembred among so many people: for what is my soule among such an infinite number of creatures?
text.NRSV: Do not say, “I am hidden from the Lord, and who from on high has me in mind? Among so many people I am unknown, for what am I in a boundless creation?”
text.DRB: In a great people I shall not be known: for what is my soul among so innumerable creatures?

text.KJV: 16:18 Behold, the heauen, and the heauen of heauens, the deepe and the earth, and all that therein is, shall be mooued when he shall visit.
text.NRSV: Behold, heaven and the highest heaven, the abyss and the earth, tremble at his visitation!
text.DRB: Behold heaven, and the heavens of heavens, the depth, and all the earth, & the things that are in them, in his sight shall be moved,
the mountains together, and the little hills, & the foundations of the earth: & when God shall behold them, they shall be shaken with trembling.
text.KJV: 16:19 The mountaines also, and foundations of the earth shall bee shaken with trembling, when the Lord looketh vpon them.

text.DRB: And in all these things the heart is senseless: and every heart is understood of him:
text.KJV: 16:20 No heart can thinke vpon these things worthily: and who is able to conceiue his wayes?

text.DRB: and his ways who doth understand, and the storm, which neither the eye of man shall see?
text.KJV: 16:21 It is a tempest, which no man can see: for the most part of his workes are hidde.

text.DRB: For many of his works are in secrets: but the works of his justice who shall declare? Or who shall sustain? For the testament is far from some, and the examination of all is in consummation.
text.KJV: 16:22 Who can declare the workes of his iustice? or who can endure them? for his Couenant is afarre off, and the triall of all things is in the ende.

text.DRB: He that is less of heart, thinketh vain things: and the unwise, and erring man, thinketh foolish things.
text.KJV: 16:23 He that wanteth vnderstanding, will thinke vpon vaine things: and a foolish man erring, imagineth follies.

text.DRB: Hear me my son, and learn the discipline of understanding, and attend to my words in thy heart,
text.KJV: 16:24 My sonne, hearken vnto mee, and learne knowledge, and marke my words with thy heart.

text.DRB: and I will speak discipline in equity, and will search to declare wisdom: and to my words attend in thy heart, and I speak in equity of spirit the virtues, that God hath put upon his works from the beginning, and in truth I shew forth his knowledge.
text.KJV: 16:25 I will shewe foorth doctrine in weight, and declare his knowledge exactly.
16:26 The works of the Lord are done in iudgement from the beginning: and from the time he made them, hee disposed the parts thereof.
text.NRSV: When the Lord created his works from the beginning, and, in making them, determined their boundaries,
text.DRB: In the judgement of God are his works from the beginning, and from the institution of them he distinguished their parts, and their beginnings in their nations.

text.KJV: 16:27 Hee garnished his workes for euer, and in his hand are the chiefe of them vnto all generations: they neither labour, nor are weary, nor cease from their workes.
text.NRSV: he arranged his works in an eternal order, and their dominion for all generations of them. They neither hunger nor grow weary, and they do not abandon their tasks.
text.DRB: He adorned their works for ever, neither have they hungered, nor laboured, and they have not ceased from their works.
Every one shall not vex his neighbour for ever.
text.KJV: 16:28 None of them hindreth another, and they shall neuer disobey his word.

text.DRB: Be not incredulous to his word.
text.KJV: 16:29 After this, the Lord looked vpon the earth, and filled it with his blessings.

text.DRB: After this God looked upon the earth, & filled it with his good things.
text.KJV: 16:30 With all maner of liuing things hath hee couered the face thereof, and they shall returne into it againe.

text.DRB: And the soul of every living thing shall shew before the face thereof, and into it again is their return.

text.KJV: 17:1 The Lord created man of the earth, and turned him into it againe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Creation of Human Beings The Lord created human beings from the earth, and makes them return to earth again.
text.NRSV: The Lord created human beings out of earth, and makes them return to it again.
text.DRB: God created man of the earth, and after his own image he made him.
And again he turned him into it, and conformable to himself clothed him with strength.
text.NABRE: A limited number of days he gave them, but granted them authority over everything on earth.
text.KJV: 17:2 He gaue them few dayes, and a short time, and power also ouer the things therein.
17:3 He endued them with strength by themselues, and made them according to his image,
text.NABRE: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his image.
text.NRSV: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his own image.
text.DRB: He gave him a number of days and time, and gave him power of those things that are upon the earth.
He put his fear over all flesh, and he had dominion of beasts and fowls.
text.NABRE: He put fear of them in all flesh, and gave them dominion over beasts and birds.
text.KJV: 17:4 And put the feare of man vpon all flesh, and gaue him dominion ouer beasts and foules.

text.DRB: He created of him an helper like to himself: he gave them counsel, and tongue, and eyes, & ears, and heart to devise: and he filled them with the discipline of understanding.
He created in them the knowledge of the spirit, he filled their heart with understanding, and evil and good he shewed them.
text.KJV: 17:6 Counsell, and a tongue, and eyes, eares, and a heart, gaue he them to vnderstand.
17:7 Withall, hee filled them with the knowledge of vnderstanding, & shewed them good and euill.
text.NRSV: He filled them with knowledge and understanding, and showed them good and evil.
text.DRB: He set his eye upon their hearts to shew them the great things of his works:
that they might praise the name of sanctification: & glory in his marvelous works, that they might declare the glorious things of his works.
text.KJV: 17:8 Hee set his eye vpon their hearts, that he might shew them the greatnesse of his workes.

text.DRB: He added discipline unto them, and made them inherit the law of life.
text.KJV: 17:9 He gaue them to glory in his marueilous actes for euer, that they might declare his works with vnderstanding.

text.DRB: He made an everlasting testament with them, & he shewed them justice and his judgements.
text.KJV: 17:10 And the elect shall praise his holy Name.
17:11 Beside this he gaue them knowledge, and the law of life for an heritage.
text.NRSV: He bestowed upon them knowledge, and gave them the law of life as a heritage.
text.DRB: And their eye saw the glorious things of his honour, & their ears heard the honour of his voice, and he said to them: Beware of every unjust thing.
