text.KJV: 17:12 He made an euerlasting couenant with them, and shewed them his iudgements.
text.NRSV: He established with them an eternal covenant, and revealed to them his decrees.
text.DRB: And he gave them commandment every one concerning his neighbour.
Their ways are before him always, they are not hid from his eyes.
text.KJV: 17:13 Their eyes saw the maiestie of his glory, and their eares heard his glorious voyce.

text.DRB: Over every nation he appointed a ruler.
text.KJV: 17:14 And he said vnto them, Beware of all vnrighteousnes, and he gaue euery man commandement concerning his neighbour,
17:15 Their wayes are euer before him, and shall not be hid from his eyes.
text.NRSV: Their ways are always before him; they will not be hidden from his eyes.
text.DRB: And Israel was made the manifest portion of God.
And all their works as the sun in the sight of God: and his eyes without intermission looking on their ways.
text.KJV: 17:16 Euery man from his youth is giuen to euil, neither could they make to themselues fleshie hearts for stonie.

text.DRB: The testaments were not hid by their iniquity, and all their iniquities are in the sight of God.
text.KJV: 17:17 For in the diuision of the nations of the whole earth, he set a ruler ouer euery people, but Israel is the Lords portion.

text.DRB: The alms of a man is as a seal with him, and shall preserve the grace of a man as the apple of the eye:
text.KJV: 17:18 Whom being his first borne, hee nourisheth with discipline, and giuing him the light of his loue, doth not forsake him.
17:19 Therefore all their workes are as the Sunne before him, and his eyes are continually vpon their wayes.
text.NRSV: All their works are as the sun before him, and his eyes are continually upon their ways.
text.DRB: And afterward he shall arise, and shall render them reward, to every one upon their head, and shall turn into the inner parts of the earth.
But to the penitent he hath given the way of justice, and he hath confirmed them that fail to sustain, and hath appointed to them the lot of truth.
text.KJV: 17:20 None of their vnrighteous deeds are hid from him, but all their sinnes are before the Lord:

text.DRB: Turn to our Lord, and forsake thy sins:
text.KJV: 17:21 But the Lord being gracious, and knowing his workemanship, neither left nor forsooke them, but spared them.

text.DRB: pray before the face of our Lord, and diminish offences.
text.KJV: 17:22 The almes of a man is as a signet with him, and he will keep the good deedes of man, as the apple of the eye, and giue repentance to his sonnes and daughters.

text.DRB: Return to our Lord, & turn away from thine injustice, and hate exceedingly abomination:
text.KJV: 17:23 Afterward he will rise vp and reward them, and render their recompense vpon their heads.
17:24 But vnto them that repent, he granted them returne, and comforted those that faile in patience.
text.NRSV: But to those who repent he grants a return, and he encourages those who are losing their hope.
text.DRB: and know the justices and judgments of God, and stand in the lot of thy purpose, and of prayer of the most high God.

text.KJV: 17:25 Returne vnto the Lord, and forsake thy sinnes, make thy prayer before his face, and offend lesse.
text.NRSV: Turn to the Lord and forsake your sins; pray in his presence and lessen your offense.
text.DRB: Go into the parts of the holy world, with the living, and them that give praise to God.

text.KJV: 17:26 Turne againe to the most High, and turne away from iniquitie: for he will leade thee out of darkenesse into the light of health, and hate thou abomination vehemently.
text.NRSV: Return to the Most High and turn away from iniquity, and hate intensely what he abhors.
text.DRB: Tarry not in the error of the impious, before death confess. From the dead as nothing, confession perisheth.
Thou shalt confess living, alive and in health thou shalt confess, and shalt praise God, and shalt glory in his mercies.
text.KJV: 17:27 Who shall praise the most High in the graue, in stead of them which liue and giue thanks?
17:28 Thankesgiuing perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not: the liuing and sound in heart, shall praise the Lord.
text.NRSV: How great is the mercy of the Lord, and his forgiveness for those who return to him!
text.DRB: How great is the mercy of our Lord, and his propitiation to them that turn to him!
For all things cannot be in men, because the son of man is not immortal, and they have delighted in the vanity of malice.
text.KJV: 17:29 How great is the louing kindnes of the Lord our God, and his compassion vnto such as turne vnto him in holinesse?

text.DRB: What is brighter than the sun? & it shall fail. Or what more wicked than that which flesh and blood hath invented? And this shall be reproved.
text.KJV: 17:30 For all things cannot bee in men, because þ sonne of man is not immortal.

text.DRB: He beholdeth the power of the height of heaven: and all men be earth and ashes.
text.KJV: 17:31 What is brighter then the Sun? yet the light thereof faileth: and flesh and blood will imagine euill.
18:1 Hee that liueth for euer, created all things in generall.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 The Divine Power and Mercy He who lives forever created the whole universe;
text.NRSV: He who lives forever created the whole universe.
text.DRB: He that liveth for ever, created all things together. God only shall be justified, and remaineth an invincible King for ever.
Who is sufficient to declare his works?
text.NABRE: the Lord alone is just.
text.KJV: 18:2 The Lord onely is righteous, and there is none other but he.

text.DRB: For who shall search out his glorious things?
text.KJV: 18:3 Who gouerneth the world with the palme of his hand, and all things obey his will, for he is the king of all, by his power diuiding holy things among them from prophane.

text.DRB: and who shall shew forth the power of his greatness? Or who shall add to declare his mercy?
text.KJV: 18:4 To whom hath he giuen power to declare his works? and who shall finde out his noble actes?

text.DRB: It is not possible to diminish, nor add, neither is it possible to find the glorious works of God:
text.KJV: 18:5 Who shall number the strength of his maiestie? and who shall also tel out his mercies?

text.DRB: When a man, shall have done, then shall he begin: and when he shall rest, he shall work.
text.KJV: 18:6 As for the wonderous workes of the Lord, there may nothing bee taken from them, neither may any thing bee put vnto them, neither can the ground of them be found out.

text.DRB: What is man: and what is his grace? And what is his good, or what his evil?
text.KJV: 18:7 When a man hath done, then he beginneth, and when hee leaueth off, then he shall be doubtfull.
18:8 What is man, and whereto serueth he? what is his good, & what is his euil?
text.NRSV: What are mortals, and of what use are they? What is their good and what is their evil?
text.DRB: The number of the days of men at the most an hundred years: as drops of the water of the sea they are reputed: and as the gravel stone of the sand, so a few years in the day of eternity.

text.KJV: 18:9 The number of a mans dayes at the most are an hundred yeeres.
text.NRSV: The number of their days is great if they reach a hundred years.
text.DRB: For this cause God is patient toward them, and poureth out his mercy upon them.

text.KJV: 18:10 As a drop of water vnto the Sea, and a grauell stone in comparison of the sand, so are a thousand yeeres to the dayes of eternitie.
text.NRSV: Like a drop of water from the sea and a grain of sand, so are a few years among the days of eternity.
text.DRB: He hath seen the presumption of their heart that it is naught, and hath known their subversion that it is evil.

text.KJV: 18:11 Therfore is God patient with them, & powreth forth his mercy vpon them.
text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord is patient with them and pours out his mercy upon them.
text.DRB: Therefore hath he fulfilled his propitiation toward them, and hath shewed them the way of equity.
Man's compassion is touching his neighbour: but the mercy of God is upon all flesh.
text.KJV: 18:12 He saw and perceiued their end to be euill, therefore he multiplied his compassion.
18:13 The mercy of man is toward his neighbour, but the mercy of the Lord is vpon all flesh: he reprooueth and nurtureth, and teacheth, & bringeth againe as a shepheard his flocke.
text.NRSV: The compassion of human beings is for their neighbors, but the compassion of the Lord is for every living thing.
text.DRB: He that hath mercy, teacheth and instructeth, as a pastor his flock.
He hath mercy on him that receiveth the doctrine of compassion, and he that hasteneth in his judgements.
text.KJV: 18:14 He hath mercy on them that receiue discipline, and that diligently seeke after his iudgements.
18:15 My sonne, blemish not thy good deeds, neither vse vncomfortable words when thou giuest any thing.
text.NRSV: My child, do not mix reproach with your good deeds, or bitter words with any gift.
text.DRB: Son in good deeds give no blame, & in every gift give not the sadness of an evil word.
Shall not the dew cool heat? So also a word better than a gift.
text.KJV: 18:16 Shall not the deaw asswage the heate? so is a word better then a gift.

text.DRB: Is not a word above a good gift? But both are with a justified man.
text.KJV: 18:17 Loe is not a word better then a gift? but both are with a gracious man.

text.DRB: Afool will upbraid bitterly: and the gift of one untaught maketh the eyes to dry away.
text.KJV: 18:18 A foole will vpbraide churlishly, and a gift of the enuious consumeth the eyes.
18:19 Learne before thou speake, and vse phisicke, or euer thou be sicke.
text.NRSV: Before you speak, learn; and before you fall sick, take care of yourself.
text.DRB: Before judgment prepare thee justice, and before thou speak learn.

text.KJV: 18:20 Before iudgement examine thy selfe, and in the day of visitation thou shalt find mercy.
text.NRSV: Before judgment comes, examine yourself; and at the time of scrutiny you will find forgiveness.
text.DRB: Before sickness take medicine, and before judgement examine thyself, and in the sight of God thou shalt find propitiation.
Before sickness humble thyself, and in time of infirmity shew thy conversation.
text.KJV: 18:21 Humble thy selfe before thou be sicke, and in the time of sinnes shew repentance.

text.DRB: Be not hindered to pray always, & fear not to be justified even to death, because the reward of God abideth for ever.
text.KJV: 18:22 Let nothing hinder thee to pay thy vowe in due time, and deferre not vntill death to be iustified.

text.DRB: Before prayer prepare thy soul: and be not as a man that tempteth God.
text.KJV: 18:23 Before thou prayest, prepare thy selfe, and be not as one that tempteth the Lord.

text.DRB: Remember the wrath in the day of consummation, and the time of reward in conversation of the face.
text.KJV: 18:24 Thinke vpon the wrath that shall be at the end; and the time of vengeance when he shall turne away his face.

text.DRB: Remember poverty in the time of aboundance, & the necessities of poverty in the day of riches.
text.KJV: 18:25 When thou hast enough remember the time of hunger, and when thou art rich thinke vpon pouerty and need.

text.DRB: From morning unto evening time shall be changed, & all these are sown in the eyes of God.
text.KJV: 18:26 From the morning vntill the euening the time is changed, and all things are soone done before the Lord.

text.DRB: Awise man in all things will fear, and in the days of offences will beware of sloth.
text.KJV: 18:27 A wise man will feare in euery thing, and in the day of sinning he will beware of offence: but a foole will not obserue time.

text.DRB: Every subtile man knoweth wisdom, and to him that findeth her he will give praise.
text.KJV: 18:28 Euery man of vnderstanding knoweth wisedome, and wil giue praise vnto him that found her.

text.DRB: The wise in words, and they also have done wisely: and have understood truth and justice, and have rained proverbs & judgments.
text.KJV: 18:29 They that were of vnderstanding in sayings, became also wise themselues, and powred forth exquisite parables.
18:30 Goe not after thy lustes, but refraine thy selfe from thine appetites.
text.NRSV: Do not follow your base desires, but restrain your appetites.
text.DRB: Go not after thy concupiscences, and turn away from thy will.
If thou give to thy soul her concupiscences, she will make thee a joy to thy enemies.
text.KJV: 18:31 If thou giuest thy soule the desires that please her, she will make thee a laughing stocke to thine enemies, that maligne thee.

text.DRB: Be not delighted in multitudes, nor in few: for their concertation is continual.
text.KJV: 18:32 Take not pleasure in much good cheere, neither be tyed to the expence thereof.

text.DRB: Be not poor in contention of borrowing, and thou hast nothing in thy purse: for thou shalt be envious to thine own life.
text.KJV: 18:33 Be not made a begger by banquetting vpon borrowing, when thou hast nothing in thy purse, for thou shalt lie in waite for thy owne life: and be talked on.
19:1 A labouring man that is giuen to drunkennesse shal not be rich, and hee that contemneth small things shall fall by little & little.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Whoever does this grows no richer; those who waste the little they have will be stripped bare.
text.NRSV: A worker who is a drunkard will never become rich; one who despises small things will fail little by little.
text.DRB: Aworkman that is a drunkard shall not be rich: and he that contemneth small things, shall fall by little and little.

text.KJV: 19:2 Wine and women will make men of vnderstanding to fall away, and he that cleaueth to harlots will become impudent.
text.NABRE: Wine and women make the heart lustful, and the companion of prostitutes becomes reckless.
text.NRSV: Wine and women lead intelligent men astray, and the man who consorts with prostitutes is reckless.
text.DRB: Wine and women make wisemen to apostatate, and shall reprove the prudent:
and he that joineth himself to harlots, shall be naught. Rottenness and worms shall inherit him, and he shall be lifted up for a greater example, and his life shall be taken out of the number.
text.NABRE: Rottenness and worms will possess him, and the reckless will be snatched away.
text.KJV: 19:3 Mothes and wormes shall haue him to heritage, and a bold man shall be taken away.
19:4 He that is hasty to giue credit is light minded, and he that sinneth shall offend against his owne soule.
text.NABRE: Whoever trusts others too quickly has a shallow mind, and those who sin wrong themselves.
text.NRSV: One who trusts others too quickly is light-minded, and one who sins does wrong to himself.
text.DRB: He that giveth credit quickly, is light of heart, and shall be lessened: and he shall moreover be counted one that sinneth against his own soul.
He that rejoiceth in iniquity, shall be reprehended, and he that hateth chastisement shall be diminished of life: and he that hateth babbling, extinguisheth malice.
text.NABRE: The Proper Use of Speech Whoever gloats over evil will be destroyed,
text.KJV: 19:5 Who so taketh pleasure in wickednesse shall be condemned, but he that resisteth pleasures, crowneth his life.

text.DRB: He that sinneth against his own soul, shall repent: and he that is delighted in naughtiness, shall be reprehended.
text.NABRE: and whoever repeats gossip has no sense.
text.KJV: 19:6 He that can rule his tongue shall liue without strife, and he that hateth babbling, shall haue lesse euill.

text.DRB: Iterate not a wicked and hard word, and thou shalt not be lessened.
text.NABRE: Never repeat gossip, and no one will reproach you.
text.KJV: 19:7 Rehearse not vnto another that which is told vnto thee, and thou shalt fare neuer the worse.

text.DRB: To friend and foe tell not thy mind: and if thou have sin disclose it not.
text.NABRE: Tell nothing to friend or foe; and unless it be a sin for you, do not reveal a thing.
text.KJV: 19:8 Whether it be to friend or foe, talk not of other mens liues, and if thou canst without offence reueale them not.

text.DRB: For he will hear thee, and will watch thee, and as it were defending the sin he will hate thee, and so will he be present with thee always.
text.NABRE: For someone may have heard you and watched you, and in time come to hate you.
text.KJV: 19:9 For he heard and obserued thee, and when time commeth he will hate thee.

text.DRB: Hast thou heard a word against thy neighbour? Let it die together in thee, trusting that it will not burst thee.
text.NABRE: Let anything you hear die with you; never fear, it will not make you burst!
text.KJV: 19:10 If thou hast heard a word, let it die with thee, and be bold it will not burst thee.

text.DRB: At the presence of a word the fool travaileth, as the groaning of the childbirth of an infant.
text.NABRE: Having heard something, the fool goes into labor, like a woman giving birth to a child.
text.KJV: 19:11 A foole trauaileth with a word, as a woman in labour of a child.

text.DRB: An arrow stickt in the thigh of flesh: so is a word in the heart of a fool.
text.NABRE: Like an arrow stuck in a fool’s thigh, so is gossip in the belly of a fool.
text.KJV: 19:12 As an arrowe that sticketh in a mans thigh, so is a word within a fooles belly.
19:13 Admonish a friend, it may be he hath not done it, and if he haue  that he doe it no more.
text.NABRE: Admonish your friend—he may not have done it; and if he did, that he may not do it again.
text.NRSV: Question a friend; perhaps he did not do it; or if he did, so that he may not do it again.
text.DRB: Rebuke a friend, lest perhaps he hath not understood, and say: I did it not: or if he did it, that he do it not again.

text.KJV: 19:14 Admonish thy friend, it may be he hath not said it, and if he haue, that he speake it not againe.
text.NABRE: Admonish your neighbor—he may not have said it; and if he did, that he may not say it again.
text.NRSV: Question a neighbor; perhaps he did not say it; or if he said it, so that he may not repeat it.
text.DRB: Rebuke thy neighbour, lest perhaps he said it not: and if he said it, lest perhaps he iterate it.

text.KJV: 19:15 Admonish a friend: for many times it is a slander, & beleeue not euery tale.
text.NABRE: Admonish your friend—often it may be slander; do not believe every story.
text.NRSV: Question a friend, for often it is only slander; do not believe everything you hear.
text.DRB: Rebuke thy friend: for there is often a fault committed.
And believe not every word. There is that offendeth with the tongue, but not from his heart.
text.NABRE: Then, too, a person can slip and not mean it; who has not sinned with his tongue?
text.KJV: 19:16 There is one that slippeth in his speach, but not from his heart, and who is he that hath not offended with his tongue?

text.DRB: For who is there that hath not offended in his tongue? Rebuke thy neighbour before thou threaten.
text.NABRE: Admonish your neighbor before you break with him; and give due place to the Law of the Most High.
text.KJV: 19:17 Admonish thy neighbour before thou threaten him, and not being angry giue place to the Law of the most high.

text.DRB: And give place to the fear of the Highest: because the fear of God is all wisdom, and to fear God is in it, & the disposition of the law is in all wisdom.
text.KJV: 19:18 The feare of the Lord is the first step to be accepted  and wisedome obtaineth his loue.

text.DRB: And the discipline of wickedness is not wisdom: and the cogitation of sinners is not prudence.
text.KJV: 19:19 The knowledge of the Commandements of the Lord, is the doctrine of life, and they that do things that please him, shall receiue the fruit of the tree of immortalitie.
19:20 The feare of the Lord is all wisedome, and in all wisedome is the performance of the Law, and the knowledge of his omnipotencie.
text.NRSV: All wisdom is the fear of the Lord, and in all wisdom there is the fulfillment of the law.
text.DRB: There is wickedness, and in it execration: and there is a fool that hath less wisdom.
Better is a man that hath less wisdom and lacketh understanding, in fear, than he that aboundeth in understanding and transgresseth the law of the Highest.
text.KJV: 19:21 If a seruant say to his master, I will not doe as it pleaseth thee, though afterward hee doe it, hee angereth him that nourisheth him.

text.DRB: There is an assured subtility, & the same wicked.
text.KJV: 19:22 The knowledge of wickednes is not wisedome, neither at any time the counsell of sinners, prudence.

text.DRB: And there is that uttereth an exact word telling the truth. There is that wickedly humbleth himself, and his inner parts be full of deceit:
text.KJV: 19:23 There is a wickednesse, and the same an abomination, and there is a foole wanting in wisedome.
19:24 He that hath smal vnderstanding and feareth God, is better then one that hath much wisedome, and transgresseth the Law of the most High.
text.NRSV: Better are the God-fearing who lack understanding than the highly intelligent who transgress the law.
text.DRB: and there is a just man that submitteth himself overmuch of great humility: and there is a just one that boweth his face, and feigneth himself not to see that which is unknown:
and if he be forbidden to sin for imbecility of power, if he shall find a time to do evil, he will do evil.
text.KJV: 19:25 There is an exquisite subtilty, and the same is vniust, and there is one that turneth aside to make iudgement appeare: and there is a wise man that iustifieth in iudgement.

text.DRB: Aman is known by the sight, and a wiseman is known by the shew of his face.
text.KJV: 19:26 There is a wicked man that hangeth downe his head sadly; but inwardly he is full of deceit,

text.DRB: The clothing of the body, and the laughing of the teeth, and the going of the man tell of him.
text.KJV: 19:27 Casting downe his countenance, and making as if he heard not: where he is not knowen, he will do thee a mischiefe before thou be aware.

text.DRB: There is a lying chastisement in the anger of a contumelious person: and there is a judgement, that is not allowed to be good: and there is that holdeth his peace, and he is wise.
text.KJV: 19:28 And if for want of power hee be hindered from sinning, yet when he findeth opportunitie he wil doe euil.
20:1 There is a reproofe that is not comely: againe some man holdeth his tongue, and he is wise.
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 Conduct of the Wise and the Foolish There is an admonition that is untimely, but the silent person is the wise one.
text.NRSV: There is a rebuke that is untimely, and there is the person who is wise enough to keep silent.
text.DRB: How good a thing is it to rebuke, rather than to be angry, and not to hinder him that confesseth in prayer!

text.KJV: 20:2 It is much better to reprooue, then to be angry secretly, and he that confesseth his fault, shall be preserued from hurt.
text.NABRE: It is much better to admonish than to lose one’s temper;
text.NRSV: How much better it is to rebuke than to fume!
text.DRB: The concupiscence of an eunuch shall deflower a young-maid:
so he that by violence doth unjust judgement.
text.NABRE: one who admits a fault will be kept from disgrace.
text.KJV: 20:3 How good is it when thou art reproued, to shew repentance? for so shalt thou escape wilfull sinne.

text.DRB: How good a thing is it being rebuked to shew repentance! For so thou shalt avoid willful sin.
text.NABRE: Like a eunuch lusting to violate a young woman is the one who does right under compulsion.
text.KJV: 20:4 As is the lust of an Eunuch to defloure a virgine; so is he that executeth iudgement with violence.
20:5 There is one that keepeth silence and is found wise: and another by much babling becommeth hatefull.
text.NABRE: One is silent and is thought wise; another, for being talkative, is disliked.
text.NRSV: One person keeps silent and is found to be wise; another is detested for being talkative.
text.DRB: There is that holdeth his peace, which is found wise: and there is that is odious, he which is malapert to speak.
There is that holdeth his peace having not understanding to speak: and there is that holdeth his peace, knowing the time of fit opportunity.
text.NABRE: One is silent, having nothing to say; another is silent, biding his time.
text.KJV: 20:6 Some man holdeth his tongue, because hee hath not to answere, and some keepeth silence, knowing his time.
20:7 A wise man wil hold his tongue till he see opportunitie: but a babler and a foole will regard no time.
text.NABRE: The wise remain silent till the right time comes, but a boasting fool misses the proper time.
text.NRSV: A wise person will keep silent until the right moment, but a boasting fool misses the right moment.
text.DRB: Awise man will hold his peace until a time: but a wanton, and the unwise will not observe time.
He that useth many words, shall hurt his own soul: & he that taketh authority to himself unjustly, shall be hated.
text.NABRE: Whoever talks too much is detested; whoever pretends to authority is hated.
text.KJV: 20:8 He that vseth many words shalbe abhorred; and hee that taketh to himselfe authoritie therein, shalbe hated.

text.DRB: There is proceeding in evils to a man without discipline, and there is finding to loss.
text.NABRE: There is the misfortune that brings success; and there is the gain that turns into loss.
text.KJV: 20:9 There is a sinner that hath good successe in euill things; and there is a gaine that turneth to losse.

text.DRB: There is a gift, that is not profitable: and there is a gift, the reward whereof is double.
text.NABRE: There is the gift that profits you nothing, and there is the gift that must be paid back double.
text.KJV: 20:10 There is a gift that shall not profit thee; and there is a gift whose recompence is double.

text.DRB: There is debasing because of glory: and there is that from humility shall lift up the head.
text.NABRE: There is the loss for the sake of glory, and there is the one who rises above humble circumstances.
text.KJV: 20:11 There is an abasement because of glory; and there is that lifteth vp his head from a low estate.

text.DRB: There is that redeemeth many things for a small price, and restoreth the same seven-fold.
text.NABRE: There is one who buys much for little, but pays for it seven times over.
text.KJV: 20:12 There is that buyeth much for a little, and repayeth it seuen fold.

text.DRB: Aman wise in words shall make himself beloved: but the graces of fools shall be poured out.
text.NABRE: The wise make themselves beloved by a few words, but the courtesies of fools are wasted.
text.KJV: 20:13 A wise man by his words maketh himselfe beloued: but the graces of fooles shalbe powred out.

text.DRB: The gift of the unwise shall not be profitable for thee: for his eyes are seven-fold.
text.NABRE: A gift from a fool will do you no good, for in his eyes this one gift is equal to many.
text.KJV: 20:14 The gift of a foole shall doe thee no good when thou hast it; neither yet of the enuious for his necessitie: for hee looketh to receiue many things for one.

text.DRB: He will give few things, and upbraid many: and the opening of his mouth is an inflammation.
text.NABRE: He gives little, criticizes often, and opens his mouth like a town crier. He lends today and asks for it tomorrow; such a person is hateful.
text.KJV: 20:15 Hee giueth little and vpbraideth much; hee openeth his mouth like a crier; to day he lendeth, and to morrow will he aske it againe: such an one is to be hated of God and man.

text.DRB: Today a man lendeth, and tomorrow he asketh it again: such a man is odious.
text.NABRE: A fool says, “I have no friends nor thanks for my generosity.” Those who eat his bread have a mocking tongue.
text.KJV: 20:16 The foole saith, I haue no friends, I haue no thanke for all my good deeds: and they that eate my bread speake euill of me.

text.DRB: Afool shall have no friend, and there shall be not thank to his goods.
text.NABRE: How many will ridicule him, and how often!
text.KJV: 20:17 How oft, and of how many shall he be laughed to scorne? for hee knoweth not aright what it is to haue; and it is all one vnto him, as if he had it not.
20:18 To slip vpon a pauement, is better then to slip with the tongue: so, the fall of the wicked shall come speedily.
text.NABRE: A slip on the floor is better than a slip of the tongue; in like manner the downfall of the wicked comes quickly.
text.NRSV: A slip on the pavement is better than a slip of the tongue; so the downfall of the wicked will come speedily.
text.DRB: For they that eat his bread, are of a false tongue. How often, and how many will laugh him to scorn?
For he doth not distribute with right understanding, that which was to be had: in like manner also that which was not to be had.
text.NABRE: A coarse person, an untimely story; the ignorant are always ready to offer it.
text.KJV: 20:19 An vnseasonable tale will alwayes be in the mouth of the vnwise.

text.DRB: The slipping of a false tongue, as he that falleth on the pavement: so the falls of the evil shall come hastily.
text.NABRE: A proverb spoken by a fool is unwelcome, for he does not tell it at the proper time.
text.KJV: 20:20 A wise sentence shall be reiected when it commeth out of a fools mouth: for he will not speake it in due season.

text.DRB: Aman without grace is as a vain fable, it shall be continual in the mouth of them that are without discipline.
text.NABRE: There is a person whose poverty prevents him from sinning, but when he takes his rest he has no regrets.
text.KJV: 20:21 There is that is hindred from sinning through want: and when hee taketh rest, he shall not be troubled.

text.DRB: Aparable out of a fool's mouth shall be rejected: for he doth not speak it in his time.
text.NABRE: There is a person who is destroyed through shame, and ruined by foolish posturing.
text.KJV: 20:22 There is that destroyeth his owne soule through bashfulnesse, and by accepting of persons ouerthroweth himselfe.

text.DRB: There is that is forbid to sin for poverty, and in his rest he shall be pricked.
text.NABRE: There is one who promises a friend out of shame, and so makes an enemy needlessly.
text.KJV: 20:23 There is that for bashfulnes promiseth to his friend, and maketh him his enemy for nothing.
20:24 A lie is a foule blot in a man, yet it is continually in the mouth of the vntaught.
text.NABRE: A lie is a foul blot in a person, yet it is always on the lips of the ignorant.
text.NRSV: A lie is a foul blot on a person; it is continually on the mouth of the ignorant.
text.DRB: There is that will destroy his soul for shamefastness, and by an unwise person he will destroy it: and by acception of person he will destroy himself.
There is that for shamefastness promiseth to his friend, and hath gotten an enemy of him for naught.
text.NABRE: A thief is better than an inveterate liar, yet both will suffer ruin.
text.KJV: 20:25 A thiefe is better then a man that is accustomed to lie: but they both shall haue destruction to heritage.

text.DRB: Lying is a wicked reproach in a man, and in the mouth of men without discipline it shall be continually.
text.NABRE: A liar’s way leads to dishonor, and his shame remains ever with him.
text.KJV: 20:26 The disposition of a liar is dishonourable, and his shame is euer with him.
20:27 A wise man shall promote himselfe to honour with his words: and hee that hath vnderstanding, will please great men.
text.NABRE: The wise gain promotion with few words, the prudent please the great.
text.NRSV: The wise person advances himself by his words, and one who is sensible pleases the great.
text.DRB: Better is a thief, than the continual custom of a lying man, but both shall inherit perdition.
The manners of lying men are without honour: and their confusion is with them without intermission.
text.NABRE: Those who work the land have abundant crops, and those who please the great are pardoned their faults.
text.KJV: 20:28 He that tilleth his land, shall increase his heape: and he that pleaseth great men, shal get pardon for iniquity.

text.DRB: Awise man in his words shall utter himself, and a prudent man shall please great persons.
text.NABRE: Favors and gifts blind the eyes; like a muzzle over the mouth they silence reproofs.
text.KJV: 20:29 Presents and gifts blind the eyes of the wise, and stoppe vp his mouth that he cannot reprooue.
20:30 Wisedome that is hidde, and treasure that is hoarded vp, what profit is in them both?
text.NABRE: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure— what value has either?
text.NRSV: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure, of what value is either?
text.DRB: He that tilleth his land, shall make an high heap of corn: and who so worketh justice, he shall be exalted: and he that pleaseth great men, shall avoid iniquity.

text.KJV: 20:31 Better is he that hideth his folly, then a man that hideth his wisedome.
text.NABRE: Better are those who hide their folly than those who hide their wisdom.
text.NRSV: Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom.
text.DRB: Presents & gifts blind the eyes of judges, and as one dumb in the mouth turneth away their chastisements.
Wisdom hid, and treasure not seen: what profit is there in both?
text.KJV: 20:32 Necessary patience in seeking the Lord, is better then he that leadeth his life without a guide.

text.DRB: Better is he that concealeth his foolishness, than the man that hideth his wisdom.

text.KJV: 21:1 My sonne, hast thou sinned? doe so no more, but aske pardon for thy former sinnes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Dangers from Sin My child, if you have sinned, do so no more, and for your past sins pray to be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Have you sinned, my child? Do so no more, but ask forgiveness for your past sins.
text.DRB: Son hast thou sinned? Do so no more: but for the old also pray that they may be forgiven thee.

text.KJV: 21:2 Flee from sinne as from the face of a Serpent: for if thou commest too neere it, it will bite thee: the teeth thereof, are as the teeth of a lyon, slaying the soules of men.
text.NABRE: Flee from sin as from a serpent that will bite you if you go near it; Its teeth, lion’s teeth, destroying human lives.
text.NRSV: Flee from sin as from a snake; for if you approach it, it will bite you; its teeth are lion’s teeth, and can destroy human lives.
text.DRB: As from the face of a serpent fly from sins: and if thou approach to them they will receive thee.

text.KJV: 21:3 All iniquitie is as a two edged sword, the wounds whereof cannot be healed.
text.NABRE: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; when it cuts, there is no healing.
text.NRSV: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; there is no healing for the wound it inflicts.
text.DRB: The teeth of a lion the teeth thereof, killing the souls of men.
All iniquity is as a two-edged sword, there is no remedy for the wound thereof.
text.NABRE: Panic and pride wipe out wealth; so too the house of the proud is uprooted.
text.KJV: 21:4 To terrifie and doe wrong, will waste riches: thus the house of proude men shalbe made desolate.
21:5 A prayer out of a poore mans mouth reacheth to the eares of God, and his iudgement commeth speedily.
text.NABRE: Prayer from the lips of the poor is heard at once, and justice is quickly granted them.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgment comes speedily.
text.DRB: Brawling and injuries shall bring the substance to nothing: and the house that is very rich, shall be made nothing by pride: so the substance of the proud shall be rooted out.
The prayer of the poor out of the mouth shall come to his ears, and judgement shall come for him speedily.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates correction walks the sinner’s path, but whoever fears the Lord repents in his heart.
text.KJV: 21:6 He that hateth to be reprooued, is in the way of sinners: but hee that feareth the Lord, will repent from his heart.

text.DRB: He that hateth chastisement, is the trace of a sinner: and he that feareth God, will turn to his own heart.
text.NABRE: Glib speakers are widely known, but when they slip the sensible perceive it.
text.KJV: 21:7 An eloquent man is knowen farre and neere, but a man of vnderstanding knoweth when he slippeth.

text.DRB: The mighty in a bold tongue is known a far off, and a wiseman knoweth himself to fall by him.
text.NABRE: Those who build their houses with someone else’s money are like those who collect stones for their funeral mounds.
text.KJV: 21:8 He that buildeth his house with other mens money, is like one that gathereth himselfe stones for the tombe of his buriall.

text.DRB: He that buildeth his house at other men's charges, is as he that gathereth his stones in the winter.
text.NABRE: A band of criminals is like a bundle of tow; they will end in a flaming fire.
text.KJV: 21:9 The congregation of the wicked is like tow wrapped together: and the end of them is a flame of fire to destroy them.
21:10 The way of sinners is made plaine with stones, but at the ende thereof is the pit of hell.
text.NABRE: The path of sinners is smooth stones, but its end is the pit of Sheol.
text.NRSV: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.
text.DRB: The synagogue of sinners is as tow gathered together, and their consummation a flame of fire.
The way of sinners is paved with stones, & in their end, hell, & darkness, and pains.
text.NABRE: The Wise and Foolish: A Contrast Those who keep the Law control their thoughts; perfect fear of the Lord is wisdom.
text.KJV: 21:11 Hee that keepeth the Law of the Lord, getteth the vnderstanding thereof: and the perfection of the feare of the Lord, is wisedome.

text.DRB: He that keepeth justice, shall contain the understanding thereof.
text.NABRE: One who is not clever can never be taught, but there is a cleverness filled with bitterness.
text.KJV: 21:12 He that is not wise, will not be taught: but there is a wisedome which multiplieth bitternesse.

text.DRB: The consummation of the fear of God wisdom and understanding.
text.NABRE: The knowledge of the wise wells up like a flood, and their counsel like a living spring.
text.KJV: 21:13 The knowledge of a wise man shall abound like a flood: and his counsell is like a pure fountaine of life.

text.DRB: He shall not be taught, that is not wise in good.
text.NABRE: A fool’s mind is like a broken jar: it cannot hold any knowledge at all.
text.KJV: 21:14 The inner parts of a foole, are like a broken vessell, and he will holde no knowledge as long as he liueth.
21:15 If a skilfull man heare a wise word, hee will commend it, and adde vnto it: but assoone as one of no vnderstanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behinde his backe.
text.NABRE: When the intelligent hear a wise saying, they praise it and add to it. The wanton hear it with distaste and cast it behind their back.
text.NRSV: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it;
text.DRB: But there is wisdom that aboundeth in evil: and there is no understanding where bitterness is.

text.KJV: 21:16 The talking of a foole is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.
text.NABRE: A fool’s chatter is like a load on a journey, but delight is to be found on the lips of the intelligent.
text.NRSV: The chatter of a fool is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.
text.DRB: The knowledge of the wise shall abound as an inundation, and his counsel is permanent as a fountain of life.
The heart of a fool is as a broken vessel, and all wisdom it shall not hold.
text.NABRE: The views of the prudent are sought in an assembly, and their words are taken to heart.
text.KJV: 21:17 They inquire at the mouth of the wise man in the congregation, and they shall ponder his words in their heart.

text.DRB: Aman of knowledge will praise what soever wise word he shall hear, and will apply it to himself: the riotous man hath heard it, and it shall displease him, and he will cast it behind his back.
text.NABRE: Like a house in ruins is wisdom to a fool; to the stupid, knowledge is incomprehensible chatter.
text.KJV: 21:18 As is a house that is destroyed, so is wisedome to a foole: and the knowledge of the vnwise, is as talke without sense.

text.DRB: The narration of a fool is as a burden in the way: for in the lips of the wise shall grace be found.
text.NABRE: To the senseless, education is fetters on the feet, like manacles on the right hand.
text.KJV: 21:19 Doctrine vnto fooles, is as fetters on the feete, and like manacles on the right hand.
21:20 A foole lifteth vp his voyce with laughter, but a wise man doeth scarce smile a litle.
text.NABRE: Fools raise their voice in laughter, but the prudent at most smile quietly.
text.NRSV: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but a clever person smiles quietly.
text.DRB: The mouth of the prudent is sought in the Church, and they will think upon his words in their hearts.
As a house destroyed, so is wisdom to a fool: & the knowledge of the unwise inexplicable words.
text.NABRE: Like a gold ornament is education to the wise, like a bracelet on the right arm.
text.KJV: 21:21 Learning is vnto a wise man, as an ornament of gold, and like a bracelet vpon his right arme.

text.DRB: Fetters on the feet, doctrine to a fool, and as manacles upon the right hand.
text.NABRE: A fool steps boldly into a house, while the well-bred are slow to make an entrance.
text.KJV: 21:22 A foolish mans foote is soone in his  house: but a man of experience is ashamed of him.

text.DRB: Afool in laughter exalteth his voice, but a wise man will scarce laugh secretly.
text.NABRE: A boor peeps through the doorway of a house, but the educated stay outside.
text.KJV: 21:23 A foole will peepe in at the doore into the house, but he that is well nurtured, will stand without.

text.DRB: Doctrine to the prudent is a golden ornament, and as it were a bracelet on the right arm.
text.NABRE: It is rude for one to listen at a door; the discreet person would be overwhelmed by the disgrace.
text.KJV: 21:24 It is the rudenesse of a man to hearken at the doore: but a wise man will be grieued with the disgrace.

text.DRB: The foot of a fool goeth easily into his neighbour's house: & a cunning man will be abashed at the person of the mighty.
text.NABRE: The lips of the arrogant talk of what is not their concern, but the discreet carefully weigh their words.
text.KJV: 21:25 The lips of talkers will bee telling such things as pertaine not vnto them: but the words of such as haue vnderstanding, are weighed in the ballance.
21:26 The heart of fooles is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.NABRE: The mind of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.
text.NRSV: The heart of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.DRB: Afool will look from the window into the house: but he nurtured will stand without.
It is the folly of a man to harken by the door: and a wiseman will be grieved with the contumely.
text.NABRE: When the godless curse their adversary, they really curse themselves.
text.KJV: 21:27 When the vngodly curseth Satan, he curseth his owne soule.

text.DRB: The lips of the unwise shall tell foolish things: but the words of the wise shall be pondered in balance.
text.NABRE: Slanderers sully themselves, and are hated by their neighbors.
text.KJV: 21:28 A whisperer defileth his owne soule, and is hated wheresoeuer hee dwelleth.

text.DRB: The heart of fools is in their mouth: and the mouth of wisemen is in their heart.
Whilst the impious curseth the devil, he curseth his own soul.
The whisperer shall defile his soul, and shall be hated in all: and he that shall abide with him, shall be odious: the still man and wise shall be honoured.

text.KJV: 22:1 A slouthful man is compared to a filthy stone, and euery one will hisse him out to his disgrace.
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 On Laziness and Foolishness The sluggard is like a filthy stone; everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.NRSV: The sluggard is like a filthy stone, and everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.DRB: The sluggard is stoned with a dirty stone, & all men will speak of his disgrace.
The sluggard is stoned with the dung of oxen: and every one that shall touch him, will shake his hands.
text.NABRE: The sluggard is like a lump of dung; whoever touches it shakes it off the hands.
text.KJV: 22:2 A slouthfull man is compared to the filth of a dunghill: euery man that takes it vp, will shake his hand.
22:3 An euill nurtured sonne is the dishonour of his father that begate him: and a  daughter is borne to his losse.
text.NABRE: An undisciplined child is a disgrace to its father; if it be a daughter, she brings him to poverty.
text.NRSV: It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.
text.DRB: The confusion of the father is of a son without discipline: and the daughter shall be made of less account.

text.KJV: 22:4 A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but shee that liueth dishonestly, is her fathers heauinesse.
text.NABRE: A thoughtful daughter obtains a husband of her own; a shameless one is her father’s grief.
text.NRSV: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.
text.DRB: Awise daughter is an inheritance to her husband: for she that confoundeth, is made a contumely to her father.
She that is bold shameth father and husband, and shall not be inferior to the impious: but of them both she shall be dishonoured.
text.NABRE: A hussy shames her father and her husband; she is despised by both.
text.KJV: 22:5 Shee that is bold, dishonoureth both her father and her husband, but they both shall despise her.

text.DRB: Music in mourning is a tale out of time: scourges and doctrine are at all time wisdom.
text.NABRE: Like music at the time of mourning is ill-timed talk, but lashes and discipline are at all times wisdom.
text.KJV: 22:6 A tale out of season  musick in mourning: but stripes and correction of wisedome are neuer out of time.
22:7 Who so teacheth a foole, is as one that gleweth a potsheard together, and as hee that waketh one from a sound sleepe.
text.NRSV: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.
text.DRB: He that teacheth a fool, is as he that glueth together a pot-sherd.
He that telleth a word to him that heareth not, is as he that raiseth up a man sleeping out of an heavy sleep.
text.KJV: 22:8 Hee that telleth a tale to a foole, speaketh to one in a slumber: when hee hath told his tale, he will say, What is the matter?

text.DRB: He speaketh with him that sleepeth, which uttereth wisdom to a fool: and in the end of the narration he saith: Who is this?
text.KJV: 22:9 If children liue honestly, and haue wherwithall, they shall couer the basenesse of their parents.

text.DRB: Weep upon the dead, for his light hath failed: and weep upon a fool, for he faileth in understanding.
text.KJV: 22:10 But children being haughtie through disdaine, and want of nurture, doe staine the nobilitie of their kinred.

22:11 Weepe for the dead, for hee hath lost the light: and weepe for the foole, for he wanteth vnderstanding: make litle weeping for the dead, for hee is at rest: but the life of the foole is worse then death.
text.NRSV: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind.
text.DRB: Weep a little upon the dead, because he is at rest.

text.KJV: 22:12 Seuen dayes doe men mourne for him that is dead; but for a foole, and an vngodly man, all the dayes of his life.
text.NRSV: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for a fool or an ungodly person it lasts all the days of their lives.
text.DRB: For the wicked life of the very wicked, above the death of a fool.
The mourning of the dead is seven days: but of a fool and of the impious, all the days of their life.
text.KJV: 22:13 Talke not much with a foole, and goe not to him that hath no vnderstanding, beware of him lest thou haue trouble, and thou shalt neuer be defiled with his fooleries: depart from him, and thou shalt find rest, and neuer bee disquieted with madnesse.

text.DRB: Speak not much with a fool, and go not with the unwise.
text.KJV: 22:14 What is heauier then lead? and what is the name thereof, but a foole?

text.DRB: Keep thyself from him, that thou have no molestation, and thou shalt not be defiled with his sin.
text.KJV: 22:15 Sand, and salt, and a masse of yron is easier to beare then a man without vnderstanding.

text.DRB: Turn aside from him, and thou shalt find rest, and shall not be wearied with his folly.
text.KJV: 22:16 As timber girt and bound together in a building, cannot be loosed with shaking: so the heart that is stablished by aduised counsel, shal feare at no time.

text.DRB: What shall be heavier than lead? And what other name hath it but fool?
text.KJV: 22:17 A heart setled vpon a thought of vnderstanding, is as a faire plaistering on the wall of a gallerie.

text.DRB: It is easier to bear sand and salt, and a mass of iron, than an unwise man, and a fool, and impious.
text.KJV: 22:18 Pales set on an high place will neuer stand against the wind: so a feareful heart in the imagination of a foole, can not stand against any feare.

text.DRB: Aframe of wood bound together in the foundation of a building, shall not be dissolved: so also the heart confirmed in the cogitation of counsel.
text.KJV: 22:19 He that pricketh the eye, wil make teares to fall: and he that pricketh the heart, maketh it to shewe her knowledge.

text.DRB: The cogitation of the wise at all time, yea by fear shall not be depraved.
text.KJV: 22:20 Who so casteth a stone at the birds, frayeth them away, and he that vpbraideth his friend, breaketh friendship.

text.DRB: As stakes in high places, and plasterings laid without cost, shall not abide against the face of the wind:
text.KJV: 22:21 Though thou drewest a sword at thy friend, yet despaire not, for there way be a returning (to fauour.)

22:22 If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, feare not, for there may be a reconciliation: except for vpbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound, for, for these things euery friend will depart.
text.NRSV: If you have opened your mouth against a friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow—in these cases any friend will flee.
text.DRB: so also a fearful heart in the cogitation of a fool shall not resist against the violence of fear.
As a trembling heart in the cogitation of a fool, all time will not fear, so also he that continueth always in the precepts of God.
text.KJV: 22:23 Be faithfull to thy neighbour in his pouertie, that thou mayest reioyce in his prosperitie: abide stedfast vnto him in the time of his trouble, that thou mayest bee heire with him in his heritage: for a meane estate is not alwayes to be contemned, nor the rich that is foolish, to be had in admiration.

text.DRB: He that pricketh the eye, bringeth forth tears: and he that pricketh the heart, bringeth forth feeling.
text.KJV: 22:24 As the vapour and smoke of a furnace goeth before the fire: so reuiling before blood.

text.DRB: He that casteth a stone at fowls, and shall throw them down: so he that speaketh reproachfully to his friend, dissolveth friendship.
text.KJV: 22:25 I will not be ashamed to defend a friend: neither will I hide my selfe from him.

text.DRB: Although thou drawest a sword at a friend, despair not: for there is returning to a friend.
text.KJV: 22:26 And if any euill happen vnto me by him, euery one that heareth it will beware of him.
22:27 Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seale of wisedome vpon my lippes, that I fall not suddenly by them, & that my tongue destroy me not?
text.NRSV: Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?
text.DRB: If thou open a sad mouth, fear not: for there is agreement: except taunt, and reproach, and pride, and revealing of secret, and a treacherous wound: in all these things a friend will fly away.
Possess fidelity with a friend in his poverty, that in his goods also thou mayst rejoice.
In the time of his tribulation continue faithful to him, that in his inheritance also thou mayst be heir with him.
Before the fire the vapour of the chimney, and the smoke of the fire riseth on high: so also before blood evil words, and contumelies, & threats.
I will not be ashamed to salute a friend, from his face I will not hide myself: and if there chance evils to me by him, I will bear it.
Every one that shall hear, will beware of him.
Who will give a guard to my mouth, and a sure seal upon my lips, that I fall not by them, and my tongue destroy me?

text.KJV: 23:1 O Lord, father and gouernour of all my whole life, leaue me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, do not let me fall because of them!
text.NRSV: O Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!
text.DRB: O Lord father, and dominatour of my life, leave me not in their counsel: nor suffer me to fall in them.
Who layeth on stripes in my cogitation, and in my heart the doctrine of wisdom, and in their ignorances they spare me not, and their offences appear not,
text.NABRE: Who will apply the lash to my thoughts, and to my mind the rod of discipline, That my failings may not be spared or the sins of my heart overlooked?
text.KJV: 23:2 Who will set scourges ouer my thoughts, and the discipline of wisedome ouer mine heart? that they spare me not for mine ignorances and it passe not by my sinnes:

text.DRB: and my ignorances increase not, and my offences be multiplied, and my sins abound, and I fall in the sight of mine adversaries, & mine enemy rejoice?
text.NABRE: Otherwise my failings may increase, and my sins be multiplied; And I fall before my adversaries, and my enemy rejoice over me?
text.KJV: 23:3 Least mine ignorances increase, and my sinnes abound to my destruction, and I fall before mine aduersaries, and mine enemie reioyce ouer mee, whose hope is farre from thy mercy.

text.DRB: O Lord father, and God of my life, leave me not in their cogitation.
text.NABRE: Lord, Father and God of my life, do not give me haughty eyes;
text.KJV: 23:4 O Lord, father and God of my life, giue me not a proud looke, but turne away from thy seruants alwaies a haughty minde:

text.DRB: Haughtiness of mine eyes give me not, and all desire turn away from me.
text.NABRE: remove evil desire from my heart.
text.KJV: 23:5 Turne away from mee vaine hopes, and concupiscence, and thou shalt hold him vp that is desirous alwaies to serue thee.

text.DRB: Take from me the concupiscences of the belly, and let not the concupiscences of copulation take hold of me, and give me not over to a shameless and foolish mind.
text.NABRE: Let neither gluttony nor lust overcome me; do not give me up to shameless desires.
text.KJV: 23:6 Let not the greedinesse of the belly, nor lust of the flesh take hold of me, and giue not ouer me thy seruant into an impudent minde.
23:7 Heare, O yee children, the discipline of the mouth: He that keepeth it, shall neuer be taken in his lippes.
text.NABRE: Proper Use of the Tongue Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth, for whoever keeps it will not be ensnared.
text.NRSV: Listen, my children, to instruction about the mouth; the one who keeps it will not be caught.
text.DRB: O children hear the doctrine of the mouth: and he that will keep it, shall not perish by his lips, nor be scandalized in most wicked works.
Asinner is taught in his vanity, and the proud and the evil speaker shall be scandalized in them.
text.NABRE: Through the lips the sinner is caught; by them the reviler and the arrogant are tripped up.
text.KJV: 23:8 The sinner shall be left in his foolishnesse: both the euill speaker and the proud shall fall thereby.
23:9 Accustome not thy mouth to swearing: neither vse thy selfe to the naming of the holy one.
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, or habitually utter the Holy Name.
text.NRSV: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One.
text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to swearing: for there be many falls in it.
But let not the naming of God be usual in thy mouth, and meddle not with the names of Saints, because thou shalt not scape free from them.
text.NABRE: Just as a servant constantly under scrutiny will not be without bruises, So one who swears continually by the Holy Name will never remain free from sin.
text.KJV: 23:10 For as a seruant that is continually beaten, shall not be without a blew marke: so hee that sweareth and nameth God continually, shal not be faultlesse.

text.DRB: For as a servant daily examined, lacketh not the mark thereof: so every one that sweareth, and nameth, shall not be wholly purged from sin.
text.NABRE: Those who swear many oaths heap up offenses; and the scourge will never be far from their houses. If they swear in error, guilt is incurred; if they neglect their obligation, the sin is doubly great. If they swear without reason they cannot be declared innocent, for their households will be filled with calamities.
text.KJV: 23:11 A man that vseth much swearing shall be filled with iniquity, and the plague shall neuer depart from his house: If he shall offend, his sinne shall be vpon him: and if he acknowledge not his sinne, hee maketh a double offence, and if he sweare in vaine, he shall not be innocent, but his house shall be full of calamities.

text.DRB: Aman that sweareth much shall be filled with iniquity, and plague shall not depart from his house.
text.NABRE: There are words comparable to death; may they never be heard in the inheritance of Jacob. To the devout all such words are foreign; they do not wallow in sin.
text.KJV: 23:12 There is a word that is clothed about with death: God graunt that it be not found in the heritage of Iacob, for all such things shall be farre from the godly, and they shall not wallow in their sinnes.

text.DRB: And if he frustrate it, his sin shall be upon him: and if he dissemble, he offendeth double:
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to coarse talk, for it involves sinful speech.
text.KJV: 23:13 Use not thy mouth to vntemperate swearing, for therein is the word of sinne.

text.DRB: and if he swear in vain, he shall not be justified: for his house shall be filled with retribution.
text.NABRE: Keep your father and mother in mind when you sit among the mighty, Lest you forget yourself in their presence and disgrace your upbringing. Then you will wish you had never been born and will curse the day of your birth.
text.KJV: 23:14 Remember thy father and thy mother, when thou sittest among great men. Be not forgetfull before them, and so thou by thy custome become a foole, and wish that thou hadst not beene borne, and curse the day of thy natiuitie.

text.DRB: There is another contrary speech, also to death, be it not found in the inheritance of Jacob.
text.NABRE: Those accustomed to using abusive language will never acquire discipline as long as they live.
text.KJV: 23:15 The man that is accustomed to opprobrious words, will neuer be reformed all the daies of his life.

text.DRB: For of the merciful all those things shall be taken away, and they will not wallow in sins.
text.NABRE: Sins of the Flesh Two types of people multiply sins, and a third draws down wrath: Burning passion is like a blazing fire, not to be quenched till it burns itself out; One unchaste with his kindred never stops until fire breaks forth.
text.KJV: 23:16 Two sorts of men multiply sinne, and the third will bring wrath: a hot minde is as a burning fire, it will neuer be quenched till it be consumed: a fornicatour in the body of his flesh, will neuer cease till he hath kindled a fire.

text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to unuttered speech: for there is in it a word of sin.
text.NABRE: To the unchaste all bread is sweet; he is never through till he dies.
text.KJV: 23:17 All bread is sweete to a whoremonger, he will not leaue off till he die.

23:18 A man that breaketh wedlocke, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darknesse: the walles couer me; & no body seeth me, what neede I to feare? The most high wil not remember my sinnes:
text.NABRE: The man who dishonors his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, walls hide me, no one sees me. Who can stop me from sinning?” He is not mindful of the Most High,
text.NRSV: A man who violates his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me; why should I fear? The Most High will not remember my sins.”
text.DRB: Remember thy father and thy mother, for thou sittest in the midst of great men:

text.KJV: 23:19 Such a man only feareth the eies of men, and knoweth not that the eies of the Lord are tenne thousand times brighter then the Sunne, beholding all the waies of men, and considering the most secret parts.
text.NABRE: fearing only human eyes. He does not realize that the eyes of the Lord, ten thousand times brighter than the sun, Observe every step taken and peer into hidden corners.
text.NRSV: His fear is confined to human eyes, and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun.
text.DRB: lest perhaps God forget thee in their sight, & being besotted with thy daily custom, thou suffer reproach, & hadst better not have been born, and curse the day of thy nativity.
Aman accustomed to the words of reproach, will not be instructed in all his days.
text.NABRE: The one who knows all things before they exist still knows them all after they are made.
text.KJV: 23:20 He knew all things ere euer they were created, so also after they were perfited, he looked vpon them all:

text.DRB: Two sorts abound in sins, and the third bringeth wrath and perdition.
text.NABRE: Such a man will be denounced in the streets of the city; and where he least suspects it, he will be apprehended.
text.KJV: 23:21 This man shall bee punished in the streets of the citie, and where he suspecteth not, he shall be taken.
23:22 Thus shall it goe also with the wife, that leaueth her husband, and bringeth in an heire by another:
text.NABRE: So it is with the woman unfaithful to her husband, who offers him an heir by another man.
text.NRSV: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.
text.DRB: An hot soul as a burning fire will not be quenched, till it swallow somewhat.
and a wicked man in the mouth of his flesh will not cease till he kindle a fire.
text.NABRE: First of all, she has disobeyed the law of the Most High; second, she has wronged her husband; Third, through her wanton adultery she has brought forth children by another man.
text.KJV: 23:23 For first she hath disobeyed the Law of the most High: and secondly, she hath trespassed against her owne husband, and thirdly, she hath played the whore in adultery, and brought children by another man.

text.DRB: To a man that is a fornicator all bread is sweet, he will not be weary transgressing unto the end.
text.NABRE: Such a woman will be dragged before the assembly, and her punishment will extend to her children.
text.KJV: 23:24 Shee shall be brought out into the congregation, and inquisition shalbe made of her children.

text.DRB: Every man that passeth beyond his own bed, contemning against his own soul, and saying: Who seeth me?
text.NABRE: Her children will not take root; her branches will not bring forth fruit.
text.KJV: 23:25 Her children shall not take root, and her branches shall bring foorth no fruit.

text.DRB: Darkness compasseth me, and the walls cover me, and no man beholdeth me: whom do I fear? The Highest will not be mindful of my sins.
text.NABRE: She will leave behind an accursed memory; her disgrace will never be blotted out.
text.KJV: 23:26 She shall leaue her memorie to be cursed, and her reproch shall not be blotted out.
23:27 And they that remaine, shall know that there is nothing better then the feare of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter then to take heed vnto the Commandement of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus all who dwell on the earth shall know, all who remain in the world shall understand, That nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, nothing sweeter than obeying the commandments of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Those who survive will know that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the Lord’s commandments.
text.DRB: And he understandeth not that his eye seeth all things, for that such fear of man expelleth from him the fear of God, & the eyes of men fearing him:

and he knoweth not that the eyes of our Lord are much more brighter than the sun, beholding round about all the ways of man, and the bottom of the depth, and the hearts of men, looking into the hidden parts.
text.KJV: 23:28 It is great glory to follow the Lord, & to be receiued of him is long life.

text.DRB: For all things were known unto our Lord God, before they were created: so also after it is perfected he beholdeth all things.
Revenge shall be taken on this man in the streets of the city, and as an horse-colt he shall be chased: and where he expected not, he shall be apprehended.
And he shall be in dishonour with all men, for that he understood not the fear of our Lord.
So every woman also that forsaketh her husband, & getteth inheritance by marriage of another.
For first she hath been unfaithful in the law of the Highest: and secondly she hath sinned against her husband: thirdly she hath fornicated in adultery, and hath gotten her children of another man.
This woman shall be brought into the Church, and upon her children there shall be examination.
Her children shall not take root, & her boughs shall not yield fruit.
She shall leave her memory to be cursed, and her dishonour shall not be wiped out.
And they that are left shall know, that nothing is better than the fear of God: and nothing sweeter, than to have regard to the commandments of our Lord.
It is great glory to follow our Lord: for length of days shall be taken of him.

text.KJV: 24:1 Wisedome shall praise her selfe, and shall glory in the midst of her people.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 Praise of Wisdom Wisdom sings her own praises, among her own people she proclaims her glory.
text.NRSV: Wisdom praises herself, and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.
text.DRB: Wisdom shall praise her soul, & shall be honoured in God, and shall glory in the midst of her people,

text.KJV: 24:2 In the Congregation of the most high, shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.
text.NABRE: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, in the presence of his host she tells of her glory:
text.NRSV: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:
text.DRB: and shall open her mouth in the churches of the Highest, and shall glory in the sight of his power,

text.KJV: 24:3 I came out of the mouth of the most High, and couered the earth as a cloud.
text.NABRE: “From the mouth of the Most High I came forth, and covered the earth like a mist.
text.NRSV: “I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.”
text.DRB: and in the midst of her people she shall be exalted, and in the holy assembly she shall be admired,
and in the multitude of the elect she shall have praise, and among the blessed she shall be blessed, saying:
text.NABRE: In the heights of heaven I dwelt, and my throne was in a pillar of cloud.
text.KJV: 24:4 I dwelt in high places, and my throne is in a cloudy pillar.

text.DRB: I came forth from the mouth of the Highest, the first begotten before all creatures.
text.NABRE: The vault of heaven I compassed alone, and walked through the deep abyss.
text.KJV: 24:5 I alone compassed the circuit of heauen, and walked in the bottome of the deepe.

text.DRB: I made that in the heavens, there should rise light that faileth not, and as a cloud I covered all the earth.
text.NABRE: Over waves of the sea, over all the land, over every people and nation I held sway.
text.KJV: 24:6 In the waues of the sea, and in all the earth, and in euery people, and nation, I got a possession.

text.DRB: I dwelt in the highest places, and my throne is in the pillar of a cloud.
text.NABRE: Among all these I sought a resting place. In whose inheritance should I abide?
text.KJV: 24:7 With all these I sought rest: and in whose inheritance shall I abide?
24:8 So the creatour of all things gaue mee a commandement, and hee that made me, caused my tabernacle to rest: and said, Let thy dwelling be in Iacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.
text.NABRE: “Then the Creator of all gave me his command, and my Creator chose the spot for my tent. He said, ‘In Jacob make your dwelling, in Israel your inheritance.’
text.NRSV: “Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, ‘Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.’”
text.DRB: I alone have gone round about the compass of heaven, and have penetrated into the bottom of the depth, and have walked in the waves of the sea,

text.KJV: 24:9 Hee created mee from the beginning before the world, and I shall neuer faile.
text.NABRE: Before all ages, from the beginning, he created me, and through all ages I shall not cease to be.
text.NRSV: “Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.”
text.DRB: and stood in all the earth: and in all people.
and in every nation I have had the primacy:
text.NABRE: In the holy tent I ministered before him, and so I was established in Zion.
text.KJV: 24:10 In the holy Tabernacle I serued before him: and so was I established in Sion.

text.DRB: and I have by strength trodden down the hearts of all the excellent, and the base; and in all these things I sought rest, & I shall abide in the inheritance of our Lord.
text.NABRE: In the city he loves as he loves me, he gave me rest; in Jerusalem, my domain.
text.KJV: 24:11 Likewise in the beloued citie he gaue mee rest, and in Ierusalem was my power.

text.DRB: Then the creator of all commanded, and said to me: & he that created me, rested in my tabernacle,
text.NABRE: I struck root among the glorious people, in the portion of the Lord, his heritage.
text.KJV: 24:12 And I tooke roote in an honourable people, euen in the portion of the Lords inheritance.

text.DRB: and he said to me: Inhabit in Jacob, and inherit in Israel, and take root in mine elect.
text.NABRE: “Like a cedar in Lebanon I grew tall, like a cypress on Mount Hermon;
text.KJV: 24:13 I was exalted like a Cedar in Libanus, and as a Cypresse tree vpon the mountaines of Hermon.

text.DRB: From the beginning and before the worlds was I created, and unto the world to come I shall not cease, and in the holy habitation I have ministered before him.
text.NABRE: I grew tall like a palm tree in Engedi, like rosebushes in Jericho; Like a fair olive tree in the field, like a plane tree beside water I grew tall.
text.KJV: 24:14 I was exalted like a palme tree in Engaddi, and as a rose-plant in Iericho, as a faire oliue tree in a pleasant fielde, and grew vp as a planetree by the water.

text.DRB: And so in Sion was I established, and in the sanctified city likewise I rested, and my power was in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Like cinnamon and fragrant cane, like precious myrrh I gave forth perfume; Like galbanum and onycha and mastic, like the odor of incense in the holy tent.
text.KJV: 24:15 I gaue a sweete smell like cinamon, and aspalathus, and I yeelded a pleasant odour like the best mirrhe, as Galbanum and Onix, and sweet Storax, and as the sume of franckincense in the Tabernacle.

text.DRB: And I took root in an honourable people, and in the portion of my God his inheritance, and my abiding is in the full assembly of saints.
text.NABRE: “I spread out my branches like a terebinth, my branches so glorious and so graceful.
text.KJV: 24:16 As the Turpentine tree, I stretched out my branches, and my branches are the branches of honour and grace.

text.DRB: I am exalted as a cedar in Libanus, and as a cypress tree in mount Sion.
text.NABRE: I bud forth delights like a vine; my blossoms are glorious and rich fruit.
text.KJV: 24:17 As the Uine brought I foorth pleasant sauour, and my flowers are the fruit of honour and riches.

text.DRB: As a palm tree in Cades am I exalted, and as a rose plant in Jericho:
text.KJV: 24:18 I am the mother of faire loue, and feare, and knowledge, and holy hope, I therefore being eternall, am giuen to all my children which are named of him.
24:19 Come vnto me all ye that be desirous of mee, and fill your selues with my fruits.
text.NRSV: “Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.”
text.DRB: As a fair olive tree in the fields, and as a plane tree by the water in the streets am I exalted.

text.KJV: 24:20 For my memorial is sweeter then hony, and mine inheritance then the hony combe.
text.NRSV: “For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.”
text.DRB: I gave an odour as cinnamon, & aromatical balm: as chosen myrrh have I given the sweetness of odour:

text.KJV: 24:21 They that eate mee shall yet be hungry, and they that drinke me shall yet be thirstie.
text.NRSV: “Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.”
text.DRB: and as storax, and galbanum, and onyx, and aloes, and as Libanus not cut, have I perfumed mine habitation, and mine odour is as balm not mingled.
I have spread out my boughs as the terebinth, and my boughs are of honour & grace.
text.KJV: 24:22 He that obeyeth me, shall neuer be confounded, and they that worke by me, shall not doe amisse.
24:23 All these things are the booke of the Couenant of the most high God, euen the Law which Moses commanded for an heritage vnto the Congregations of Iacob.
text.NRSV: All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.
text.DRB: I as a vine have fructified sweetness of odour: and my flowers are fruit of honour and honesty.
I am the mother of beautiful love, and of fear, and of knowledge, and of holy hope.
text.KJV: 24:24 Faint not to bee strong in the Lord; that he may confirme you, cleaue vnto him: for the Lord Almightie is God alone, and besides him there is no other Sauiour.

text.DRB: In me is all grace of way and truth, in me all hope of life and virtue.
text.KJV: 24:25 He filleth all things with his wisdome, as Physon, and as Tigris in the time of the new fruits.

text.DRB: Pass to me all ye that desire me, and be filled of my generations.
text.KJV: 24:26 He maketh the vnderstanding to abound like Euphrates, and as Iorden in the time of the haruest.

text.DRB: For my spirit is sweet above honey, and mine inheritance above honey and the honey comb.
text.KJV: 24:27 He maketh the doctrine of knowledge appeare as the light, and as Geon in the time of vintage.

text.DRB: My memory is unto generations of worlds.
text.KJV: 24:28 The first man knew her not perfectly: no more shall the last finde her out.

text.DRB: They that eat me, shall yet hunger: and they that drink me, shall yet thirst.
text.KJV: 24:29 For her thoughts are more then the Sea, and her counsels profounder then the great deepe.

text.DRB: He that heareth me, shall not be confounded: and they that work in me, shall not sin.
text.KJV: 24:30 I also came out as a brooke from a riuer, and as a conduit into a garden.

text.DRB: They that explicate me, shall have life everlasting.
text.KJV: 24:31 I said, I will water my best garden, and will water abundantly my garden bedde: and loe, my brooke became a riuer, and my riuer became a sea.

text.DRB: All these things are the book of life, and the testament of the Highest, & the knowledge of truth.
text.KJV: 24:32 I will yet make doctrine to shine as the morning, and will send forth her light afarre off.

text.DRB: Moyses commanded a law in the precepts of justices, and an inheritance to the house of Jacob, and the promises to Israel.
text.KJV: 24:33 I will yet powre out doctrine as prophecie, and leaue it to all ages for euer.

text.DRB: He appointed to David his servant for to raise up a king of him most strong, and sitting in the throne of honour for ever.
text.KJV: 24:34 Behold that I haue not laboured for my selfe onely, but for all them that seeke wisedome.

text.DRB: Who filleth wisdom as Phison, and as Tigris in the days of new fruits.
Who replenisheth understanding as Euphrates, who multiplieth it as Jordan in the time of harvest.
Who sendeth discipline as the light, and assisting as Gehon in the day of vintage.
Who first hath perfect knowledge of it, & a weaker shall not search it out.
For her cogitation shall abound above the sea, and her counsels above the great depth.
I wisdom have poured out rivers.
I as a sluse of a mighty water out of the river, I as the river Dioryx, & as a water conduit I came out of paradise.
Isaid: I will water my garden of plants, and will inebriate the fruit of my meadow.
And behold my sluse was made aboundant, and my river came near to a sea.
Because I illuminated doctrine to all as the morning light, & I will declare it far.
I will penetrate all the inferior parts of the earth, and will behold all that sleep, and will illuminate all that hope in our Lord.
I will yet pour out doctrine as prophecy, and will leave it to them that seek wisdom, and will not cease unto their progenies even to the holy age.
See ye that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all that seek out the truth.

text.KJV: 25:1 In three things I was beautified, and stoode vp beautiful, both before God and men: the vnitie of brethren, the loue of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Those Who Are Worthy of Praise With three things I am delighted, for they are pleasing to the Lord and to human beings: Harmony among relatives, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband living happily together.
text.NRSV: I take pleasure in three things, and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband who live in harmony.
text.DRB: In three things my spirit is pleased, which are approved before God, and men:

text.KJV: 25:2 Three sorts of men my soule hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poore man that is proud, a rich man that is a lyar, and an olde adulterer that doteth.
text.NABRE: Three kinds of people I hate, and I loathe their manner of life: A proud pauper, a rich liar, and a lecherous old fool.
text.NRSV: I detest three kinds of people... a beggar who is proud, a rich man who is a liar, and an adulterous old man who lacks good sense.
text.DRB: The concord of brethren, and the love of neighbours, and man and wife well agreeing together.
Three sorts my soul hateth, and I am greatly grieved at their life:
text.NABRE: In your youth you did not gather. How will you find anything in your old age?
text.KJV: 25:3 If thou hast gathered nothing in thy youth, how canst thou finde any thing in thine age?

text.DRB: A poor man proud: & a rich man a liar: an old man a fool, and doting.
text.NABRE: How appropriate is sound judgment in the gray-haired, and good counsel in the elderly!
