text.KJV: 33:5 The heart of the foolish is like a cartwheele: and his thoughts are like a rolling axeltree.

text.DRB: Astallion horse neigheth under every one that sitteth upon him, so a friend that is a scorner.
text.NABRE: A mocking friend is like a stallion that neighs, no matter who the rider may be.
text.KJV: 33:6 A stallion horse is as a mocking friend, hee neigheth vnder euery one that sitteth vpon him.
33:7 Why doth one day excell another? when as all the light of euery day in the yeere is of the Sunne.
text.NABRE: Why is one day more important than another, when the same sun lights up every day of the year?
text.NRSV: Why is one day more important than another, when all the daylight in the year is from the sun?
text.DRB: Why doth one day excel another, and one light another, and one year another year of the sun?

text.KJV: 33:8 By the knowledge of the Lord they were distinguished: and he altered seasons and feasts.
text.NABRE: By the Lord ’s knowledge they are kept distinct; and he designates the seasons and feasts.
text.NRSV: By the Lord’s knowledge they were kept distinct, and he appointed the different seasons and festivals.
text.DRB: By the knowledge of our Lord they were separated, the sun being made, and keeping the precept.
And he changed times, and the festival days thereof, and in the same they celebrated the festival days at an hour.
text.NABRE: Some he exalts and sanctifies, and others he lists as ordinary days.
text.KJV: 33:9 Some of them hath hee made high dayes, and hallowed them, and some of them hath hee made ordinary dayes.
33:10 And all men are from the ground, and Adam was created of earth.
text.NABRE: Likewise, all people are of clay, and from earth humankind was formed;
text.NRSV: All human beings come from the ground, and humankind was created out of the dust.
text.DRB: Of them God exalted and magnified, and of them he put into the number of days. And all men are of the ground, and of the earth, from whence Adam was created.

text.KJV: 33:11 In much knowledge the Lord hath diuided them, and made their wayes diuers.
text.NABRE: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them, and he designated their different ways.
text.NRSV: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them and made their ways diverse.
text.DRB: In the multitude of the discipline of our Lord he separated them, and changed their ways.
Of them he blessed, and exalted: and of them he sanctified, and applied to himself: and of them he cursed and humbled, and converted them from their separation.
text.NABRE: Some he blessed and exalted, and some he sanctified and drew to himself. Others he cursed and brought low, and expelled them from their place.
text.KJV: 33:12 Some of them hath hee blessed, and exalted, and some of them hath hee sanctified, and set neere himselfe: but some of them hath hee cursed, and brought low, and turned out of their places.
33:13 As the clay is in the potters hand to fashion it at his pleasure: so man is in the hand of him that made him, to render to them as liketh him best.
text.NABRE: Like clay in the hands of a potter, to be molded according to his pleasure, So are people in the hands of their Maker, to be dealt with as he decides.
text.NRSV: Like clay in the hand of the potter, to be molded just as he pleases, so all are in the hand of their Maker, to be given whatever he decides.
text.DRB: As potter's clay is in his hand, to fashion and dispose it.
All his ways according to his disposition: so man in the hand of him, and he will render to him according to his judgement.
text.NABRE: As evil contrasts with good, and death with life, so are sinners in contrast with the godly.
text.KJV: 33:14 Good is set against euill, and life against death: so is the godly against the sinner, and the sinner against the godly.

text.DRB: Against evil is good, and against death life: so also against a just man a sinner. And so look upon all the works of the Highest. Two against two, and one against one.
text.NABRE: See now all the works of the Most High: they come in pairs, one the opposite of the other.
text.KJV: 33:15 So looke vpon all the workes of the most High, and there are two and two, one against another.

text.DRB: And I awaked last, and as he that gathereth berries after the grape gatherers.
text.NABRE: Now I am the last to keep vigil, like a gleaner following the grape-pickers;
text.KJV: 33:16 I awaked vp last of all, as one that gathereth after the grape-gatherers: by the blessing of the Lord I profited, and filled my wine-presse, like a gatherer of grapes.

text.DRB: In the blessing of God I also have hoped: and as he that gathereth grapes, have I filled the wine press.
text.NABRE: Since by the Lord’s blessing I have made progress till like a grape-picker I have filled my wine press,
text.KJV: 33:17 Consider that I laboured not for my selfe onely, but for all them that seeke learning;

text.DRB: See that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all that seek out discipline.
text.NABRE: Consider that not for myself only have I labored, but for all who seek instruction.
text.KJV: 33:18 Heare me, O ye great men of the people, and hearken with your eares ye rulers of the Congregation:

text.DRB: Hear me ye great men, and all peoples, and ye rulers of the Church harken with your ears.
text.NABRE: Property and Servants Listen to me, leaders of the people; rulers of the congregation, pay heed!
text.KJV: 33:19 Giue not thy sonne, and wife, thy brother and friend power ouer thee while thou liuest, and giue not thy goods to another, lest it repent thee: and thou intreat for the same againe.

text.DRB: To son and wife, brother and friend, give not power over thee in thy life: and give not thy possession to another: lest perhaps thou repent thee, and thou entreat for them.
text.NABRE: Let neither son nor wife, neither brother nor friend, have power over you as long as you live. While breath of life is still in you, let no one take your place. Do not give your wealth to another, lest you must plead for support yourself.
text.KJV: 33:20 As long as thou liuest and hast breath in thee, giue not thy selfe ouer to any.

text.DRB: Whilst thou art yet alive and takest breath, all flesh shall not change thee.
text.NABRE: Let neither son nor wife, neither brother nor friend, have power over you as long as you live. While breath of life is still in you, let no one take your place. Do not give your wealth to another, lest you must plead for support yourself.
text.KJV: 33:21 For better it is that thy children should seeke to thee, then that thou shouldst stand to their courtesie.

text.DRB: For it is better that thy children ask of thee, than that thou look toward the hands of thy children.
text.NABRE: Far better that your children plead with you than that you should look for a handout from them.
text.KJV: 33:22 In all thy workes keepe to thy selfe the preheminence, leaue not a staine in thine honour.

text.DRB: In all thy works be exquisite.
text.NABRE: Keep control over all your affairs; bring no stain on your honor.
text.KJV: 33:23 At the time when thou shalt end thy dayes, and finish thy life, distribute thine inheritance.
33:24 Fodder, a wand, and burdens, are for the asse: and bread, correction, and worke for a seruant.
text.NABRE: When your few days reach their limit, at the time of death distribute your inheritance.
text.NRSV: Fodder and a stick and burdens for a donkey; bread and discipline and work for a slave.
text.DRB: Give no stain to thy glory. In the day of the consummation of the days of thy life, and in the time of thy decease distribute thine inheritance.
Fodder, and wand, and burden for an ass: bread, and discipline, and work for a servant.
text.NABRE: Fodder and whip and loads for a donkey; food, correction and work for a slave.
text.KJV: 33:25 If thou set thy seruant to labour, thou shalt finde rest: but if thou let him goe idle, he shall seeke libertie.

text.DRB: He worketh in discipline, and seeketh to rest: release him his hands, and he seeketh liberty.
text.NABRE: Make a slave work, and he will look for rest; let his hands be idle and he will seek to be free.
text.KJV: 33:26 A yoke and a collar doe bow the necke: so are tortures and torments for an euill seruant.
33:27 Sende him to labour that hee be not idle: for idlenesse teacheth much euill.
text.NABRE: The yoke and harness will bow the neck; and for a wicked slave, punishment in the stocks.
text.NRSV: Put him to work, so that he may not be idle, for idleness teaches much evil.
text.DRB: The yoke and the rein bend a stiff neck, and continual works do bow a servant.
For a malicious servant torment & fetters, send him into work, that he be not idle.
text.NABRE: Force him to work that he be not idle,
text.KJV: 33:28 Set him to worke, as is fit for him; if he be not obedient, put on more heauy fetters.

text.DRB: For idleness hath taught much naughtiness.
text.NABRE: for idleness teaches much mischief.
text.KJV: 33:29 But be not excessiue toward any, and without discretion doe nothing.

text.DRB: Set him to work: for so it becometh him. And if he be not obedient, bow him with fetters, and exceed not over all flesh: but without judgement do no grievous thing.
text.NABRE: Put him to work, as is fitting for him; and if he does not obey, load him with chains. But never lord it over any human being, and do nothing unjust.
text.KJV: 33:30 If thou haue a seruant, let him bee vnto thee as thy selfe, because thou hast bought him with a price.

text.DRB: If thou have a faithful servant, let him be unto thee as thy soul: as a brother so entreat him: because in the blood of thy soul thou hast gotten him.
text.NABRE: If you have but one slave, treat him like yourself, for you have acquired him with your life’s blood; If you have but one slave, deal with him as a brother, for you need him as you need your life.
text.KJV: 33:31 If thou haue a seruant, intreate him as a brother: for thou hast neede of him, as of thine owne soule: if thou intreate him euill, and he runne from thee, which way wilt thou goe to seeke him?

text.DRB: If thou hurt him unjustly, he will run away:
text.NABRE: If you mistreat him and he runs away,

text.DRB: if rising up he depart, thou knowest not whom to ask, and what way to seek him.
text.NABRE: in what direction will you look for him?

text.KJV: 34:1 The hopes of a man voyd of vnderstanding are vaine, and false: and dreames lift vp fooles.
text.NABRE: Chapter 34 Trust in the Lord and Not in Dreams Empty and false are the hopes of the senseless, and dreams give wings to fools.
text.NRSV: The senseless have vain and false hopes, and dreams give wings to fools.
text.DRB: Vain hope & lying is to a foolish man: & dreams extol the unwise.

text.KJV: 34:2 Who so regardeth dreames, is like him that catcheth at a shadow, and followeth after the winde.
text.NABRE: Like one grasping at shadows or chasing the wind, so anyone who believes in dreams.
text.NRSV: As one who catches at a shadow and pursues the wind, so is anyone who believes in dreams.
text.DRB: As he that apprehendeth a shadow, and pursueth the wind: so is he also that attendeth to lying visions.
According to this is the vision of dreams: as a man's similitude before the face of a man.
text.NABRE: What is seen in dreams is a reflection, the likeness of a face looking at itself.
text.KJV: 34:3 The vision of dreames is the resemblance of one thing to another, euen as the likenesse of a face to a face.

text.DRB: Of the unclean what shall be made clean? And of a liar what truth shall be said?
text.NABRE: How can the unclean produce what is clean? How can the false produce what is true?
text.KJV: 34:4 Of an vncleane thing, what can be cleansed? and from that thing which is false, what trueth can come?

text.DRB: Divination of error, and lying soothsayings, and the dreams of them that do evil, are vanity.
text.NABRE: Divination, omens, and dreams are unreal; what you already expect, the mind fantasizes.
text.KJV: 34:5 Diuinations, and soothsayings, and dreames are vaine: and the heart fancieth as a womans heart in trauell.

text.DRB: And as a woman that travaileth, thy heart suffereth phantasies: unless it be a vision sent forth from the Highest, set not thy heart upon them.
text.NABRE: Unless they are specially sent by the Most High, do not fix your heart on them.
text.KJV: 34:6 If they be not sent from the most high in thy visitation, set not thy heart vpon them.

text.DRB: For dreams have made many to err, and they that hoped in them have failed.
text.NABRE: For dreams have led many astray, and those who put their hope in them have perished.
text.KJV: 34:7 For dreames haue deceiued many, and they haue failed that put their trust in them.

text.DRB: The word of the law shall be fulfilled without lying, and wisdom in the mouth of the faithful shall be made plain.
text.NABRE: Without such deceptions the Law will be fulfilled, and in the mouth of the faithful is complete wisdom.
text.KJV: 34:8 The Law shall be found perfect without lies: and wisedome is perfection to a faithfull mouth.

text.DRB: He that hath not been proved, what knoweth he? A man expert in many things, shall think many things: and he that hath learned many things, shall declare understanding.
text.NABRE: A much-traveled person knows many things; and one with much experience speaks sense.
text.KJV: 34:9 A man that hath trauailed knoweth many things: and hee that hath much experience, wil declare wisedome.

text.DRB: He that is not tried, knoweth few things: and he that hath been in many things, multiplieth wickedness.
text.NABRE: An inexperienced person knows little,
text.KJV: 34:10 He that hath no experience, knoweth little: but he that hath trauailed, is full of prudence.

text.DRB: He that hath not been proved, what manner of things knoweth he? He that is deceived, shall abound with wickedness.
text.NABRE: whereas with travel one adds to resourcefulness.
text.KJV: 34:11 When I trauailed, I saw many things: and I vnderstand more, then I can expresse.

text.DRB: I have seen many things in wandering to and fro, and very many fashions of words.
text.NABRE: I have seen much in my travels, and learned more than I could ever say.
text.KJV: 34:12 I was oft times in danger of death, yet I was deliuered because of these things.

text.DRB: Sometimes I have been in danger unto death for these things, and I was delivered by the grace of God.
text.NABRE: Often I was in danger of death, but by these experiences I was saved.
text.KJV: 34:13 The spirit of those that feare the Lord shall liue, for their hope is in him that saueth them.
34:14 Who so feareth the Lord, shall not feare nor be afraid, for hee is his hope.
text.NABRE: Living is the spirit of those who fear the Lord,
text.NRSV: The one who fears the Lord will not be timid, or play the coward, for the Lord is his hope.
text.DRB: The spirit of them that fear God is sought, & at his sight shall be blessed.

text.KJV: 34:15 Blessed is the soule of him that feareth the Lord: to whom doeth hee looke? and who is his strength?
text.NABRE: for their hope is in their savior.
text.NRSV: Happy is the soul of the one who fears the Lord! To whom does he look? And who is his support?
text.DRB: For their hope is on him that saveth them, and the eyes of God upon them that love him.
He that feareth our Lord shall tremble at nothing, and shall not dread: because he is his hope.
text.NABRE: Whoever fear the Lord are afraid of nothing and are never discouraged, for he is their hope.
text.KJV: 34:16 For the eyes of the Lord are vpon them that loue him, he is their mightie protection, and strong stay, a defence from heat, and a couer from the Sunne at noone, a preseruation from stumbling, and a helpe from falling.

text.DRB: His soul is blessed that feareth our Lord.
text.NABRE: Happy the soul that fears the Lord!
text.KJV: 34:17 He raiseth vp the soule, and lighteneth the eyes: hee giueth health, life, and blessing.
34:18 Hee that sacrificeth of a thing wrongfully gotten, his offering is ridiculous, and the giftes of vniust men are not accepted.
text.NABRE: In whom does he trust, and who is his support?
text.NRSV: If one sacrifices from what has been wrongfully acquired, the offering is blemished; the gifts of the lawless are not acceptable.
text.DRB: To whom doth he look, and who is his strength?

text.KJV: 34:19 The most high is not pleased with the offerings of the wicked, neither is he pacified for sinne by the multitude of sacrifices.
text.NABRE: The eyes of the Lord are upon those who love him; he is their mighty shield and strong support, A shelter from the heat, a shade from the noonday sun, a guard against stumbling, a help against falling.
text.NRSV: The Most High is not pleased with the offerings of the ungodly, nor for a multitude of sacrifices does he forgive sins.
text.DRB: The eyes of our Lord are upon them that fear him, a protector of might, a stay of strength, a cover from the heat, and shadow for the noon time,
a saving from offence, and help from falling, exalting the soul, and illuminating the eyes, giving health, and life, and blessing.
text.NABRE: He lifts up spirits, brings a sparkle to the eyes, gives health and life and blessing.
text.KJV: 34:20 Who so bringeth an offering of the goods of the poore, doeth as one that killeth the sonne before his fathers eyes.

text.DRB: The oblation of him that immolateth of an unjust thing is spotted, and the scornings of the unjust are not acceptable.
text.NABRE: True Worship of God Ill-gotten goods offered in sacrifice are tainted.
text.KJV: 34:21 The bread of the needie, is their life: he that defraudeth him thereof, is a man of blood.

text.DRB: Our Lord is only theirs that expect him in the way of truth and justice.
text.NABRE: Presents from the lawless do not win God’s favor.
text.KJV: 34:22 Hee that taketh away his neighbours liuing, slayeth him: and hee that defraudeth the labourer of his hire, is a bloodshedder.

text.DRB: The Highest alloweth not the gifts of the wicked: neither hath he regard to the oblations of the unjust, neither will he be made propitious for sins by the multitude of their sacrifices.
text.NABRE: The Most High is not pleased with the gifts of the godless, nor for their many sacrifices does he forgive their sins.
text.KJV: 34:23 When one buildeth, and another pulleth downe, what profite haue they then but labour?

text.DRB: He that offereth sacrifice of poor men's substance, is as he that sacrificeth the son in the presence of his father.
text.NABRE: One who slays a son in his father’s presence— whoever offers sacrifice from the holdings of the poor.
text.KJV: 34:24 When one prayeth, and another curseth, whose voice will the Lorde heare?
34:25 He that washeth himselfe after the touching of a dead body, if he touch it againe, what auaileth his washing?
text.NABRE: The bread of charity is life itself for the needy; whoever withholds it is a murderer.
text.NRSV: If one washes after touching a corpse, and touches it again, what has been gained by washing?
text.DRB: The bread of the needy, is the life of the poor: he that defraudeth it, is a man of blood.

text.KJV: 34:26 So is it with a man that fasteth for his sinnes, and goeth againe and doeth the same: who will heare his prayer, or what doeth his humbling profit him?
text.NABRE: To take away a neighbor’s living is to commit murder;
text.NRSV: So if a person fasts for his sins, and goes again and does the same things, who will listen to his prayer? And what has he gained by humbling himself?
text.DRB: He that taketh away bread in sweat, is as he that killeth his neighbour.
He that sheddeth blood, and that defraudeth the hired man, are brethren.
text.NABRE: to deny a laborer wages is to shed blood.

text.DRB: One building, and another destroying: what profit have they but the labour?
text.NABRE: If one builds up and another tears down, what do they gain but trouble?

text.DRB: One praying, and another cursing: whether's voice will God hear?
text.NABRE: If one prays and another curses, whose voice will God hear?

text.DRB: He that is washed from the dead, and toucheth him again, what doth his washing profit?
text.NABRE: If one again touches a corpse after bathing, what does he gain by the purification?

text.DRB: so a man that fasteth in his sins: and doing the same again, what doth he profit in humbling himself? Who will hear his prayer?
text.NABRE: So one who fasts for sins, but goes and commits them again: Who will hear his prayer, what is gained by mortification?

text.KJV: 35:1 Hee that keepeth the law, bringeth offerings enow: he that taketh heed to the commandement, offereth a peace offering.
text.NABRE: Chapter 35 To keep the law is to make many offerings;
text.NRSV: The one who keeps the law makes many offerings; one who heeds the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.
text.DRB: He that keepeth the law, multiplieth oblation.

text.KJV: 35:2 He that requiteth a good turne, offereth fine floure: and he that giueth almes, sacrificeth praise.
text.NABRE: whoever observes the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.
text.NRSV: The one who returns a kindness offers choice flour, and one who gives alms sacrifices a thank offering.
text.DRB: It is an wholesome sacrifice to attend to the commandments, and to depart from all iniquity.

text.KJV: 35:3 To depart from wickednesse is a thing pleasing to the Lord: and to forsake vnrighteousnesse, is a propitiation.
text.NABRE: By works of charity one offers fine flour,
text.NRSV: To keep from wickedness is pleasing to the Lord, and to forsake unrighteousness is an atonement.
text.DRB: And to offer propitiation by sacrifice for injustices, and to retire from injustice is prayer for sins.

text.KJV: 35:4 Thou shalt not appeare emptie before the Lord:
text.NABRE: and one who gives alms presents a sacrifice of praise.
text.NRSV: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,
text.DRB: He who offereth the flour of wheat, shall give thanks: and he that is merciful offereth sacrifice.

text.KJV: 35:5 For all these things  because of the commandement.
text.NABRE: To refrain from evil pleases the Lord, and to avoid injustice is atonement.
text.NRSV: for all these things are to be done because of the commandment.
text.DRB: To depart from iniquity is a thing that pleaseth our Lord well, and to depart from injustice is an entreating for sins.
Thou shalt not appear before the sight of our Lord empty.
text.NABRE: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,
text.KJV: 35:6 The offering of the righteous maketh the Altar fat, and the sweete sauour thereof is before the most high.

text.DRB: For all these things are done because of the commandment of God.
text.NABRE: for all that you offer is in fulfillment of the precepts.
text.KJV: 35:7 The sacrifice of a iust man is acceptable, and the memoriall thereof shall neuer be forgotten.

text.DRB: The oblation of the just maketh a fat altar, and is an odour of sweetness in the sight of the Highest.
text.NABRE: The offering of the just enriches the altar: a sweet odor before the Most High.
text.KJV: 35:8 Giue the Lord his honour with a good eye, and diminish not the first fruits of thine hands.

text.DRB: The sacrifice of the just is acceptable, and our Lord will not forget the memory thereof.
text.NABRE: The sacrifice of the just is accepted, never to be forgotten.
text.KJV: 35:9 In all thy gifts shew a cheerefull countenance, and dedicate thy tithes with gladnesse.
35:10 Giue vnto the most high, according as hee hath enriched thee, and as thou hast gotten, giue with a cheerefull eye.
text.NABRE: With a generous spirit pay homage to the Lord, and do not spare your freewill gifts.
text.NRSV: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, and as generously as you can afford.
text.DRB: Render glory to God with a good mind: and diminish not the first fruits of thine hands.

text.KJV: 35:11 For the Lord recompenseth, and will giue thee seuen times as much.
text.NABRE: With each contribution show a cheerful countenance, and pay your tithes in a spirit of joy.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is the one who repays, and he will repay you sevenfold.
text.DRB: In every gift make thy countenance cheerful, and in joyfulness sanctify thy tithes.
Give to the Highest according to his gift, and with a good eye do according to the ability of thine hands:
text.NABRE: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, generously, according to your means.
text.KJV: 35:12 Doe not thinke to corrupt with gifts, for such he will not receiue: and trust not to vnrighteous sacrifices, for the Lord is iudge, and with him is no respect of persons.

text.DRB: because our Lord is a rewarder, and will repay thee seven times so much.
text.NABRE: For he is a God who always repays and will give back to you sevenfold.
text.KJV: 35:13 Hee will not accept any person against a poore man: but will heare the prayer of the oppressed.

text.DRB: Offer not wicked gifts, for he will not receive them.
text.NABRE: But offer no bribes; these he does not accept!
text.KJV: 35:14 He will not despise the supplication of the fatherlesse: nor the widowe when she powreth out her complaint.
35:15 Doeth not the teares run downe the widowes cheeks? and is not her crie against him that causeth them to fall?
text.NABRE: Do not trust in sacrifice of the fruits of extortion, For he is a God of justice, who shows no partiality.
text.NRSV: Do not the tears of the widow run down her cheek as she cries out against the one who causes them to fall?
text.DRB: And look not upon an unjust sacrifice, because our Lord is judge, and there is not with him the glory of person.
Our Lord will not accept person against the poor, and he will hear the prayer of him that is hurt.
text.NABRE: He shows no partiality to the weak but hears the grievance of the oppressed.
text.KJV: 35:16 He that serueth the Lord, shall be accepted with fauour, and his prayer shall reach vnto the cloudes.
35:17 The prayer of the humble pierceth the clouds: and till it come nigh he will not be comforted: and will not depart till the most High shall beholde to iudge righteously, and execute iudgement.
text.NABRE: He does not forsake the cry of the orphan, nor the widow when she pours out her complaint.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the humble pierces the clouds, and it will not rest until it reaches its goal.
text.DRB: He will not despise the prayers of the pupil: nor the widow, if she pour out speech of mourning.
Do not the widow's tears run down the cheek, & her exclamation upon him that causeth them to run?
text.NABRE: Do not the tears that stream down her cheek
text.KJV: 35:18 For the Lord wil not be slacke, neither will the mightie be patient towards them, till he hath smitten in sunder the loines of the vnmercifull, and repaid vengeance to the heathen: till he haue taken away the multitude of the proud, and broken the scepter of the vnrighteous:

text.DRB: For from the cheek they go up even to heaven, and our Lord the hearer will not be delighted in them.
text.NABRE: cry out against the one that causes them to fall?
text.KJV: 35:19 Till he haue rendred to euery man according to his deeds, and to the works of men according to their deuises, till he haue iudged the cause of his people: and made them to reioyce in his mercie,

text.DRB: He that adoreth God in delectation, shall be received, & his petition shall approach even to the clouds.
text.NABRE: Those who serve God to please him are accepted; their petition reaches the clouds.
text.KJV: 35:20 Mercie is seasonable in the time of affliction, as cloudes of raine in the time of drought.

text.DRB: The prayer of him that humbleth himself, shall penetrate the clouds: and till it approach he will not be comforted: and he will not depart till the Highest behold.
text.NABRE: The prayer of the lowly pierces the clouds; it does not rest till it reaches its goal; Nor will it withdraw till the Most High responds,

text.DRB: And our Lord will not be long, but will judge the just, and will do judgement: and the strongest will not have patience in them, that he may crush their back:
text.NABRE: judges justly and affirms the right. God indeed will not delay, and like a warrior, will not be still Till he breaks the backs of the merciless

text.DRB: and he will repay vengeance to the Gentiles, till he take away the multitude of the proud: & break the sceptres of the unjust,
text.NABRE: and wreaks vengeance upon the nations; Till he destroys the scepter of the proud, and cuts off the staff of the wicked;

text.DRB: till he reward men according to their doings: and according to the works of Adam, and according to his presumption.
text.NABRE: Till he requites everyone according to their deeds, and repays them according to their thoughts;

text.DRB: till he judge the judgement of his people, and shall delight the just with his mercy:
text.NABRE: Till he defends the cause of his people, and makes them glad by his salvation.

text.DRB: The mercy of God is beautiful in the time of tribulation, as a cloud of rain in the time of drought.
text.NABRE: Welcome is his mercy in time of distress as rain clouds in time of drought.

text.KJV: 36:1 Haue mercie vpon vs, O Lord God of all, and behold vs:
text.NABRE: Chapter 36 A Prayer for God’s People Come to our aid, O God of the universe,
text.NRSV: Have mercy upon us, O Lord, the God of all, and look upon us,
text.DRB: Have mercy upon us o God of all, and respect us, and shew us the light of thy mercies:

text.KJV: 36:2 And send thy feare vpon all the nations that seeke not after thee.
text.NABRE: and put all the nations in dread of you!
text.NRSV: and put the fear of you upon all the nations.
text.DRB: and send in thy fear upon the nations, that have not sought after thee, that they may know that there is no God but thou, and that they may shew forth thy glorious things.
Lift up thy hand over the strange Nations, that they may see thy might.
text.NABRE: Raise your hand against the foreign people, that they may see your mighty deeds.
text.KJV: 36:3 Lift vp thy hand against the strange nations, and let them see thy power.

text.DRB: For as in their sight thou art sanctified in us, so in our sight thou shalt be magnified in them,
text.NABRE: As you have used us to show them your holiness, so now use them to show us your glory.
text.KJV: 36:4 As thou wast sanctified in vs before them: so be thou magnified among them before vs.

text.DRB: that they may know thee, as we also have known, that there is no God beside thee o Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus they will know, as we know, that there is no God but you.
text.KJV: 36:5 And let them know thee, as we haue knowen thee, that there is no God, but onely thou, O God.

text.DRB: Renew signs, and change marvels.
text.NABRE: Give new signs and work new wonders;
text.KJV: 36:6 Shew new signes, and make other strange wonders: glorifie thy hand and thy right arme, that they may set forth thy wonderous workes.

text.DRB: Glorify thy hand, and thy right arm.
text.NABRE: show forth the splendor of your right hand and arm.
text.KJV: 36:7 Raise vp indignation, and powre out wrath: take away the aduersarie and destroy the enemie.

text.DRB: Raise up fury, and pour out wrath.
text.NABRE: Rouse your anger, pour out wrath;
text.KJV: 36:8 Make the time short, remember the couenant, and let them declare thy wonderfull works.

text.DRB: Take away the adversary, and afflict the enemy.
text.NABRE: humble the enemy, scatter the foe.
text.KJV: 36:9 Let him that escapeth, be consumed by the rage of the fire, and let them perish that oppresse the people.

text.DRB: Hasten the time, and remember the end, that they may declare thy marvels.
text.NABRE: Hasten the ending, appoint the time, and let people proclaim your mighty deeds.
text.KJV: 36:10 Smite in sunder the heads of the rulers of the heathen, that say, There is none other but we.

text.DRB: Let him that is saved be devoured in the wrath of flame: and let them that evil entreat thy people, find perdition.
text.NABRE: Let raging fire consume the fugitive, and your people’s oppressors meet destruction.
text.KJV: 36:11 Gather all the tribes of Iacob together, and inherit thou them, as from the beginning.

text.DRB: Break the head of Princes of the enemies, that say: There is none other beside us.
text.NABRE: Crush the heads of the hostile rulers who say, “There is no one besides me.”
text.KJV: 36:12 O Lord haue mercie vpon the people, that is called by thy name, and vpon Israel, whom thou hast named thy first borne.

text.DRB: Gather together all the tribes of Jacob: and let them know that there is no God but thou, that they may declare thy great works: & thou shalt inherit them as from the beginning.
text.NABRE: Gather all the tribes of Jacob,
text.KJV: 36:13 O bee mercifull vnto Ierusalem thy holy citie, the place of thy rest.

text.DRB: Have mercy on thy people, upon which thy name is invocated: and upon Israel, whom thou hast made equal to thy first begotten.
text.KJV: 36:14 Fill Sion with thine vnspeakable oracles, and thy people with thy glory.

text.DRB: Have mercy on the city of thy sanctification Jerusalem, the city of thy rest.
text.KJV: 36:15 Giue testimonie vnto those that thou hast possessed from the beginning, and raise vp prophets that haue bin in thy name.
36:16 Reward them that wait for thee, and let thy prophets be found faithfull.
text.NRSV: Reward those who wait for you, and let your prophets be found trustworthy.
text.DRB: Replenish Sion with thy words that cannot be uttered, & thy people with thy glory.
Give the testimony to them that are thy creatures from the beginning, and raise up the prophecies, which the former prophets spake in thy name.
text.KJV: 36:17 O Lord heare the prayer of thy seruants, according to the blessing of Aaron ouer thy people, that all they which dwel vpon the earth, may know that thou art the Lord, the eternall God.

text.DRB: Give reward to them that patiently expect thee, that thy prophets may be found faithful: and hear the prayers of thy servants,
text.KJV: 36:18 The belly deuoureth all meates, yet is one meat better then another.

text.DRB: according to Aaron's benediction of thy people, and direct us into the way of justice, and let all know that inhabit the earth, that thou art God the beholder of the worlds.
text.KJV: 36:19 As the palate tasteth diuers kinds of venison: so doth an heart of vnderstanding false speeches.

text.DRB: The belly will eat all meat, and one meat is better than another meat.
text.KJV: 36:20 A froward heart causeth heauinesse: but a man of experience will recompense him.
36:21 A woman will receiue euery man, yet is one daughter better then another
text.NRSV: A woman will accept any man, but one girl is better than another.
text.DRB: The jaws taste venison, & the wise heart lying words,

text.KJV: 36:22 The beautie of a woman cheareth the countenance, and a man loueth nothing better.
text.NRSV: A woman’s beauty gladdens the countenance, and a man desires nothing more.
text.DRB: Aperverse heart will give sorrow, and a cunning man will resist it.
Some woman will receive every man: and one daughter is better than another daughter.
text.KJV: 36:23 If there be kindnesse, meekenes, and comfort in her tongue, then is not her husband like other men.
36:24 He that getteth a wife, beginneth a possession, a helpe like vnto himselfe, and a pillar of rest.
text.NRSV: The man who acquires a wife gets his best possession, a helper fit for him and a pillar of support.
text.DRB: The beauty of a woman cheereth the face of her husband, and increaseth the desire above all man's concupiscence.

text.KJV: 36:25 Where no hedge is, there the possession is spoiled: and he that hath no wife will wander vp and downe mourning.
text.NRSV: Where there is no fence, the property will be plundered; and where there is no wife, a man will become a fugitive and a wanderer.
text.DRB: If there be a tongue of curing, there is also a mitigating and of mercy: her husband is not according to the sons of men.
He that possesseth a good woman beginneth riches: she is an help like unto him, & a pillar as rest.
text.KJV: 36:26 Who will trust a thiefe well appointed, that skippeth from citie to citie? so  a man that hath no house? and lodgeth wheresoeuer the night taketh him?

text.DRB: Where there is no hedge, the possession shall be spoiled: and where there is no wife, he mourneth wanting.
Who doth credit him that hath no nest, and turning aside wheresoever it waxeth dark, as a robber girded, leaping from city to city.

text.KJV: 37:1 Euery friend saieth, I am his friend also: but there is a friend which is onely a friend in name.
text.NABRE: Chapter 37 Every friend declares friendship, but there are friends who are friends in name only.
text.NRSV: Every friend says, “I too am a friend”; but some friends are friends only in name.
text.DRB: Every friend will say: I also have joined friendship: but there is a friend, in name only a friend. Doth there not sorrow remain even to death?

text.KJV: 37:2 Is it not a griefe vnto death, when a companion and friend is turned to an enemie?
text.NABRE: Is it not a sorrow unto death when your other self becomes your enemy?
text.NRSV: Is it not a sorrow like that for death itself when a dear friend turns into an enemy?
text.DRB: But a companion and friend will be turned to enmity.
O most wicked presumption, whence wast thou created to cover the dry land with malice, and with the deceitfulness thereof?
text.NABRE: “Alas, my companion! Why were you created to fill the earth with deceit?”
text.KJV: 37:3 O wicked imagination, whence camest thou in to couer the earth with deceit?

text.DRB: Acompanion is pleasant with his friend in delectations, and in the time of tribulation he will be an adversary.
text.NABRE: A harmful friend will look to your table, but in time of trouble he stands aloof.
text.KJV: 37:4 There is a companion, which reioyceth in the prosperity of a friend: but in the time of trouble will be against him.

text.DRB: Acompanion is sorry with his friend for his belly's sake, and he will take a shield against the enemy.
text.NABRE: A good friend will fight with you against the foe, and against your enemies he will hold up your shield.
text.KJV: 37:5 There is a companion which helpeth his friend for the belly, and taketh vp the buckler against the enemie.

text.DRB: Forget not thy friend in thy mind, and be not unmindful of him in thy riches.
text.NABRE: Do not forget your comrade during the battle, and do not neglect him when you distribute your spoils.
text.KJV: 37:6 Forget not thy friend in thy minde, and be not vnmindfull of him in thy riches.

text.DRB: Consult not with him which betrayeth, and hide thy counsel from them that envy thee.
text.NABRE: Every counselor points out a way, but some counsel ways of their own.
text.KJV: 37:7 Euery counseller extolleth counsell; but there is some that counselleth for himselfe.

text.DRB: Every counseller uttereth counsel, but there is a counseller in himself.
text.NABRE: Watch out when one offers advice; find out first of all what he wants. For he also may be thinking of himself— Why should the opportunity fall to him?
text.KJV: 37:8 Beware of a counseller, and know before what neede he hath (for he will counsell for himselfe) lest hee cast the lot vpon thee:

text.DRB: From such a counseller keep thy soul. First know what his necessity is: for he will devise to his own mind:
text.NABRE: He may tell you how good your way will be, and then stand by to see you impoverished.
text.KJV: 37:9 And say vnto thee, Thy way is good: and afterward he stand on the other side, to see what shall befall thee.

text.DRB: lest perhaps he thrust a sharp stake into the ground and say to thee:
text.NABRE: Seek no advice from your father-in-law, and from one who is envious of you, keep your intentions hidden.
text.KJV: 37:10 Consult not with one that suspecteth thee: and hide thy counsell from such as enuie thee.

text.DRB: Thy way is good; and stand over against thee to see what will befall thee.
text.NABRE: Seek no advice from a woman about her rival, from a coward about war, from a merchant about business, from a buyer about value, from a miser about generosity, from a cruel person about well-being, from a worthless worker about his work, from a seasonal laborer about the harvest, from an idle slave about a great task— pay no attention to any advice they give.
text.KJV: 37:11 Neither consult with a woman touching her of whom she is iealous; neither with a coward in matters of warre, nor with a merchant concerning exchange; nor with a buyer of selling; nor with an enuious man of thankfulnesse; nor with an vnmercifull man touching kindnesse; nor with the slouthfull for any worke; nor with an hireling for a yeere, of finishing worke; nor with an idle seruant of much businesse: Hearken not vnto these in any matter of counsell.
37:12 But be continually with a godly man, whom thou knowest to keepe the commandements of the Lord, whose minde is according to thy minde, and will sorrow with thee, if thou shalt miscarry.
text.NABRE: Instead, associate with a religious person, who you know keeps the commandments; Who is like-minded with yourself and will grieve for you if you fall.
text.NRSV: But stay constantly with a God-fearing person whom you know to keep the commandments,
text.DRB: With an irreligious man treat not of holiness, & with the unjust of justice, and with a woman of the thing whereof she is jealous: with a fearful man of war, with a merchant of traffic, with a buyer of selling, with an envious man of giving thanks,

text.KJV: 37:13 And let the counsell of thine owne heart stand: for there is no man more faithfull vnto thee then it.
text.NABRE: Then, too, heed your own heart’s counsel; for there is nothing you can depend on more.
text.NRSV: And heed the counsel of your own heart, for no one is more faithful to you than it is.
text.DRB: with the impious of piety, with the unhonest of honesty, with the field labourer of all work,

text.KJV: 37:14 For a mans minde is sometime wont to tell him more then seuen watchmen, that sit aboue in an high towre.
text.NABRE: The heart can reveal your situation better than seven sentinels on a tower.
text.NRSV: For a person’s soul sometimes keeps him better informed than seven sentinels sitting high on a watchtower.
text.DRB: with him that worketh by the year of the ending of the year, with a slothful servant of much working: attend not to these in all counsel.
But be continual with a holy man, whomsoever thou shalt know to observe the fear of God,
text.NABRE: Then with all this, pray to God to make your steps firm in the true path.
text.KJV: 37:15 And aboue all this pray to the most high, that he will direct thy way in trueth.

text.DRB: whose soul is according to thine own soul: and who when thou shalt stumble in the dark, will be sorry for thee.
text.NABRE: Wisdom and Temperance A word is the source of every deed; a thought, of every act.
text.KJV: 37:16 Let reason goe before euery enterprise, & counsell before euery action.

text.DRB: And establish with thyself an heart of good counsel: for there is none other thing more worth to thee than it.
text.NABRE: The root of all conduct is the heart;
text.KJV: 37:17 The countenance is a signe of changing of the heart.

text.DRB: The soul of a holy man uttereth sometime true things, more than seven watchmen that sit in a high place to watch.
text.NABRE: four branches it shoots forth: Good and evil, death and life, and their absolute mistress is the tongue.
text.KJV: 37:18 Foure maner of things appeare: good and euill, life and death: but the tongue ruleth ouer them continually.

text.DRB: And in all these beseech the Highest, that he direct thy way in truth.
text.NABRE: One may be wise and benefit many, yet appear foolish to himself.
text.KJV: 37:19 There is one that is wise and teacheth many, and yet is vnprofitable to himselfe.

text.DRB: Before all works let a true word go before thee, and stable counsel before every act.
text.NABRE: One may be wise, but if his words are rejected, he will be deprived of all enjoyment.
text.KJV: 37:20 There is one that sheweth wisedome in words, and is hated: he shall be destitute of all foode.

text.DRB: Awicked word shall change the heart: out of which, rise four parts, good, and evil, life, and death: and the tongue is a continual ruler of them. There is a subtile man teacher of many, & to his own soul he is unprofitable.
text.KJV: 37:21 For grace is not giuen him from the Lord: because he is depriued of all wisedome.

text.DRB: Acunning man hath taught many, and is sweet to his own soul.
text.KJV: 37:22 Another is wise to himselfe: and the fruits of vnderstanding are commendable in his mouth.

text.DRB: He that speaketh sophistically, is odious: in every thing he shall be defrauded.
text.KJV: 37:23 A wise man instructeth his people, and the fruits of his vnderstanding faile not.

text.DRB: Grace is not given him of our Lord: for he is defrauded of all wisdom.
text.KJV: 37:24 A wise man shall be filled with blessing, and all they that see him, shall count him happy.

text.DRB: There is a wise man, wise to his own soul: and the fruit of his understanding is laudable.
text.KJV: 37:25 The daies of the life of man may be numbred: but the daies of Israel are innumerable.

text.DRB: Awise man teacheth his people, and the fruits of his understanding are faithful.
text.KJV: 37:26 A wise man shall inherite glory among his people, and his name shalbe perpetuall.
37:27 My sonne prooue thy soule in thy life, and see what is euill for it, and giue not that vnto it.
text.NRSV: My child, test your soul while you live; see what is bad for it and do not give it that.
text.DRB: Awise man shall be filled with blessings, and they that see will praise him.

text.KJV: 37:28 For all things are not profitable for all men, neither hath euery soule pleasure in euery thing.
text.NRSV: For not everything is good for everyone, and no one enjoys everything.
text.DRB: The life of a man is in the number of days: but the days of Israel are innumerable.
Awise man in the people shall inherit honour, and his name shall live for ever.
text.KJV: 37:29 Be not vnsatiable in any dainty thing: nor too greedy vpon meates.
37:30 For excesse of meates, bringeth sicknesse, and surfetting will turne into choler.
text.NRSV: For overeating brings sickness, and gluttony leads to nausea.
text.DRB: Son in thy life prove thy soul: & if it be wicked, give it not power:
for all things are not expedient for all, and every kind pleaseth not every soul.
text.KJV: 37:31 By surfetting haue many perished, but hee that taketh heed, prolongeth his life.

text.DRB: Be not greedy in all feasting, and pour not out thyself upon all meat:
for in many meats there shall be infirmity, and greediness shall approach even to choler.
Because of surfeit many have died: but he that is abstinent, shall add life.

text.KJV: 38:1 Honour a Phisitian with the honour due vnto him, for the vses which you may haue of him: for the Lord hath created him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 38 Sickness and Death Make friends with the doctor, for he is essential to you; God has also established him in his profession.
text.NRSV: Honor physicians for their services, for the Lord created them;
text.DRB: Honour the physician for necessity: for the Highest hath created him.

text.KJV: 38:2 For of the most High commeth healing, and he shall receiue honour of the King.
text.NABRE: From God the doctor has wisdom, and from the king he receives sustenance.
text.NRSV: for their gift of healing comes from the Most High, and they are rewarded by the king.
text.DRB: For all medicine is of God, & it shall receive gift of the king.
The knowledge of the physician shall exalt his head, and in the sight of great men he shall be praised.
text.NABRE: Knowledge makes the doctor distinguished, and gives access to those in authority.
text.KJV: 38:3 The skill of the Phisitian shall lift vp his head: and in the sight of great men he shalbe in admiration.
38:4 The Lord hath created medicines out of the earth; and he that is wise will not abhorre them.
text.NABRE: God makes the earth yield healing herbs which the prudent should not neglect;
text.NRSV: The Lord created medicines out of the earth, and the sensible will not despise them.
text.DRB: The Highest hath created medicines of the earth, and a wise man will not abhor them.
Was not bitter water made sweet by wood?
text.NABRE: Was not the water sweetened by a twig, so that all might learn his power?
text.KJV: 38:5 Was not the water made sweet with wood, that the vertue thereof might be knowen?

text.DRB: The virtue of these things is come to the knowledge of men, and the Highest hath given knowledge to men, for to be honoured in his marvelous things.
text.NABRE: He endows people with knowledge, to glory in his mighty works,
text.KJV: 38:6 And he hath giuen men skill, that hee might be honoured in his marueilous workes.

text.DRB: Curing with these things he shall mitigate pain, and the apothecary shall make confections of sweetness, and shall make ointments of health, and his works shall not be consummated.
text.NABRE: Through which the doctor eases pain,
text.KJV: 38:7 With such doeth he heale  and taketh away their paines.

text.DRB: For the peace of God is upon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: and the druggist prepares his medicines. Thus God’s work continues without cease in its efficacy on the surface of the earth.
text.KJV: 38:8 Of such doeth the Apothecarie make a confection; and of his workes there is no end, and from him is peace ouer all the earth.
38:9 My sonne, in thy sickenesse be not negligent: but pray vnto the Lord, and he will make thee whole.
text.NABRE: My son, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to God, for it is he who heals.
text.NRSV: My child, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to the Lord, and he will heal you.
text.DRB: Son in thine infirmity contemn not thyself, but pray our Lord, and he will cure thee.

text.KJV: 38:10 Leaue off from sinne, and order thy hands aright, and cleanse thy heart from all wickednesse.
text.NABRE: Flee wickedness and purify your hands; cleanse your heart of every sin.
text.NRSV: Give up your faults and direct your hands rightly, and cleanse your heart from all sin.
text.DRB: Turn away from sin, and direct thy hands, and from all offence cleanse thy heart.
Give sweetness and a memorial of fine flour, and make a fat oblation, and give place to the physician.
text.NABRE: Offer your sweet-smelling oblation and memorial, a generous offering according to your means.
text.KJV: 38:11 Giue a sweet sauour, and a memoriall of fine flowre: and make a fat offering, as not being.
38:12 Then giue place to the phisitian, for the Lord hath created him: let him not go from thee, for thou hast need of him.
text.NABRE: Then give the doctor his place lest he leave; you need him too,
text.NRSV: Then give the physician his place, for the Lord created him; and do not let him leave you, for you need him.
text.DRB: For our Lord created him: and let him not depart from thee, because his works be necessary.
For there is a time when thou mayst fall into their hands:
text.NABRE: For there are times when recovery is in his hands.
text.KJV: 38:13 There is a time when in their hands there is good successe.

text.DRB: and they shall beseech our Lord, that he direct their rest, and healing; for their conversation.
text.NABRE: He too prays to God That his diagnosis may be correct and his treatment bring about a cure.
text.KJV: 38:14 For they shall also pray vnto the Lord, that hee would prosper that, which they giue, for ease and remedy to prolong life.

text.DRB: He that sinneth in his sight, that made him, shall fall into the hands of the physician.
text.NABRE: Whoever is a sinner before his Maker will be defiant toward the doctor.
text.KJV: 38:15 He that sinneth before his maker, let him fal into the hand of the Phisitian.
38:16 My sonne, let teares fall downe ouer the dead, and begin to lament, as if thou hadst suffered great harme thy selfe: and then couer his body according to the custome, & neglect not his buriall.
text.NABRE: My son, shed tears for one who is dead with wailing and bitter lament; As is only proper, prepare the body, and do not absent yourself from the burial.
text.NRSV: My child, let your tears fall for the dead, and as one who is suffering terribly begin the lament.
text.DRB: Son, upon the dead shed tears, and begin to weep as having suffered doleful things, and according to judgement cover his body, and neglect not his burial.
But for detraction bear bitterly the mourning of him one day, and be comforted for the heaviness,
text.NABRE: Weeping bitterly, mourning fully, pay your tribute of sorrow, as deserved: A day or two, to prevent gossip; then compose yourself after your grief.
text.KJV: 38:17 Weepe bitterly, and make great moane, and vse lamentation, as hee is worthy, and that a day or two, lest thou be euill spoken of: and then comfort thy selfe for thy heauinesse.

text.DRB: and make mourning according to his desert one day, or two, because of detraction.
text.NABRE: For grief can bring on death, and heartache can sap one’s strength.
text.KJV: 38:18 For of heauinesse commeth death, and the heauinesse of the heart, breaketh strength.

text.DRB: For by heaviness death hasteneth, and it covereth the strength, and sorrow of the heart boweth the neck.
text.NABRE: When a person is carried away, sorrow is over; and the life of the poor one is grievous to the heart.
text.KJV: 38:19 In affliction also sorrow remaineth: and the life of the poore, is the curse of the heart.

text.DRB: In abstraction sorrow is permanent: and the substance of the poor is according to his heart.
text.NABRE: Do not turn your thoughts to him again; cease to recall him; think rather of the end.
text.KJV: 38:20 Take no heauines to heart: driue it away, and remember the last end.
38:21 Forget it not, for there is no turning againe: thou shalt not doe him good, but hurt thy selfe.
text.NABRE: Do not recall him, for there is no hope of his return; you do him no good, and you harm yourself.
text.NRSV: Do not give your heart to sorrow; drive it away, and remember your own end.
text.DRB: Give not thine heart into heaviness, but expel it from thee: and remember the latter ends,
and forget not: for neither is there return, and him thou shalt profit nothing, and thou shalt hurt thyself.
text.NABRE: Remember that his fate will also be yours; for him it was yesterday, for you today.
text.KJV: 38:22 Remember my iudgement: for thine also shall be so; yesterday for me, and to day for thee.

text.DRB: Be mindful of my judgement: for thine also must be so: to me yesterday, and to thee today.
text.NABRE: With the dead at rest, let memory cease; be consoled, once the spirit has gone.
text.KJV: 38:23 When the dead is at rest, let his remembrance rest, & be comforted for him, when his spirit is departed from him.
38:24 The wisedome of a learned man cōmeth by opportunitie of leasure: & he that hath litle busines shal become wise.
text.NABRE: Vocations of the Skilled Worker and the Scribe The scribe’s wisdom increases wisdom; whoever is free from toil can become wise.
text.NRSV: The wisdom of the scribe comes through opportunity of leisure; the one who has little business can become wise.
text.DRB: In the repose of the dead make the memory of him to rest, and comfort him in the departing of his spirit.
The wisdom of a scribe in the time of vacancy: and he that is less in action, shall receive wisdom.
text.NABRE: How can one become learned who guides the plow, and thrills in wielding the goad like a lance, Who guides the ox and urges on the bullock, and whose every concern is for cattle?
text.KJV: 38:25 How can he get wisdome that holdeth the plough, and that glorieth in the goad; that driueth oxen, and is occupied in their labours, and whose talke is of bullocks?

text.DRB: With what wisdom shall he be replenished, that holdeth the plough, and glorieth in the goad, driveth oxen with the prickle, and converseth in their works, and his talk is in the breed of bulls?
text.NABRE: His concern is to plow furrows, and he is careful to fatten the livestock.
text.KJV: 38:26 He giueth his minde to make furrowes: and is diligent to giue the kine fodder.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to turn up furrows, and his watching in the feeding of kine.
text.NABRE: So with every engraver and designer who, laboring night and day, Fashions carved seals, and whose concern is to vary the pattern. His determination is to produce a lifelike impression, and he is careful to finish the work.
text.KJV: 38:27 So euery carpenter, and workemaster, that laboureth night and day: and they that cut and graue seales, and are diligent to make great variety, and giue themselues to counterfait imagerie, and watch to finish a worke.

text.DRB: So every craftsman and workmaster that passeth the night as the day, that maketh graven seals, and his continual diligence varieth the picture: he will give his heart to the similitude of the picture, and his watching will perfect the work.
text.NABRE: So too the smith sitting by the anvil, intent on the iron he forges. The flame from the fire sears his flesh, yet he toils away in the furnace heat. The clang of the hammer deafens his ears; his eyes are on the object he is shaping. His determination is to finish the work, and he is careful to perfect it in detail.
text.KJV: 38:28 The smith also sitting by the anuill, & considering the iron worke; the vapour of the fire wasteth his flesh, and he fighteth with the heat of the furnace: the noise of the hammer & the anuill is euer in his eares, and his eies looke still vpon the patterne of the thing that he maketh, he setteth his mind to finish his worke, & watcheth to polish it perfitly.

text.DRB: So the ironsmith sitting by the anvil and considering the work of iron. The vapour of the fire will parch his flesh, and he striveth in the heat of the furnace:
text.NABRE: So also the potter sitting at his labor, revolving the wheel with his feet. He is always concerned for his products, and turns them out in quantity.
text.KJV: 38:29 So doeth the potter sitting at his worke, and turning the wheele about with his feet, who is alway carefully set at his worke: and maketh all his worke by number.

text.DRB: The noise of the hammer reneweth his ear, and his eye is against the similitude of the vessel.
text.NABRE: With his hands he molds the clay, and with his feet softens it. His determination is to complete the glazing, and he is careful to fire the kiln.
text.KJV: 38:30 He fashioneth the clay with his arme, and boweth downe his strength before his feet: he applieth himselfe to lead it ouer; and he is diligent to make cleane the furnace.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to the finishing of the works, and his watching will polish to perfection.
text.NABRE: All these are skilled with their hands, each one an expert at his own work;
text.KJV: 38:31 All these trust to their hands: and euery one is wise in his worke.

text.DRB: So the potter sitting at his work, turning the wheel with his feet, who is always set in carefulness for his work, and all his working is in number:
text.NABRE: Without them no city could be lived in, and wherever they stay, they do not go hungry. But they are not sought out for the council of the people,
text.KJV: 38:32 Without these cannot a citie be inhabited: and they shall not dwell where they will, nor goe vp and downe.

text.DRB: With his arm he will fashion the clay, and before his feet he will bend his strength:
text.NABRE: nor are they prominent in the assembly. They do not sit on the judge’s bench, nor can they understand law and justice. They cannot expound discipline or judgment, nor are they found among the rulers.
text.KJV: 38:33 They shall not be sought for in publike counsaile; nor sit high in the congregation: they shal not sit on the Iudges seate, nor vnderstand the sentence of iudgement: they cannot declare iustice, and iudgement, and they shall not be found where parables are spoken.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to finish the varnishing thereof, and his watching will make clean the furnace.
text.NABRE: Yet they maintain the fabric of the world, and their concern is for exercise of their skill.
text.KJV: 38:34 But they will maintaine the state of the world, and  their desire is in the worke of their craft.

text.DRB: All these have hoped in their hands, and every one is wise in his own art.
Without these a city is not built.
And they shall not inhabit, nor walk therein, and they shall not leap high into the congregation.
Upon the judge's seat they shall not sit, and the ordinance of judgement they shall not understand, neither shall they declare discipline and judgement, and in parables they shall not be found:
but they shall confirm the creature of the world, and their prayer shall be in the work of their art, applying their soul, & searching in the law of the Highest.

text.KJV: 39:1 But hee that giueth his minde to the Law of the most high, and is occupied in the meditation thereof, wil seeke out the wisdome of all the ancient, and be occupied in prophecies.
text.NABRE: Chapter 39 How different the person who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He explores the wisdom of all the ancients and is occupied with the prophecies;
text.NRSV: How different is the one who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He seeks out the wisdom of all the ancients, and is concerned with prophecies;
text.DRB: The wise man will search out the wisdom of all the ancients, and will be occupied in the prophets.

text.KJV: 39:2 Hee will keepe the sayings of the renowmed men: and where subtile parables are, he will be there also.
text.NABRE: He preserves the discourses of the famous, and goes to the heart of involved sayings;
text.NRSV: he preserves the sayings of the famous and penetrates the subtleties of parables;
text.DRB: He will keep the narration of famous men, and will enter withal into the subtilties of parables.
He will search out the hidden senses of proverbs, and will converse in the secrets of parables.
text.NABRE: He seeks out the hidden meaning of proverbs, and is busied with the enigmas found in parables.
text.KJV: 39:3 Hee will seeke out the secrets of graue sentences, and be conuersant in darke parables.

text.DRB: In the midst of great men he will minister, and in the sight of the president he shall appear.
text.NABRE: He is in attendance on the great, and appears before rulers. He travels among the peoples of foreign lands to test what is good and evil among people.
text.KJV: 39:4 He shall serue among great men, and appeare before princes: he will trauaile through strange countreys, for hee hath tried the good, and the euill among men.

text.DRB: He shall pass into the land of strange nations: for he shall try good and evil in men.
text.NABRE: His care is to rise early to seek the Lord his Maker, to petition the Most High, To open his mouth in prayer, to ask pardon for his sins.
text.KJV: 39:5 Hee will giue his heart to resort early to the Lord that made him, and will pray before the most high, and will open his mouth in prayer, and make supplication for his sinnes.
39:6 When the great Lord will, he shall bee filled with the spirit of vnderstanding: he shal powre out wise sentences, and giue thankes vnto the Lord in his prayer.
text.NABRE: If it pleases the Lord Almighty, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; He will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give praise to the Lord.
text.NRSV: If the great Lord is willing, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; he will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give thanks to the Lord.
text.DRB: He will give his heart to watch early unto our Lord, that made him, and he will pray in the sight of the Highest.
He will open his mouth in prayer, and will entreat for his sins.
text.NABRE: He will direct his knowledge and his counsel, as he meditates upon God’s mysteries.
text.KJV: 39:7 Hee shall direct his counsell and knowledge, and in his secrets shall hee meditate.

text.DRB: For if it shall please our great Lord, he will fill him with the spirit of understanding:
text.NABRE: He will show the wisdom of what he has learned and glory in the Law of the Lord’s covenant.
text.KJV: 39:8 Hee shall shew foorth that which he hath learned, and shall glory in the Law of the couenant of the Lord.

text.DRB: and he will pour forth the words of his wisdom as showers, and in prayer will confess to our Lord.
text.NABRE: Many will praise his understanding; his name can never be blotted out; Unfading will be his memory, through all generations his name will live;
text.KJV: 39:9 Many shall commend his vnderstanding, and so long as the world endureth, it shall not be blotted out, his memoriall shall not depart away, and his name shall liue from generation to generation.

text.DRB: And he will direct his counsel, and discipline, and in his secrets he will consult.
text.NABRE: Peoples will speak of his wisdom, and the assembly will declare his praise.
text.KJV: 39:10 Nations shall shewe foorth his wisedome, and the congregation shall declare his praise.

text.DRB: He will open the discipline of his doctrine, & will glory in the law of the testament of our Lord.
text.NABRE: While he lives he is one out of a thousand, and when he dies he leaves a good name.
text.KJV: 39:11 If hee die, he shall leaue a greater name then a thousand: and if he liue, he shall increase it.

text.DRB: Many will praise his wisdom, and it shall not be abolished for ever.
text.NABRE: Praise of God the Creator Once more I will set forth my theme to shine like the moon in its fullness!
text.KJV: 39:12 Yet I haue more to say which I haue thought vpon, for I am filled as the Moone at the full.

text.DRB: The memory of him shall not depart, and his name shall be required from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: Listen to me, my faithful children: open up your petals, like roses planted near running waters;
text.KJV: 39:13 Hearken vnto me, ye holy children, and budde foorth as a rose growing by the brooke of the field:

text.DRB: Nations shall declare his wisdom, & the Church will shew forth his praise.
text.NABRE: Send up the sweet odor of incense, break forth in blossoms like the lily. Raise your voices in a chorus of praise; bless the Lord for all his works!
text.KJV: 39:14 And giue yee a sweete sauour as frankincense, and flourish as a lilly, send foorth a smell, and sing a song of praise, blesse the Lord in all his workes.

text.DRB: If he continue, he shall leave a name more than a thousand: and if he rest, it shall profit him.
text.NABRE: Proclaim the greatness of his name, loudly sing his praises, With music on the harp and all stringed instruments; sing out with joy as you proclaim:
text.KJV: 39:15 Magnifie his Name, and shewe foorth his praise with the songs of your lips, and with harpes, and in praising him you shall say after this maner:
39:16 Al the works of the Lord are exceeding good, & whatsoeuer hee commandeth, shalbe accomplished in due season.
text.NABRE: The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.
text.NRSV: “All the works of the Lord are very good, and whatever he commands will be done at the appointed time.”
text.DRB: I will yet consult that I may declare: For as with fury I am replenished.
In voice he saith: Hear me ye divine fruits, and as the rose planted upon the rivers of waters fructify ye.
text.NABRE: At his word the waters become still as in a flask; he had but to speak and the reservoirs were made.
text.KJV: 39:17 And none may say, What is this? wherefore is that? for at time conuenient they shall all be sought out: at his commaundement the waters stood as an heape, & at the wordes of his mouth the receptacles of waters.

text.DRB: As Libanus have ye the odours of sweetness.
text.NABRE: He has but to command and his will is done; nothing can limit his saving action.
text.KJV: 39:18 At his commandement is done whatsoeuer pleaseth him, and none can hinder when he will saue.

text.DRB: Flourish ye flowers, as the lily, and give forth an odour, and bring forth leaves in grace, and praise with song, and bless our Lord in his works.
text.NABRE: The works of all humankind are present to him; nothing is hidden from his eyes.
text.KJV: 39:19 The workes of all flesh are before him, & nothing can be hid from his eyes.

text.DRB: Give magnificence to his name, and confess unto him in the voice of your lips, and in songs of the lips, and harps, & thus shall ye say in confession:
text.NABRE: His gaze spans all the ages: is there any limit to his saving action? To him, nothing is small or insignificant, and nothing too wonderful or hard for him.
text.KJV: 39:20 He seeth from euerlasting to euerlasting, and there is nothing wonderfull before him.

text.DRB: All the works of our Lord are exceeding good.
text.NABRE: No cause then to say: “What is the purpose of this?” Everything is chosen to satisfy a need.
text.KJV: 39:21 A man neede not to say, What is this? wherefore is that? for hee hath made all things for their vses.

text.DRB: At his word the water stood as an heap: and at the word of his mouth as it were receptacles of waters:
text.NABRE: His blessing overflows like the Nile; like the Euphrates it enriches the surface of the earth.
text.KJV: 39:22 His blessing couered the dry land as a riuer, and watered it as a flood.

text.DRB: because in his commandment placability is made, and there is no diminishing of his salvation.
text.NABRE: Even so, his wrath dispossesses the nations and turns fertile land into a salt marsh.
text.KJV: 39:23 As hee hath turned the waters into saltnesse: so shall the heathen inherite his wrath.

text.DRB: The works of all flesh are before him, and there is nothing hid from his eyes.
text.NABRE: For the virtuous his paths are level, to the haughty they are clogged with stones.
text.KJV: 39:24 As his wayes are plaine vnto the holy, so are they stumbling blockes vnto the wicked.

text.DRB: From world to world he beholdeth, and nothing is marvelous in his sight.
text.NABRE: Good things for the good he provided from the beginning, but for the wicked good things and bad.
text.KJV: 39:25 For the good, are good things created from the beginning: so euill things for sinners.
39:26 The principall things for the whole vse of mans life, are water, fire, yron, and salt, floure of wheate, honie, milke, and the blood of the grape, and oyle, and clothing.
text.NABRE: Chief of all needs for human life are water and fire, iron and salt, The heart of the wheat, milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.
text.NRSV: Basic to all the needs of human life are water and fire and iron and salt and wheat flour and milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil and clothing.
text.DRB: It is not to be said: What is this, or what is that? For all things shall be sought in their time.

text.KJV: 39:27 All these things are for good to the godly: so to the sinners they are turned into euill.
text.NABRE: For the good all these are good, but for the wicked they turn out evil.
text.NRSV: All these are good for the godly, but for sinners they turn into evils.
text.DRB: His blessing hath overflowed as a stream.
And as a flood hath watered the dry land, so his wrath shall inherit the nations, that have not sought him:
text.NABRE: There are stormwinds created to punish; in their fury they can dislodge mountains. In a time of destruction they hurl their force and calm the anger of their Maker.
text.KJV: 39:28 There be spirits that are created for vengeance, which in their furie lay on sore strokes, in the time of destruction they powre out their force, and appease the wrath of him that made them.

text.DRB: even as he turned waters into drought, & the earth was made dry: and his ways are direct to the ways of them: so to sinners stumbling blocks in his wrath.
text.NABRE: Fire and hail, famine and disease: these too were created for punishment.
text.KJV: 39:29 Fire, and haile, and famine, and death: all these were created for vengeance:

text.DRB: Good things were created for the good from the beginning, so for the wicked, good things and evil.
text.NABRE: Ravenous beasts, scorpions, vipers, and the avenging sword to exterminate the wicked: All these were created to meet a need, and are kept in his storehouse for the proper time.
text.KJV: 39:30 Teeth of wild beasts, and scorpions, serpents, & the sword, punishing the wicked to destruction.

text.DRB: The beginning of the thing necessary for the life of men, water, fire, and iron, salt, milk, and bread of flour, and honey, and the cluster of grape, and oil, & clothing.
text.NABRE: When he commands them, they rejoice, in their assigned tasks they do not disobey his command.
text.KJV: 39:31 They shall reioice in his commandement, and they shall bee ready vpon earth when neede is, and when their time is come, they shall not transgresse his word.

text.DRB: All these shall be converted to saints into good, so also to the impious and to sinners into evil.
text.NABRE: That is why from the first I took my stand, and wrote down as my theme:
text.KJV: 39:32 Therefore from the beginning I was resolued, and thought vpon these things, and haue left them in writing.

text.DRB: There are spirits, that were created for vengeance, and in their fury they have confirmed their torments:
text.NABRE: The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.
text.KJV: 39:33 All the workes of the Lord are good: and he will giue euery needefull thing in due season.

text.DRB: in the time of consummation they shall pour out strength: and they shall accomplish the fury of him, that made them.
text.NABRE: There is no cause then to say: “This is not as good as that”; for each shows its worth at the proper time.
text.KJV: 39:34 So that a man cannot say, This is worse then that: for in time they shall all be well approued.

text.DRB: Fire, hail, famine, and death, all these were created for vengeance:
text.NABRE: So now with full heart and voice proclaim and bless his name!
text.KJV: 39:35 And therefore praise ye the Lord with the whole heart and mouth, and blesse the Name of the Lord.

text.DRB: the teeth of beasts, and scorpions, and serpents, and sword revenging the impious unto destruction.
In his commandments they shall make merry, and on the earth they shall be prepared when need is, and in their times they shall not pretermit a word.
Therefore from the beginning I was confirmed, and I have consulted, and thought, and left written.
All the works of our Lord are good, & he will give every work in his hour.
It is not to be said: This is worse than that: for all shall be approved in their time.
And now with all heart and mouth praise ye, and bless the name of our Lord.

text.KJV: 40:1 Great trauaile is created for euery man, and an heauy yoke is vpon the sons of Adam, from the day that they goe out of their mothers wombe, till the day that they returne to the mother of all things.
text.NABRE: Chapter 40 Joys and Miseries of Life A great anxiety has God allotted, and a heavy yoke, to the children of Adam, From the day they leave their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.NRSV: Hard work was created for everyone, and a heavy yoke is laid on the children of Adam, from the day they come forth from their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.DRB: Great travail is created to all men, and an heavy yoke upon the children of Adam, from the day of their coming forth of their mother's womb, until the day of their burying, into the mother of all.
Their cogitations, and fears of the heart, imagination of things to come, and the day of their ending:
text.NABRE: Troubled thoughts and fear of heart are theirs and anxious foreboding until death.
text.KJV: 40:2 Their imagination of things to come, & the day of death  their thoughts, and  feare of heart:

text.DRB: from him that sitteth upon the glorious seat, unto him that is humbled in earth & ashes.
text.NABRE: Whether one sits on a lofty throne or grovels in dust and ashes,
text.KJV: 40:3 From him that sitteth on a throne of glory, vnto him that is humbled in earth and ashes.

text.DRB: From him that weareth hyacinth, and beareth the crown, even to him, that is covered with rude linen: fury, envy, tumult, wavering, and the fear of death, anger persevering, and contention,
text.NABRE: Whether one wears a splendid crown or is clothed in the coarsest of garments—
text.KJV: 40:4 From him that weareth purple, and a crown, vnto him that is clothed with a linnen frocke.

text.DRB: and in the time of repose in bed, the sleep of night changeth his knowledge.
text.NABRE: There is wrath and envy, trouble and dread, terror of death, fury and strife. Even when one lies on his bed to rest, his cares disturb his sleep at night.
text.KJV: 40:5 Wrath, and enuie, trouble and vnquietnesse, feare of death, and anger, and strife, and in the time of rest vpon his bed, his night sleepe doe change his knowledge.

text.DRB: Alittle is as nothing in rest, and afterward in sleep, as in the day of watch.
text.NABRE: So short is his rest it seems like none, till in his dreams he struggles as he did by day, Troubled by the visions of his mind, like a fugitive fleeing from the pursuer.
text.KJV: 40:6 A litle or nothing is his rest, and afterward he is in his sleepe, as in a day of keeping watch, troubled in the vision of his heart, as if he were escaped out of a battell:

text.DRB: He is troubled in the vision of his heart, as he that hath escaped in the day of battle. In the time of his safety he rose up, and marveleth at no fear:
text.NABRE: As he reaches safety, he wakes up, astonished that there was nothing to fear.
text.KJV: 40:7 When all is safe, he awaketh, and marueileth that the feare was nothing.

text.DRB: With all flesh, from man even to beast, and upon sinners sevenfold.
text.NABRE: To all flesh, human being and beast, but for sinners seven times more,
text.KJV: 40:8 vnto all flesh, both man and beast, and that is seuen fold more vpon sinners.

text.DRB: Beside these things, death, blood, contention, and sword, oppressions, famine, and contrition, and scourges:
text.NABRE: Come plague and bloodshed, fiery heat and drought, plunder and ruin, famine and death.
text.KJV: 40:9 Death and bloodshed, strife and sword, calamities, famine, tribulation, and the scourge:

text.DRB: for the wicked all these were created, and for them the flood was made.
text.NABRE: For the wicked evil was created, and because of them destruction hastens.
text.KJV: 40:10 These things are created for the wicked, and for their sakes came the flood.
40:11 All things that are of the earth shal turne to the earth againe: and that which is of the waters doeth returne into the Sea.
text.NABRE: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from above returns above.
text.NRSV: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from the waters returns to the sea.
text.DRB: All things that are of the earth, shall turn into the earth, and all waters shall return into the sea.
All bribing, and iniquity shall be clean taken away, and fidelity shall stand for ever.
text.NABRE: All that comes from bribes or injustice will be wiped out, but loyalty remains forever.
text.KJV: 40:12 All briberie and iniustice shall be blotted out: but true dealing shall endure for euer.

text.DRB: The riches of the unjust shall be dried up as a river, and they shall sound as great thunder in rain.
text.NABRE: Wealth from injustice is like a flooding wadi, like a mighty stream with lightning and thunder,
