text.KJV: 40:13 The goods of the vniust shall bee dried vp like a riuer, and shall vanish with noise, like a great thunder in raine.

text.DRB: In opening his hands he shall rejoice: so transgressors shall pine away in consumption.
text.NABRE: Which, in its rising, rolls along the stones, but suddenly, once and for all, comes to an end.
text.KJV: 40:14 While he openeth his hand he shal reioyce: so shall transgressours come to nought.

text.DRB: The nephews of the impious shall not multiply boughs, nor unclean roots sound upon the top of a rock.
text.NABRE: The offshoot of violence will not flourish, for the root of the godless is on sheer rock.
text.KJV: 40:15 The children of the vngodly shall not bring forth many branches: but are as vncleane roots vpon a hard rocke.

text.DRB: Over all water greenness, and at the brink of the river it shall be plucked up before all grass.
text.NABRE: They are like reeds on riverbanks, withered before all other plants;
text.KJV: 40:16 The weed growing vpon euery water, and banke of a riuer, shall bee pulled vp before all grasse.
40:17 Bountifulnes is as a most fruitfull garden, and mercifulnesse endureth for euer.
text.NABRE: But goodness, like eternity, will never be cut off, and righteousness endures forever.
text.NRSV: Kindness is like a garden of blessings, and almsgiving endures forever.
text.DRB: Grace is as paradise in blessings, and mercy remaineth for ever.

text.KJV: 40:18 To labour & to be content with that a man hath, is a sweet life: but hee that findeth a treasure, is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: Wealth or wages can make life sweet, but better than either, finding a treasure.
text.NRSV: To be self-sufficient and to be a worker—both are sweet, but better than both is the one who finds a treasure.
text.DRB: The life of a workman that is sufficient for himself shall be sweet, and in it thou shalt find a treasure.
Children, and building of a city shall confirm the name, and an unspotted woman shall be counted above this.
text.NABRE: A child or a city will preserve one’s name, but better than either, finding wisdom. Cattle and orchards make a person flourish; but better than either, a devoted wife.
text.KJV: 40:19 Children and the building of a citie continue a mans name: but a blamelesse wife is counted aboue them both.
40:20 Wine & musicke reioyce the heart: but the loue of wisedome is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: Wine and strong drink delight the soul, but better than either, love of friends.
text.NRSV: Wine and music gladden the heart, but the love of wisdom is better than both.
text.DRB: Wine and music make a joyful heart: and the love of wisdom is above both.

text.KJV: 40:21 The pipe and the psalterie make sweet melodie: but a pleasant tongue is aboue them both.
text.NABRE: Flute and harp offer sweet melody, but better than either, a pure tongue.
text.NRSV: Flute and harp make pleasant melody, but a gentle tongue is better than both.
text.DRB: Shalms, and Psaltery make sweet melody, and a sweet tongue is above both.
Thine eye will desire grace and beauty, and green sown fields are above this.
text.NABRE: Grace and beauty delight the eye, but better than either, the produce of the field.
text.KJV: 40:22 Thine eye desireth fauour and beautie: but more then both, corne while it is greene.
40:23 A friend and companion neuer meet amisse: but aboue both is a wife with her husband.
text.NABRE: A friend and a neighbor are timely guides, but better than either, a sensible wife.
text.NRSV: A friend and a companion encounter each other at the opportune moment, but better than both is a wife with her husband.
text.DRB: Afriend and companion meeting together in time, & above them both is a woman with her husband.
Brethren are an help in the time of tribulation, and mercy shall deliver more than they.
text.NABRE: Relatives and helpers for times of stress; but better than either, charity that rescues.
text.KJV: 40:24 Brethren and helpe are against time of trouble: but almes shall deliuer more then them both.

text.DRB: Gold & silver are the establishing of the feet: and counsel is well accepted above them both.
text.NABRE: Gold and silver make one’s way secure, but better than either, sound judgment.
text.KJV: 40:25 Golde and siluer make the foote stand sure: but counsell is esteemed aboue them both.

text.DRB: Riches & strength exalt the heart, and above these is the fear of our Lord.
text.NABRE: Wealth and vigor make the heart exult, but better than either, fear of God. In the fear of the Lord there is no want; whoever has it need seek no other support.
text.KJV: 40:26 Riches and strength lift vp the heart: but the feare of the Lord is aboue them both: there is no want in the feare of the Lord, and it needeth not to seeke helpe.

text.DRB: There is no diminution in the fear of our Lord, and in it there is no need to seek for help.
text.NABRE: The fear of God is a paradise of blessings; its canopy is over all that is glorious.
text.KJV: 40:27 The feare of the Lord is a fruitfull garden, and couereth him aboue all glory.

text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is as a paradise of blessing, and they have covered it above all glory.
text.NABRE: My son, do not live the life of a beggar; better to die than to beg.
text.KJV: 40:28 My sonne, lead not a beggers life: for better it is to die then to beg.

text.DRB: Son in thy lifetime want not: for it is better to die than to want.
text.NABRE: When one has to look to a stranger’s table, life is not worth living. The delicacies offered bring revulsion of spirit, and to the intelligent, inward torture.
text.KJV: 40:29 The life of him that dependeth on another mans table, is not to be counted for a life: for he polluteth himselfe with other mens meate, but a wise man well nurtured will beware thereof.

text.DRB: Aman that looketh toward another man's table, his life is as no life, thinking how to live, for he feedeth his soul with another man's meats.
text.NABRE: In the mouth of the shameless begging is sweet, but within him it burns like fire.
text.KJV: 40:30 Begging is sweet in the mouth of the shamelesse: but in his belly there shall burne a fire.

text.DRB: But a man nurtured and taught, will look to himself.
Poverty will be sweet in the mouth of the unwise, and in his belly a fire will burn.

text.KJV: 41:1 O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that liueth at rest in his possessions, vnto the man that hath nothing to vexe him, and that hath prosperity in all things: yea vnto him that is yet able to receiue meate?
text.NABRE: Chapter 41 O death! How bitter is the thought of you for the one at peace in his home, For the one who is serene and always successful, who can still enjoy life’s pleasures.
text.NRSV: O death, how bitter is the thought of you to the one who lives at peace among his possessions,
text.DRB: O Death how bitter is thy memory to a man that hath peace in his riches:

text.KJV: 41:2 O death, acceptable is thy sentence vnto the needy, and vnto him whose strength faileth, that is now in the last age, and is vexed with all things, and to him that despaireth and hath lost patience.
text.NABRE: O death! How welcome is your sentence to the weak, failing in strength, Stumbling and tripping on everything, with sight gone and hope lost.
text.NRSV: O death, how welcome is your sentence to one who is in need and is failing in strength,
text.DRB: to a man that is at rest, and whose ways are prosperous in all things, and that is yet able to take meat!

text.KJV: 41:3 Feare not the sentence of death, remember them that haue beene before thee, and that come after, for this is the sentence of the Lord ouer all flesh.
text.NABRE: Do not fear death’s decree for you; remember, it embraces those before you and those to come.
text.NRSV: Do not fear the sentence of death; remember those who went before you and those who will come after.
text.DRB: O death, thy judgement is good to a needy man, and him that is diminished in strength,
and faileth in age, and that is careful of all things, and to the incredulous, that loseth patience!
text.NABRE: This decree for all flesh is from God; why then should you reject a law of the Most High? Whether one has lived a thousand years, a hundred, or ten, in Sheol there are no arguments about life.
text.KJV: 41:4 And why art thou against the pleasure of the most High? there is no inquisition in the graue, whether thou haue liued ten, or a hundred, or a thousand yeeres.

text.DRB: Fear not the judgement of death. Remember what things have been before thee, and what come after thee: this is the judgement from our Lord to all flesh:
text.NABRE: The children of sinners are a reprobate line, and witless offspring are in the homes of the wicked.
text.KJV: 41:5 The children of sinners, are abhominable children: and they that are conuersant in the dwelling of the vngodly.

text.DRB: and what shall come upon thee by the good pleasure of the Highest? Whether it be ten, or an hundred, or a thousand years.
text.NABRE: The inheritance of children of sinners will perish, and on their offspring will be perpetual disgrace.
text.KJV: 41:6 The inheritance of sinners children shal perish, and their posterity shal haue a perpetuall reproch.

text.DRB: For in hell there is no accusing of life.
text.NABRE: Children curse their wicked father, for they suffer disgrace because of him.
text.KJV: 41:7 The children will complaine of an vngodly father, because they shall be reproched for his sake.

text.DRB: The children of sinners become children of abominations, and they that converse near the houses of the impious.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, O wicked people, who forsake the Law of the Most High.
text.KJV: 41:8 Woe be vnto you vngodly men which haue forsaken the law of the most high God: for if you encrease, it shall be to your destruction.

text.DRB: The inheritance of the children of sinners shall perish, and with their seed shall be continuance of reproach.
text.NABRE: If you have children, calamity will be theirs; and if you beget them, it will be only for groaning. When you stumble, there is lasting joy; and when you die, you become a curse.
text.KJV: 41:9 And if you be borne, you shall be borne to a curse: and if you die, a curse shall be your portion.

text.DRB: The children complain of an impious father, because for him they are in reproach.
text.NABRE: All that is nought returns to nought, so too the godless—from void to void.
text.KJV: 41:10 All that are of the earth shall turne to earth againe: so the vngodly shall goe from a curse to destruction.

text.DRB: Woe to you ye impious men, which have forsaken the law of our Lord the Highest.
text.NABRE: The human body is a fleeting thing, but a virtuous name will never be annihilated.
text.KJV: 41:11 The mourning of men is about their bodies: but an ill name of sinners shall be blotted out.
41:12 Haue regard to thy name: for that shall continue with thee aboue a thousand great treasures of gold.
text.NABRE: Have respect for your name, for it will stand by you more than thousands of precious treasures.
text.NRSV: Have regard for your name, since it will remain for you longer than a thousand great treasures of gold.
text.DRB: And if ye be born, ye shall be born in malediction: and if ye die, in malediction shall be your portion.

text.KJV: 41:13 A good life hath but few daies: but a good name endureth for euer.
text.NABRE: The good things of life last a number of days, but a good name, for days without number.
text.NRSV: The days of a good life are numbered, but a good name lasts forever.
text.DRB: All things that are of the earth, shall return into the earth: so the impious from malediction to perdition.
The mourning of men is in their body, but the name of the impious shall be clean wiped out.
text.NABRE: Hidden wisdom and concealed treasure, of what value is either? Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom. My children, listen to instruction about shame;
text.KJV: 41:14 My children, keepe discipline in peace: for wisedome that is hid, and a treasure that is not seene, what profit is in them both?

text.DRB: Have care of a good name: for this shall be more permanent to thee than a thousand treasures precious and great.
text.NABRE: True and False Shame Hidden wisdom and concealed treasure, of what value is either? Better is the person who hides his folly than the one who hides his wisdom. My children, listen to instruction about shame;
text.KJV: 41:15 A man that hideth his foolishnesse is better then a man that hideth his wisedome.

text.DRB: There is a number of the days of a good life: but a good name shall continue for ever.
text.NABRE: judge of disgrace according to my rules, Not every kind of shame is shameful, nor is every kind of disgrace to be recognized.
text.KJV: 41:16 Therefore be shamefast according to my word: for it is not good to retaine all shamefastnesse, neither is it altogether approoued in euery thing.

text.DRB: Children, keep ye discipline in peace. For wisdom hid, and treasure not seen, what profit is there in them both?
text.NABRE: Before father and mother be ashamed of immorality, before prince and ruler, of falsehood;
text.KJV: 41:17 Be ashamed of whoredome before father and mother, and of a lie before a prince and a mighty man:

text.DRB: Better is the man that hideth his folly, than the man that hideth his wisdom.
text.NABRE: Before master and mistress, of deceit; before the public assembly, of crime; Before associate and friend, of disloyalty,
text.KJV: 41:18 Of an offence before a iudge and ruler, of iniquitie before a congregation and people, of vniust dealing before thy partner and friend:

text.DRB: But yet have reverence to these things, which proceed from my mouth.
text.NABRE: and in the place where you settle, of theft. Be ashamed of breaking an oath or a covenant, and of stretching your elbow at dinner; Of refusing to give when asked,
text.KJV: 41:19 And of theft in regard of the place where thou soiournest, and in regard of the trueth of God and his couenant, and to leane with thine elbow vpon the meate, and of scorning to giue and take:

text.DRB: For it is not good to observe all shamefastness: & all things do not please all men in opinion.
text.NABRE: of rebuffing your own relatives; Of defrauding another of his appointed share, of failing to return a greeting; Of gazing at a man’s wife, of entertaining thoughts about another woman;
text.KJV: 41:20 And of silence before them that salute thee, and to look vpon an harlot:

text.DRB: Be ashamed before father & before mother, of fornication: and before the president and before the mighty, of lying:
text.NABRE: of rebuffing your own relatives; Of defrauding another of his appointed share, of failing to return a greeting; Of gazing at a man’s wife, of entertaining thoughts about another woman;
text.KJV: 41:21 And to turne away thy face from thy kinsman, or to take away a portion or a gift, or to gaze vpon another mans wife,

text.DRB: before the Prince, and before the judge, of offence: before the synagogue and the people, of iniquity:
text.NABRE: Of trifling with a servant girl you have, of violating her bed; Of using harsh words with friends, of following up your gifts with insults;
text.KJV: 41:22 Or to bee ouerbusie with his maide, and come not neere her bed, or of vpbraiding speaches before friends; and after thou hast giuen, vpbraide not:

text.DRB: before companion and friend, of injustice: and before the place where thou dwellest,
text.KJV: 41:23 Or of iterating and speaking againe that which thou hast heard, and of reuealing of secrets.

text.DRB: of theft, of the truth of God, and his testament: of leaning on the bread, and of reproof for the thing given and taken:
text.KJV: 41:24 So shalt thou be truely shamefast, and finde fauour before all men.

text.DRB: before them that salute thee, of silence: of beholding a woman that is an harlot: and of turning away thy countenance from thy kinsman.
Turn not away thy face from thy neighbour, & of taking away part & not restoring.
Behold not another man's wife, and search not his handmaid, neither stand by her bed.
Before friends, of opprobrious words: and when thou hast given, upbraid not.

text.KJV: 42:1 Of these things be not thou ashamed, and accept no person to sinne thereby.
text.NABRE: Chapter 42 Of repeating what you hear, of betraying any secret. Be ashamed of the right things, and you will find favor in the sight of all. But of these things do not be ashamed, lest you sin to save face:
text.NRSV: Of the following things do not be ashamed, and do not let partiality lead you to sin:
text.DRB: Repeat not the word which thou hast heard, neither reveal thou of a secret word, & thou shalt indeed be without confusion, and shalt find grace in the sight of all men: be not ashamed for all these things, and accept not person thereby to sin.

text.KJV: 42:2 Of the Law of the most High, and his Couenant, and of iudgement to iustifie the vngodly:
text.NABRE: Of the Law of the Most High and his precepts, or of justice that acquits the ungodly;
text.NRSV: of the law of the Most High and his covenant, and of justice to acquit the ungodly;
text.DRB: Of the law of the Highest, and his testament, and of judgement to justify the impious,
of the word of companions and wayfaring men, and of the giving of the inheritance of friends,
text.NABRE: Of sharing the expenses of a business or a journey, of dividing an inheritance or property;
text.KJV: 42:3 Of reckoning with thy partners, and traueilers: or of the gift of the heritage of friends:

text.DRB: of the equality of balance and weights, of the getting of many things and few,
text.NABRE: Of accuracy of scales and balances, of tested measures and weights; Of acquiring much or little,
text.KJV: 42:4 Of exactnesse of ballance, and waights: or of getting much or little:

text.DRB: of the corruption of buying, and of merchants, and of much discipline of thy children, and to make bloody the side of a wicked servant.
text.NABRE: of bargaining in dealing with a merchant; Of constant training of children, of beating the sides of a wicked servant;
text.KJV: 42:5 And of merchants indifferent selling, of much correction of children, and to make the side of an euill seruant to bleed.

text.DRB: Over a naughty woman a seal is good.
text.NABRE: Of a seal to keep a foolish wife at home, of a key where there are many hands;
text.KJV: 42:6 Sure keeping is good where an euill wife is, and shut vp where many hands are.

text.DRB: Where there are many hands, shut up, and what soever thou shalt deliver, number, and weigh it: and write every thing given and received.
text.NABRE: Of numbering every deposit, of recording all that is taken in and given out;
text.KJV: 42:7 Deliuer all things in number and waight, and put al in writing that thou giuest out, or receiuest in.

text.DRB: Of the discipline of the unwise and foolish, and of ancients, that are judged of young men: and thou shalt be well instructed in all things, and approved in the sight of all the living.
text.NABRE: Of chastisement for the silly and the foolish, for the aged and infirm answering for wanton conduct. Thus you will be truly refined and recognized by all as discreet.
text.KJV: 42:8 Be not ashamed to informe the vnwise and foolish, and the extreeme aged that contendeth with those that are yong, thus shalt thou bee truely learned and approued of all men liuing.

text.DRB: Adaughter is the secret watch of the father, and the care of her taketh away sleep, lest perhaps in her youth she become past age, & abiding with an husband she become odious:
text.NABRE: A Father’s Care for His Daughter A daughter is a treasure that keeps her father wakeful, and worry over her drives away sleep: Lest in her youth she remain unmarried, or when she is married, lest she be childless;
text.KJV: 42:9 The father waketh for the daughter when no man knoweth, and the care for her taketh away sleepe; when shee is yong lest shee passe away the flowre of her age, and being married, lest she should be hated:

text.DRB: lest at any time she be corrupted in her virginity, and in her father's house she be found with child: lest perhaps abiding with her husband she transgress, or at the least become barren.
text.NABRE: While unmarried, lest she be defiled, or in her husband’s house, lest she prove unfaithful; Lest she become pregnant in her father’s house, or be sterile in that of her husband.
text.KJV: 42:10 In her virginitie lest she should be defiled, and gotten with childe in her fathers house; and hauing an husband, lest she should misbehaue herselfe: and when shee is married, lest shee should be barren.

text.DRB: Over a dissolute daughter keep sure watch: lest at any time she make thee come into reproach with thine enemies, because of detraction in the city, and the objection of the people, and she confound thee in the multitude of the people.
text.NABRE: My son, keep a close watch on your daughter, lest she make you a laughingstock for your enemies, A byword in the city and the assembly of the people, an object of derision in public gatherings. See that there is no lattice in her room, or spot that overlooks the approaches to the house.
text.KJV: 42:11 Keepe a sure watch ouer a shamelesse daughter, lest shee make thee a laughing stocke to thine enemies, and a by-word in the citie, and a reproch among the people, and make thee ashamed before the multitude.

text.DRB: Look not on every body for beauty sake: & among women tarry not.
text.NABRE: Do not let her reveal her beauty to any male, or spend her time with married women;
text.KJV: 42:12 Behold not euery bodies beauty, and sit not in the midst of women.

text.DRB: For out of garments cometh forth the moth, and from a woman the iniquity of a man.
text.NABRE: For just as moths come from garments, so a woman’s wickedness comes from a woman.
text.KJV: 42:13 For from garments commeth a moth, and from women wickednesse.

text.DRB: For better is the iniquity of a man, than a woman doing a good turn, and a woman shaming unto reproach.
text.NABRE: Better a man’s harshness than a woman’s indulgence, a frightened daughter than any disgrace.
text.KJV: 42:14 Better is the churlishnesse of a man, then a courteous woman, a woman I say, which bringeth shame and reproch.
42:15 I will now remember the works of the Lord, and declare the things that I haue seene: in the words of the Lord are his workes.
text.NABRE: The Works of God in Nature Now will I recall God’s works; what I have seen, I will describe. By the Lord ’s word his works were brought into being; he accepts the one who does his will.
text.NRSV: I will now call to mind the works of the Lord, and will declare what I have seen. By the words of the Lord his works are done.
text.DRB: Itherefore will be mindful of the works of our Lord, and I will shew forth which I have seen. By the words of our Lord are his works.
The sun illuminating hath looked throughout all, and full of the glory of our Lord is his work.
text.NABRE: As the shining sun is clear to all, so the glory of the Lord fills all his works;
text.KJV: 42:16 The Sunne that giueth light, looketh vpon all things: and the worke thereof is full of the glory of the Lord.

text.DRB: Hath not our Lord made the saints to declare all his marvelous things, which our Lord the omnipotent confirmed to be established in his glory?
text.NABRE: Yet even God’s holy ones must fail in recounting the wonders of the Lord, Though God has given his hosts the strength to stand firm before his glory.
text.KJV: 42:17 The Lord hath not giuen power to the Saints to declare all his marueilous workes, which the Almightie Lord firmely setled, that whatsoeuer is, might be established for his glory.
42:18 He seeketh out the deepe and the heart, and considereth their crafty deuices: for the Lord knoweth all that may be knowen, and he beholdeth the signes of the world.
text.NABRE: He searches out the abyss and penetrates the heart; their secrets he understands. For the Most High possesses all knowledge, and sees from of old the things that are to come.
text.NRSV: He searches out the abyss and the human heart; he understands their innermost secrets.
text.DRB: He hath searched out the depth, and the heart of men: & in their subtility he hath considered.
For our Lord hath known all knowledge, and hath beheld the sign of age, declaring what things are past, and what are to come, revealing the tokens of secret things.
text.NABRE: He makes known the past and the future, and reveals the deepest secrets.
text.KJV: 42:19 Hee declareth the things that are past, and for to come, and reueileth the steps of hidden things.

text.DRB: No cogitation escapeth him, and no word hideth itself from him.
text.NABRE: He lacks no understanding; no single thing escapes him.
text.KJV: 42:20 No thought escapeth him, neither any word is hidden from him.

text.DRB: The glorious works of his wisdom he hath beautified: who is before the world & world without end, neither is there added,
text.NABRE: He regulates the mighty deeds of his wisdom; he is from all eternity one and the same, With nothing added, nothing taken away; no need of a counselor for him!
text.KJV: 42:21 Hee hath garnished the excellent workes of his wisedome, and hee is from euerlasting to euerlasting, vnto him may nothing be added, neither can he be diminished, and he hath no need of any counseller.

text.DRB: nor diminished, and he needeth not any man's counsel.
text.NABRE: How beautiful are all his works, delightful to gaze upon and a joy to behold!
text.KJV: 42:22 O how desireable are all his workes: and that a man may see euen to a sparke.

text.DRB: How are all his works to be desired, and which is as it were a spark to consider!
text.NABRE: Everything lives and abides forever; and to meet each need all things are preserved.
text.KJV: 42:23 All these things liue and remaine for euer, for all vses, and they are all obedient.
42:24 All things are double one against another: and hee hath made nothing vnperfit.
text.NABRE: All of them differ, one from another, yet none of them has he made in vain;
text.NRSV: All things are in pairs, one opposite the other, and he has made nothing incomplete.
text.DRB: All these live, and remain for ever, and in all necessity all things obey him.
All things double, one against one, and he hath made nothing to want.
text.NABRE: For each in turn, as it comes, is good; can one ever see enough of their splendor?
text.KJV: 42:25 One thing establisheth the good of another: and who shalbe filled with beholding his glory?

text.DRB: He hath confirmed the good things of every one. And who shall be filled seeing his glory?

text.KJV: 43:1 The pride of the height, the cleare firmament, the beautie of heauen, with his glorious shew;
text.NABRE: Chapter 43 The beauty of the celestial height and the pure firmament, heaven itself manifests its glory.
text.NRSV: The pride of the higher realms is the clear firmament, the appearance of heaven in a spectacle of glory.
text.DRB: The firmament of height is his beauty, the beauty of heaven is in the vision of glory.

text.KJV: 43:2 The Sunne when it appeareth, declaring at his rising, a marueilous instrument, the worke of the most High.
text.NABRE: The sun at its rising shines at its fullest, a wonderful instrument, the work of the Most High!
text.NRSV: The sun, when it appears, proclaims as it rises what a marvelous instrument it is, the work of the Most High.
text.DRB: The sun in sight declaring at his coming forth, a marvelous instrument, the work of the Highest.
At noon it burneth the earth, and who can abide in the presence of the heat thereof: keeping a furnace in the works of heat:
text.NABRE: At noon it scorches the earth, and who can bear its fiery heat?
text.KJV: 43:3 At noone it parcheth the country, and who can abide the burning heate thereof?

text.DRB: the sun three times so much burning the mountains, casting out fiery beams, and shining with his beams blindeth the eyes.
text.NABRE: Like a blazing furnace of solid metal, the sun’s rays set the mountains aflame; Its fiery tongue consumes the world; the eyes are burned by its fire.
text.KJV: 43:4 A man blowing a furnace is in works of heat, but the Sunne burneth the mountaines three times more; breathing out fiery vapours, and sending foorth bright beames, it dimmeth the eyes.

text.DRB: Great is our Lord that made it, and by his words it hath hastened his course.
text.NABRE: Great indeed is the Lord who made it, at whose orders it urges on its steeds.
text.KJV: 43:5 Great is the Lord that made it, and at his commandement it runneth hastily.
43:6 He made the Moone also to serue in her season, for a declaration of times, and a signe of the world.
text.NABRE: It is the moon that marks the changing seasons, governing the times, their lasting sign.
text.NRSV: The moon also, at its correct time, is the guide of the seasons and an everlasting sign.
text.DRB: And the moon in all in her time, is the shewing of season and the sign of age.
By the moon is the sign of a festival day, a light that diminisheth in consummation.
text.NABRE: By it we know the sacred seasons and pilgrimage feasts, a light which wanes in its course:
text.KJV: 43:7 From the Moone is the signe of Feasts, a light that decreaseth in her perfection.

text.DRB: The month is according to her name, increasing marvelously in consummation.
text.NABRE: The new moon like its name renews itself; how wondrous it is when it changes: A military signal for the waterskins on high, it paves the firmament with its brilliance,
text.KJV: 43:8 The moneth is called after her name, encreasing wonderfully in her changing, being an instrument of the armies aboue, shining in the firmament of heauen,

text.DRB: An instrument of the camp on high, shining gloriously in the firmament of heaven.
text.NABRE: The beauty of the heavens and the glory of the stars, a shining ornament in the heights of God.
text.KJV: 43:9 The beautie of heauen, the glory of the starres, an ornament giuing light in the highest places of the Lord.

text.DRB: The glory of the stars is the beauty of heaven, our Lord illuminating the world on high.
text.NABRE: By the Lord ’s command the moon keeps its appointed place, and does not fade as the stars keep watch.
text.KJV: 43:10 At the commandement of the holy One, they will stand in their order, and neuer faint in their watches.
43:11 Looke vpon the rainebow, and praise him that made it, very beautifull it is in the brightnesse thereof.
text.NABRE: Behold the rainbow! Then bless its Maker, for majestic indeed is its splendor;
text.NRSV: Look at the rainbow, and praise him who made it; it is exceedingly beautiful in its brightness.
text.DRB: In the words of the holy one they shall stand to judgement, and shall not fail in their watches.
See the bow, and bless him that made it: it is very beautiful in his brightness.
text.NABRE: It spans the heavens with its glory, the hand of God has stretched it out in power.
text.KJV: 43:12 It compasseth the heauen about with a glorious circle, and the hands of the most high haue bended it.
43:13 By his commandement hee maketh the snow to fall apace, and sendeth swiftly the lightnings of his iudgment.
text.NABRE: His rebuke marks out the path for the hail, and makes the flashes of his judgment shine forth.
text.NRSV: By his command he sends the driving snow and speeds the lightnings of his judgment.
text.DRB: It hath compassed heaven in the circuit of his glory, the hands of the Highest have opened it.
But by his commandment he hath hastened snow, & he hasteth to send forth the lightnings of his judgement.
text.NABRE: For his own purposes he opens the storehouse and makes the rain clouds fly like vultures.
text.KJV: 43:14 Through this the treasures are opened, and clouds flie forth as foules.

text.DRB: Therefore are the treasures opened, & the clouds fly forth as birds.
text.NABRE: His might gives the clouds their strength, and breaks off the hailstones.
text.KJV: 43:15 By his great power hee maketh the cloudes firme, and the hailestones are broken small.

text.DRB: By his greatness he hath set the clouds, & the hailstones are broken.
text.NABRE: The thunder of his voice makes the earth writhe; by his power he shakes the mountains.
text.KJV: 43:16 At his sight the mountaines are shaken, and at his will the South wind bloweth.

text.DRB: In his sight the mountains shall be moved, & at his will the south wind hath blown.
text.NABRE: A word from him drives on the south wind, whirlwind, hurricane, and stormwind. He makes the snow fly like birds; it settles down like swarms of locusts.
text.KJV: 43:17 The noise of the thunder maketh the earth to tremble: so doth the Northren storme, and the whirlewinde: as birds flying he scattereth the snow, and the falling downe thereof, is as the lighting of grashoppers.

text.DRB: The noise of his thunder shall beat the earth, the tempest of the northwind, and the gathering together of wind:
text.NABRE: Its shining whiteness blinds the eyes, the mind marvels at its steady fall.
text.KJV: 43:18 The eye marueileth at the beauty of the whitenesse thereof, and the heart is astonished at the raining of it.

text.DRB: and as the bird lighting down to sit, he scattereth snow, and the falling thereof, is as the locust diving downward.
text.NABRE: He scatters frost like salt; it shines like blossoms on the thornbush.
text.KJV: 43:19 The hoare frost also as salt hee powreth on the earth, and being congealed, it lieth on the toppe of sharpe stakes.

text.DRB: The eye shall admire the beauty of the whiteness thereof, and the heart quaketh at the shower thereof.
text.NABRE: He sends cold northern blasts that harden the ponds like solid ground, Spreads a crust over every body of water, and clothes each pool with a coat of armor.
text.KJV: 43:20 Wheu the colde North-winde bloweth, and the water is congealed into yce, it abideth vpon euery gathering together of water, and clotheth the water as with a brestplate.

text.DRB: He shall pour out frost upon the earth as salt: and when it freezeth, it shall be made as the tops of a thistle.
text.NABRE: When mountain growth is scorched by heat, and flowering plains as by fire,
text.KJV: 43:21 It deuoureth the mountaines, and burneth the wildernesse, and consumeth the grasse as fire.

text.DRB: The cold north wind blew, & of water there froze crystal, upon all gathering together of waters it shall rest, & as a breast plate it shall put itself upon the waters.
text.NABRE: The dripping clouds restore them all, and the scattered dew enriches the parched land.
text.KJV: 43:22 A present remedy of all is a miste comming speedily: a dew comming after heate, refresheth.

text.DRB: And it shall devour the mountains, & burn the desert, & extinguish that which is green as fire.
text.NABRE: His is the plan that calms the deep, and plants the islands in the sea.
text.KJV: 43:23 By his counsell he appeaseth the deepe, and planteth Ilands therein.

text.DRB: The remedy of all is in the hasty coming of a cloud, and a dew meeting it by the heat that cometh, shall make it quail.
text.NABRE: Those who go down to the sea recount its extent, and when we hear them we are thunderstruck;
text.KJV: 43:24 They that saile on the Sea, tell of the danger thereof, and when wee heare it with our eares, wee marueile thereat.

text.DRB: At his word the wind was still, and with his thought he appeased the depth, and our Lord planted Islands therein.
text.NABRE: In it are his creatures, stupendous, amazing, all kinds of life, and the monsters of the deep.
text.KJV: 43:25 For therein be strange and wonderous workes, varietie of all kindes of beasts, and whales created.

text.DRB: They that sail on the sea, tell the perils thereof: & hearing with our ears we shall marvel.
text.NABRE: For him each messenger succeeds, and at his bidding accomplishes his will.
text.KJV: 43:26 By him the ende of them hath prosperous successe, and by his word all things consist.
43:27 We may speake much, & yet come short: wherefore in summe, he is all.
text.NABRE: More than this we need not add; let the last word be, he is the all!
text.NRSV: We could say more but could never say enough; let the final word be, “He is the all.”
text.DRB: There are goodly works, & marvelous: diverse kinds of beasts, & of all cattle, & the creature of mighty beasts.
Through him is the end of their journey confirmed, and by his word all things are set in order.
text.NABRE: Let us praise him the more, since we cannot fathom him, for greater is he than all his works;
text.KJV: 43:28 How shall wee be able to magnifie him? for hee is great aboue all his workes.

text.DRB: We shall say many things, & shall fail in words; but the sum of our words is, He is in all.
text.NABRE: Awesome indeed is the Lord, and wonderful his power.
text.KJV: 43:29 The Lord is terrible and very great, and marueilous is his power.

43:30 When you glorifie the Lord exalt him as much as you can: for euen yet wil he farre exceed, and when you exalt him, put foorth all your strength, and be not weary: for you can neuer goe farre enough.
text.NABRE: Lift up your voices to glorify the Lord as much as you can, for there is still more. Extol him with renewed strength, do not grow weary, for you cannot fathom him.
text.NRSV: Exalt him as much as you can, for he will surpass even that; and when you exalt him, summon all your strength, and do not grow weary, for you can never exalt him enough.
text.DRB: Glorifying him how far shall we be able? For the omnipotent himself is above all his works.
Our Lord is terrible, & exceeding great, & his might is marvelous.
text.NABRE: For who has seen him and can describe him? Who can praise him as he is?
text.KJV: 43:31 Who hath seene him, that hee might tell vs? and who can magnifie him as he is?

text.DRB: Glorifying our Lord as much as ever you can, he shall yet surpass, and his magnificence is marvelous.
text.NABRE: Beyond these, many things lie hidden; only a few of his works have I seen.
text.KJV: 43:32 There are yet hid greater things then these be, for wee haue seene but a few of his workes:

text.DRB: Blessing our Lord, exalt him as much as you can: for he is greater than all praise.
text.NABRE: It is the Lord who has made all things; to those who fear him he gives wisdom.
text.KJV: 43:33 For the Lord hath made all things, and to the godly hath hee giuen wisedome.

text.DRB: Exalting him be ye replenished with strength. Labour not: for you shall not comprehend.
Who shall see him, and shall declare him? And who shall magnify him as he is from the beginning?
Many things hid are greater than these: for we have seen few of his works.
But our Lord made all things, and to them that live piously he hath given wisdom.

text.KJV: 44:1 Let vs now praise famous men, and our Fathers that begat vs.
text.NABRE: Chapter 44 Praise of Israel’s Great Ancestors I will now praise the godly, our ancestors, in their own time,
text.NRSV: Let us now sing the praises of famous men, our ancestors in their generations.
text.DRB: Let us praise glorious men, and our fathers in their generation.

text.KJV: 44:2 The Lorde hath wrought great glory by them, through his great power from the beginning.
text.NABRE: The abounding glory of the Most High’s portion, his own part, since the days of old.
text.NRSV: The Lord apportioned to them great glory, his majesty from the beginning.
text.DRB: Much glory hath our Lord made by his magnificence from the beginning of the world.
Ruling in their dominions, men great for force, and endued with their wisdom, declaring in the prophets the dignity of prophets,
text.NABRE: Subduers of the land in kingly fashion, renowned for their might, Counselors in their prudence, seers of all things in prophecy,
text.KJV: 44:3 Such as did beare rule in their kingdomes, men renowmed for their power, giuing counsell by their vnderstanding, and declaring prophecies:

text.DRB: and ruling in the people that was present, and by the virtue of wisdom most holy words to the peoples.
text.NABRE: Resolute princes of the flock, lawgivers and their rules, Sages skilled in composition, authors of sharp proverbs,
text.KJV: 44:4 Leaders of the people by their counsels, and learning meet for the people, wise and eloquent in their instructions.

text.DRB: In their skill seeking out musical melodies, & uttering songs of scriptures.
text.NABRE: Composers of melodious psalms, writers of lyric poems;
text.KJV: 44:5 Such as found out musical tunes, and reiected verses in writing.

text.DRB: Rich men in force studying beautifulness: living at peace in their houses.
text.NABRE: Stalwart, solidly established, at peace in their own estates—
text.KJV: 44:6 Rich men furnished with abilitie, liuing peaceably in their habitations.

text.DRB: All these in the generations of their nation have obtained glory, and in their days are praised.
text.NABRE: All these were glorious in their time, illustrious in their day.
text.KJV: 44:7 All these were honoured in their generations, and were the glory of their times.

text.DRB: They that were born of them have left a name to tell their praises:
text.NABRE: Some of them left behind a name so that people recount their praises.
text.KJV: 44:8 There be of them, that haue left a name behind them, that their praises might be reported.
44:9 And some there be, which haue no memorial, who are perished as though they had neuer bene, and are become as though they had neuer bene borne, and their children after them.
text.NABRE: Of others no memory remains, for when they perished, they perished, As if they had never lived, they and their children after them.
text.NRSV: But of others there is no memory; they have perished as though they had never existed;
text.DRB: and there are some of whom there is no memory: they are perished, as they that never were; & are born, as not born at all, & their children with them.

text.KJV: 44:10 But these were mercifull men, whose righteousnesse hath not beene forgotten.
text.NABRE: Yet these also were godly; their virtues have not been forgotten.
text.NRSV: But these also were godly men, whose righteous deeds have not been forgotten;
text.DRB: But they are men of mercy, whose godly deeds have not failed:
good things continue with their seed,
text.NABRE: Their wealth remains in their families, their heritage with their descendants.
text.KJV: 44:11 With their seed shall continually remaine a good inheritance, and their children are within the couenant.

text.DRB: their nephews are an holy inheritance, and their seed hath stood in the testaments:
text.NABRE: Through God’s covenant their family endures, and their offspring for their sake.
text.KJV: 44:12 Their seed stands fast, and their children for their sakes.
44:13 Their seed shall remaine for euer, and their glory shall not be blotted out.
text.NABRE: And for all time their progeny will endure, their glory will never be blotted out;
text.NRSV: Their offspring will continue forever, and their glory will never be blotted out.
text.DRB: and their children because of them abide for ever: their seed and their glory shall not be forsaken.

text.KJV: 44:14 Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name liueth for euermore.
text.NABRE: Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on and on.
text.NRSV: Their bodies are buried in peace, but their name lives on to generation after generation.
text.DRB: Their bodies are buried in peace, and their name liveth unto generation and generation.
Let peoples tell their wisdom, and the Church declare their praise.
text.NABRE: At gatherings their wisdom is retold, and the assembly proclaims their praises.
text.KJV: 44:15 The people will tell of their wisdome, and the congregation will shew forth their praise.
44:16 Enoch pleased the Lord, and was translated, being an example of repentance, to all generations.
text.NABRE: The Early Ancestors [ Enoch walked with the Lord and was taken, that succeeding generations might learn by his example.]
text.NRSV: Enoch pleased the Lord and was taken up, an example of repentance to all generations.
text.DRB: Henoch pleased God, and was translated into paradise, that he may give repentance to the nations.

text.KJV: 44:17 Noah was found perfect and righteous, in the time of wrath, he was taken in exchange (for the world) therefore was he left as a remnant vnto the earth, when the flood came.
text.NABRE: Noah, found just and perfect, renewed the race in the time of devastation. Because of his worth there were survivors, and with a sign to him the deluge ended.
text.NRSV: Noah was found perfect and righteous; in the time of wrath he kept the race alive.
text.DRB: Noe was found perfect, just, and in the time of wrath he was made a reconciliation.
Therefore was there a remnant left to the earth, when the flood was made.
text.NABRE: A lasting covenant was made with him, that never again would all flesh be destroyed.
text.KJV: 44:18 An euerlasting Couenant was made with him, that all flesh should perish no more by the flood.
44:19 Abraham was a great father of many people: in glory was there none like vnto him:
text.NABRE: Abraham, father of many peoples, kept his glory without stain:
text.NRSV: Abraham was the great father of a multitude of nations, and no one has been found like him in glory.
text.DRB: The testaments of the world were made with him, that all flesh should no more be destroyed with the flood.
Abraham the great father of the multitude of the nations, and there was not found the like to him in glory, who kept the law of the Highest, and was in covenant with him.
text.NABRE: He observed the Most High’s command, and entered into a covenant with him; In his own flesh he incised the ordinance, and when tested was found loyal.
text.KJV: 44:20 Who kept the Law of the most High, and was in couenant with him, hee established the Couenant in his flesh, and when he was proued, he was found faithfull.

text.DRB: In his flesh he made the covenant to stand, and in tentation he was found faithful.
text.NABRE: For this reason, God promised him with an oath to bless the nations through his descendants, To make him numerous as grains of dust, and to exalt his posterity like the stars, Giving them an inheritance from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 44:21 Therefore he assured him by an othe, that he would blesse the nations in his seed, and that he would multiply him, as the dust of the earth, and exalt his seed as the starres, and cause them to inherit from Sea to Sea, & from the riuer vnto the vtmost part of the land.

text.DRB: Therefore by an oath he gave him glory in his nation, that he should increase as an heap of earth,
text.NABRE: For Isaac, too, he renewed the same promise because of Abraham, his father. The covenant with all his forebears was confirmed,
text.KJV: 44:22 With Isaac did he establish likewise  the blessing of all men and the couenant,

text.DRB: and that he would exalt his seed as the stars, and they should inherit from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: and the blessing rested upon the head of Israel. God acknowledged him as the firstborn, and gave him his inheritance. He fixed the boundaries for his tribes and their division into twelve.
text.KJV: 44:23 And made it rest vpon the head of Iacob. Hee acknowledged him in his blessing, and gaue him an heritage, and diuided his portions, among the twelue tribes did he part them.

text.DRB: And he did in like manner in Isaac for Abraham his father.
Our Lord gave him the blessing of all nations, and confirmed his covenant upon the head of Jacob.
He knew him in his blessings, and gave him an inheritance, & divided him his portion in twelve tribes.
And he preserved unto him men of mercy, and found grace in the eyes of all flesh.

text.KJV: 45:1 And he brought out of him a mercifull man, which found fauour in the sight of all flesh, euen Moses beloued of God and men, whose memoriall is blessed:
text.NABRE: Chapter 45 Praise of Moses, Aaron, and Phinehas From him came the man who would win the favor of all the living: Dear to God and human beings, Moses, whose memory is a blessing.
text.NRSV: From Abraham’s descendants the Lord brought forth a godly man, who found favor in the sight of all and was beloved by God and people, Moses, whose memory is blessed.
text.DRB: Moyses beloved of God, and men: whose memory is in benediction.

text.KJV: 45:2 He made him like to the glorious Saints, and magnified him, so that his enemies stood in feare of him.
text.NABRE: God made him like the angels in honor, and strengthened him with fearful powers.
text.NRSV: He made him equal in glory to the holy ones, and made him great, to the terror of his enemies.
text.DRB: He made him like in the glory of saints, and magnified him in the fear of his enemies. And with his words he appeased monsters.
He glorified him in the sight of kings, and gave him commandment before his people, & shewed him his glory.
text.NABRE: At his words God performed signs and sustained him in the king’s presence. He gave him the commandments for his people, and revealed to him his glory.
text.KJV: 45:3 By his words he caused the wonders to cease, and he made him glorious in the sight of kings, and gaue him a commaundement for his people, and shewed him part of his glory.

text.DRB: In his faith and meekness he made him holy, and chose him of all flesh.
text.NABRE: Because of his trustworthiness and meekness God selected him from all flesh;
text.KJV: 45:4 He sanctified him in his faithfulnesse, and meekenesse, and chose him out of all men.

45:5 He made him to heare his voyce, and brought him into the darke cloud, and gaue him commandements before his face, euen the law of life and knowledge, that hee might teach Iacob his Couenants, and Israel his iudgments.
text.NABRE: He let him hear his voice, and led him into the cloud, Where he handed over the commandments, the law of life and understanding, That he might teach his precepts to Jacob, his judgments and decrees to Israel.
text.NRSV: He allowed him to hear his voice, and led him into the thick darkness, and gave him the commandments face to face.
text.DRB: For he heard him, and his voice, and brought him into a cloud.

text.KJV: 45:6 He exalted Aaron an holy man like vnto him, euen his brother, of the tribe of Leui.
text.NABRE: He also raised up, like Moses in holiness, his brother Aaron, of the tribe of Levi.
text.NRSV: He exalted Aaron, a holy man like Moses who was his brother, of the tribe of Levi.
text.DRB: And he gave him precepts face to face, and a law of life and discipline, to teach Jacob his testament, and Israel his judgements.
He exalted Aaron his brother high, and like to himself of the tribe of Levi.
text.NABRE: He made his office perpetual and bestowed on him priesthood for his people; He established him in honor and crowned him with lofty majesty.
text.KJV: 45:7 An euerlasting couenant he made with him, and gaue him the priesthood among the people, he beautified him with comely ornaments, and clothed him with a robe of glory.

text.DRB: He established unto him an everlasting testament, and gave him the Priesthood of the nation, and made him blessed in glory,
text.NABRE: He clothed him in splendid garments, and adorned him with glorious vestments: Breeches, tunic, and robe
text.KJV: 45:8 Hee put vpon him perfect glory: and strengthened him with rich garments, with breeches, with a long robe, and the Ephod:

text.DRB: and he girded him about with a girdle, and put upon him a robe of glory, and crowned him in furniture of power.
text.NABRE: with pomegranates at the hem And a rustle of bells round about, whose pleasing sound at each step Would make him heard within the sanctuary, a reminder for the people;
text.KJV: 45:9 And he compassed him with pomegranates, and with many golden bels round about, that as he went, there might be a sound, and a noise made that might be heard in the Temple, for a memoriall to the children of his people.

text.DRB: Garments to the feet, and breeches, and an Ephod he put upon him, and compassed him with little bells of gold very many round about,
text.NABRE: The sacred vestments of gold, violet, and crimson, worked with embroidery; The breastpiece for decision, the ephod and cincture
text.KJV: 45:10 With an holy garment, with gold and blew silke, and purple the worke of the embroiderer; with a brestplate of iudgement, and with Urim & Thummim.

text.DRB: to give a sound in his going, to make sound heard in the temple for a memory to the children of his nation.
text.NABRE: with scarlet yarn, the work of the weaver; Precious stones with seal engravings in golden settings, the work of the jeweler, To commemorate in incised letters each of the tribes of Israel;
text.KJV: 45:11 With twisted scarlet, the worke of the cunning workeman, with precious stones grauen like seales, and set in gold, the worke of the Ieweller, with a writing engraued for a memoriall, after the number of the tribes of Israel.

text.DRB: An holy robe, of gold, and hyacinth, and purple, a woven work, of a wiseman, endued with judgement and truth:
text.NABRE: On his turban a diadem of gold, its plate engraved with the sacred inscription— Majestic, glorious, renowned for splendor, a delight to the eyes, supremely beautiful.
text.KJV: 45:12 He set a crowne of gold vpon the miter, wherein was engraued holinesse an ornament of honour, a costly worke, the desires of the eies goodly & beautiful.

text.DRB: Of twisted scarlet the work of an artificer, with precious stones figured in the closure of gold, and graven by the work of a lapidary for a memorial, according to the number of the tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: Before him, no one had been adorned with these, nor may they ever be worn by any other Except his sons and them alone, generation after generation, for all time.
text.KJV: 45:13 Before him there were none such, neither did euer any stranger put them on, but onely his children, and his childrens children perpetually.

text.DRB: Acrown of gold upon his mitre graven with a seal of holiness, and the glory of honour: a work of power, & the adorned desires of the eyes.
text.NABRE: His grain offering is wholly burnt as an established offering twice each day;
text.KJV: 45:14 Their sacrifices shall be wholy consumed euery day twise continually.

45:15 Moises consecrated him, and annointed him with holy oile, this was appointed vnto him by an euerlasting couenant, and to his seed so long as the heauens should remaine, that they should minister vnto him, and execute the office of the priesthood, and blesse the people in his name.
text.NABRE: For Moses ordained him and anointed him with the holy oil, In a lasting covenant with him and his family, as permanent as the heavens, That he should serve God in the priesthood and bless the people in his name.
text.NRSV: Moses ordained him and anointed him with holy oil; this was an everlasting covenant for him and for his descendants as long as the heavens endure.
text.DRB: There were none such so fair before him, even from the beginning.
No stranger was clothed with them, but only his children alone, and his nephews for ever.
text.NABRE: He chose him from all the living to sacrifice burnt offerings and choice portions, To burn incense, sweet odor as a memorial, and to atone for the people of Israel.
text.KJV: 45:16 He chose him out of all men liuing to offer sacrifices to the Lord, incense and a sweet sauour, for a memoriall, to make reconciliation for his people.

text.DRB: His sacrifices were consumed with fire every day.
text.NABRE: He gave to him the laws, and authority to prescribe and to judge: To teach precepts to the people, and judgments to the Israelites.
text.KJV: 45:17 He gaue vnto him his commandements, and authority in the statutes of iudgements, that he should teach Iacob the testimonies, and informe Israel in his lawes.

text.DRB: Moyses filled his hands, & anointed him with holy oil.
text.NABRE: Strangers rose in anger against him, grew jealous of him in the desert— The followers of Dathan and Abiram, and the band of Korah in their defiance.
text.KJV: 45:18 Strangers conspired together against him, and maligned him in the wildernesse, euen the men that were of Dathans, and Abirons side, and the congregation of Core with fury and wrath.

text.DRB: It was made unto him for an everlasting testament, and to his seed as the days of heaven, to do the function of Priesthood, and to have praise and to glorify his people in his name.
text.NABRE: When the Lord saw this he became angry, and destroyed them in his burning wrath. He brought against them a marvel, and consumed them in flaming fire.
text.KJV: 45:19 This the Lord saw and it displeased him, and in his wrathfull indignation, were they consumed: he did wonders vpon them, to consume them with the fiery flame.

text.DRB: He chose him of all that lived, to offer sacrifice to God, incense, and good odour, for a memorial to pacify for his people:
text.NABRE: Then he increased the glory of Aaron and bestowed upon him his inheritance: The sacred offerings he allotted to him, with the showbread as his portion;
text.KJV: 45:20 But he made Aaron more honourable, and gaue him an heritage, and diuided vnto him the first fruits of the encrease, especially he prepared bread in abundance:

text.DRB: and he gave them power in his precepts, in the testaments of his judgements, to teach Jacob his testimonies, and in his law to give light to Israel.
text.NABRE: The oblations of the Lord are his food, a gift to him and his descendants.
text.KJV: 45:21 For they eate of the sacrifices of the Lord, which he gaue vnto him and his seed:

text.DRB: Because strangers stood against him, and for envy men compassed him about in the desert, they that were with Dathan and Abiron, and the congregation of Core in anger.
text.NABRE: But he holds no land among the people nor shares with them their heritage; For the Lord himself is his portion and inheritance among the Israelites.
text.KJV: 45:22 Howbeit in the land of the people he had no inheritance, neither had he any portion among the people, for the Lord himselfe is his portion and inheritance.

text.DRB: Our Lord God saw, and it please him not, and they were consumed in the violence of wrath.
text.NABRE: Phinehas too, the son of Eleazar, was the courageous third of his line When, zealous for the God of all, he met the crisis of his people And, at the prompting of his noble heart, atoned for the children of Israel.
text.KJV: 45:23 The third in glory is Phinees the sonne of Eleazar, because he had zeale in the feare of the Lord, and stood vp with good courage of heart, when the people were turned backe, and made reconciliation for Israel.

text.DRB: He did prodigious things unto them, and consumed them in flame of fire.
text.NABRE: Therefore, on him also God conferred the right, in a covenant of friendship, to provide for the sanctuary, So that he and his descendants should possess the high priesthood forever.
text.KJV: 45:24 Therfore was there a couenant of peace made with him, that he should be the cheefe of the sanctuary, and of his people, and that he, and his posteritie should haue the dignitie of the Priesthood for euer.

text.DRB: And he added glory to Aaron, and gave him an inheritance, and divided unto him the first fruits of the increase of the earth.
text.NABRE: For even his covenant with David, the son of Jesse of the tribe of Judah, Was an individual heritage through one son alone; but the heritage of Aaron is for all his descendants. So now bless the Lord who has crowned you with glory!
text.KJV: 45:25 According to the couenant made with Dauid sonne of Iesse, of the tribe of Iuda, that the inheritance of the king should be to his posterity alone: so the inheritance of Aaron should also be vnto his seed.

text.DRB: He prepared them bread in the first unto satiety: for the sacrifices also of our Lord they shall eat, which he gave to him, and to his seed.
text.NABRE: May he grant you wisdom of heart to govern his people in justice, Lest the benefits you confer should be forgotten, or your authority, throughout all time.
text.KJV: 45:26 God giue you wisedome in your heart to iudge his people in righteousnesse, that their good things be not abolished, and that their glory may endure for euer.

text.DRB: But he shall not inherit the nations in the land, and he hath no part in the nation: for himself is his portion & inheritance.
Phinees the son of Eleazar is the third in glory, in imitating him in the fear of our Lord:
and to stand in the reverence of the nation: in the goodness and alacrity of his soul he pacified God for Israel.
Therefore did he establish unto him a covenant of peace, to be the Prince of the holies, and to his nation, that the dignity of Priesthood should be to him and to his seed for ever.

And the testament to David king, the son of Jesse of the tribe of Juda, and inheritance to him and to his seed, that he might give wisdom into our heart to judge his nation in justice, that their good things might not be abolished, & their glory in their nation he made everlasting.

text.KJV: 46:1 Iesus the sonne of Naue was valiant in the wars, and was the successor of Moses in prophesies, who according to his name was made great for the sauing of the elect of God, and taking vengeance of the enemies that rose vp against them, that he might set Israel in their inheritance.
text.NABRE: Chapter 46 Joshua, Caleb, the Judges, and Samuel Valiant warrior was Joshua, son of Nun, aide to Moses in the prophetic office, Formed to be, as his name implies, the great savior of God’s chosen ones, To punish the enemy and to give to Israel their heritage.
text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun was mighty in war, and was the successor of Moses in prophesying. He became, in accordance with his name, a great savior of God’s elect.
text.DRB: Strong in battle was Jesus the son of Nave, successor of Moyses among the prophets, who was great according to his name,
most great in the salvation of God's elect, to overthrow the enemies rising up, that he might get the inheritance of Israel.
text.NABRE: What glory was his when he raised his hand, to brandish his sword against the city!
text.KJV: 46:2 How great glory gat he when he did lift vp his hands, and stretched out his sword against the cities?

text.DRB: What glory obtained he in lifting up his hands, and casting swords against the cities?
text.NABRE: Who could withstand him when he fought the battles of the Lord?
text.KJV: 46:3 Who before him so stood to it? for the Lord himselfe brought his enemies vnto him.

text.DRB: Who before him did so resist? For our Lord himself brought the enemies.
text.NABRE: Was it not by that same hand the sun stopped, so that one day became two?
text.KJV: 46:4 Did not the Sunne goe backe by his meanes? and was not one day as long as two?

text.DRB: Whether was not the sun hindered in his anger, and one day was made as two?
text.NABRE: He called upon the Most High God when his enemies beset him on all sides, And God Most High answered him with hailstones of tremendous power,
text.KJV: 46:5 He called vpon the most high Lord, when the enemies pressed vpon him on euery side, & the great Lord heard him.

text.DRB: He invocated the mighty sovereign in assaulting of the enemies on every side, and the great and holy God heard him in hail-stones of exceeding great force.
text.NABRE: That rained down upon the hostile army till on the slope he destroyed the foe; That all the doomed nations might know the Lord was watching over his people’s battles. He was indeed a devoted follower of God
text.KJV: 46:6 And with hailestones of mighty power he made the battell to fall violently vpon the nations, and in the descent (of Bethoron) hee destroyed them that resisted, that the nations might know all their strength, because hee fought in the sight of the Lord, and he followed the mightie one.

46:7 In the time of Moses also, he did a worke of mercie, hee and Caleb the sonne of Iephunne, in that they withstood the Congregation, and withheld the people from sinne, and appeased the wicked murmuring.
text.NABRE: and showed himself loyal in Moses’ lifetime. He and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, when they opposed the rebel assembly, Averted God’s anger from the people and suppressed the wicked complaint.
text.NRSV: In the days of Moses also he did a loyal deed, he and Caleb son of Jephunneh: they withstood the congregation, restrained the people from sin, and stilled the wicked grumbling.
text.DRB: He made violent assault against the nation of his enemies, and in the going down he destroyed the adversaries,
that the nations might know his might, that it is not easy to fight against God. And he followed at the back of the mighty.
text.NABRE: Because of this, these two alone were spared from the six hundred thousand infantry, To lead the people into their heritage, the land flowing with milk and honey.
text.KJV: 46:8 And of sixe hundred thousand people on foot, they two were preserued to bring them into the heritage, euen vnto the land that floweth with milk & hony.

text.DRB: And in the days of Moyses did mercy, and Caleb the son of Jephone, did stand against the enemy, and stayed the nation from sins, and appeased the murmuring of malice.
text.NABRE: The strength God gave to Caleb remained with him even in old age Till he won his way onto the summits of the land; his family too received a heritage,
text.KJV: 46:9 The Lord gaue strength also vnto Caleb, which remained with him vnto his old age, so that he entred vpon the high places of the land, and his seed obtained it for an heritage.

text.DRB: And they two being appointed, were delivered out of danger from among the number of six hundred thousand foot men, to bring them into their inheritance, into the land that yieldeth milk and honey.
text.NABRE: That all the offspring of Jacob might know how good it is to be a devoted follower of the Lord.
text.KJV: 46:10 That all the children of Israel might see that it is good to follow the Lord.
46:11 And concerning the Iudges, euery one by name, whose heart went not a whoring, nor departed from the Lord, let their memory be blessed.
text.NABRE: The Judges, each one of them, whose hearts were not deceived, Who did not abandon God— may their memory be ever blessed!
text.NRSV: The judges also, with their respective names, whose hearts did not fall into idolatry and who did not turn away from the Lord—may their memory be blessed!
text.DRB: And our Lord gave strength to Caleb himself, and his strength continued even until old age, so that he went up into the high place of the land, & his seed obtained inheritance.
That all the children of Israel might see, that it is good to obey the holy God.
text.NABRE: May their bones flourish with new life where they lie, and their names receive fresh luster in their children!
text.KJV: 46:12 Let their bones flourish out of their place, and let the name of them that were honoured, be continued vpon their children.
46:13 Samuel the Prophet of the Lord, beloued of his Lord, established a kingdom, & anointed princes ouer his people.
text.NABRE: Beloved of his people, dear to his Maker, pledged in a vow from his mother’s womb, As one consecrated to the Lord in the prophetic office, was Samuel, the judge who offered sacrifice. At God’s word he established the kingdom and anointed princes to rule the people.
text.NRSV: Samuel was beloved by his Lord; a prophet of the Lord, he established the kingdom and anointed rulers over his people.
text.DRB: And all the judges by their name, whose heart was not corrupted: which were not turned away from our Lord,
that their memory might be blessed, and their bones spring out of their place,
text.NABRE: By the law of the Lord he judged the congregation, and visited the encampments of Jacob.
text.KJV: 46:14 By the Law of the Lord hee iudged the Congregation, and the Lord had respect vnto Iacob.

text.DRB: and their name continue for ever, the glory of the holy men remaining unto their children.
text.NABRE: As a trustworthy prophet he was sought out and his words proved him to be a true seer.
text.KJV: 46:15 By his faithfulnes he was found a true Prophet, and by his word he was knowen to be faithfull in vision.

text.DRB: The beloved of our Lord his God Samuel the prophet of our Lord, renewed the empire, and anointed princes in his nation.
text.NABRE: He, too, called upon the mighty Lord when his enemies pressed him on every side, and offered up a suckling lamb.
text.KJV: 46:16 He called vpon the mighty Lord, when his enemies pressed vpon him on euery side, when he offered the sucking lambe.

text.DRB: By the law of our Lord he judged the congregation, and the God of Jacob saw, and in his fidelity was proved a prophet.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord thundered from heaven, and the tremendous roar of his voice was heard.
text.KJV: 46:17 And the Lord thundered from heauen, and with a great noise made his voice to be heard.

text.DRB: And he was known faithful in his words, because he saw the God of light:
text.NABRE: He brought low the rulers of the enemy and destroyed all the lords of the Philistines.
text.KJV: 46:18 And he destroyed the rulers of the Tyrians, and all the princes of the Philistines.

text.DRB: and invocated our Lord Omnipotent, in assaulting the enemies besetting him on every side, in the oblation of an immaculate lamb.
text.NABRE: When Samuel neared the end of life, he testified before the Lord and his anointed prince, “No bribe or secret gift have I taken from anyone!” and no one could accuse him.
text.KJV: 46:19 And before his long sleepe hee made protestations in the sight of the Lord, and his anoynted, I haue not taken any mans goods, so much as a shoe, and no man did accuse him.
46:20 And after his death he prophesied, and shewed the King his end, and lift vp his voyce from the earth in prophesie, to blot out the wickednesse of the people.
text.NABRE: Even after death his guidance was sought; he made known to the king his fate. From the grave he spoke in prophecy to put an end to wickedness.
text.NRSV: Even after he had fallen asleep, he prophesied and foretold to the king his death, and lifted up his voice from the ground in prophecy, to blot out the wickedness of the people.
text.DRB: And our Lord thundered from heaven, and in great sound he made his voice heard,
and he discomfited the Princes of the Tyrians, and all the dukes of the Philisthiims:

and before the time of the end of his life, and the world, he gave testimony before our Lord, and his Christ, money and what soever besides unto the very shoes he took not of all flesh, and no man accused him.
And after this he slept, and he notified to the king, and shewed him the end of his life, and he exalted his voice out of the earth in prophecy, to take clean away the impiety of the nation.

text.KJV: 47:1 And after him rose vp Nathan to prophesie in the time of Dauid.
text.NABRE: Chapter 47 Nathan, David, and Solomon After him came Nathan who served in David’s presence.
text.NRSV: After him Nathan rose up to prophesy in the days of David.
text.DRB: After these things arose Nathan the Prophet in the days of David.

text.KJV: 47:2 As is the fat taken away from the peace offering, so was Dauid chosen out of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Like the choice fat of sacred offerings, so was David in Israel.
text.NRSV: As the fat is set apart from the offering of well-being, so David was set apart from the Israelites.
text.DRB: And as the fat separated from the flesh, so was David from the children of Israel.
He played with lions as it were with lambs: and with bears he did in like manner as with lambs of sheep in his youth.
text.NABRE: He played with lions as though they were young goats, and with bears, like lambs of the flock.
text.KJV: 47:3 Hee played with Lions as with kids, and with beares as with lambs.

text.DRB: Did not he kill the giant, & took away reproach from his nation?
text.NABRE: As a youth he struck down the giant and wiped out the people’s disgrace; His hand let fly the slingstone that shattered the pride of Goliath.
text.KJV: 47:4 Slew he not a gyant when hee was yet but yong? and did he not take away reproch from the people, when he lifted vp his hand with the stone in the sling, and beat downe the boasting of Goliah?

text.DRB: In lifting up his hand, with a stone of the sling he overthrew the boasting of Goliath:
text.NABRE: For he had called upon the Most High God, who gave strength to his right arm To defeat the skilled warrior and establish the might of his people.
text.KJV: 47:5 For he called vpon the most high Lord, and he gaue him strength in his right hand to slay that mighty warriour, and set vp the horne of his people:

text.DRB: for he invocated our Lord the omnipotent, and he gave in his right hand, to take away the man strong in battle, and to exalt the horn of his nation.
text.NABRE: Therefore the women sang his praises and honored him for “the tens of thousands.” When he received the royal crown, he battled
text.KJV: 47:6 So the people honoured him with ten thousands, and praised him in the blessings of the Lord, in that hee gaue him a crowne of glory.

text.DRB: So in ten thousand did he glorify him, and praised him in the blessings of our Lord, in offering to him a crown of glory:
text.NABRE: and subdued the enemy on every side. He campaigned against the hostile Philistines and shattered their power till our own day.
text.KJV: 47:7 For hee destroyed the enemies on euery side, and brought to nought the Philistines his aduersaries, and brake their horne in sunder vnto this day.
47:8 In all his workes hee praised the holy one most High, with words of glory, with his whole heart he sung songs, and loued him that made him.
text.NABRE: With his every deed he offered thanks to God Most High, in words of praise. With his whole heart he loved his Maker
text.NRSV: In all that he did he gave thanks to the Holy One, the Most High, with ascriptions of glory; he sang praise with all his heart, and he loved his Maker.
text.DRB: for he destroyed the enemies on every side, and rooted out the Philisthiims the adversaries even until this present day: he brake their horn for ever.
In every work he gave confession to the Holy one, and to the Highest, in the word of glory.
text.NABRE: and daily had his praises sung; He added beauty to the feasts and solemnized the seasons of each year With string music before the altar, providing sweet melody for the psalms So that when the Holy Name was praised, before daybreak the sanctuary would resound.
text.KJV: 47:9 He set singers also before the Altar, that by their voyces they might make sweet melody, and daily sing praises in their songs.

text.DRB: From all his heart he praised our Lord, & loved God, that made him, and gave him might against his enemies:
text.NABRE: and daily had his praises sung; He added beauty to the feasts and solemnized the seasons of each year With string music before the altar, providing sweet melody for the psalms So that when the Holy Name was praised, before daybreak the sanctuary would resound.
text.KJV: 47:10 He beautified their feasts, and set in order the solemne times, vntill the ende, that they might praise his holy Name, and that the Temple might sound from morning.
47:11 The Lord tooke away his sinnes, and exalted his horne for euer: he gaue him a couenant of kings, and a throne of glory in Israel.
text.NABRE: The Lord forgave him his sins and exalted his strength forever; He conferred on him the rights of royalty and established his throne in Israel.
text.NRSV: The Lord took away his sins, and exalted his power forever; he gave him a covenant of kingship and a glorious throne in Israel.
text.DRB: and he made singers to stand before the altar, and by their sound he made sweet tunes.

text.KJV: 47:12 After him rose vp a wise sonne, and for his sake he dwelt at large.
text.NABRE: Because of his merits he had as successor a wise son, who lived in security:
text.NRSV: After him a wise son rose up who because of him lived in security.
text.DRB: And in the solemnities he gave honour, and adorned the times even to the end of his life, that they should praise the holy name of our Lord, and magnify the holiness of God in the morning.

text.KJV: 47:13 Salomon reigned in a peaceable time, and was honoured; for God made all quiet round about him, that hee might build an house in his Name, and prepare his Sanctuary for euer.
text.NABRE: Solomon reigned during an era of peace, for God brought rest to all his borders. He built a house to the name of God, and established a lasting sanctuary.
text.NRSV: Solomon reigned in an age of peace, because God gave him rest on every side, so that he might build a house for his name and prepare a sanctuary to stand forever.
text.DRB: Our Lord purged his sins, and exalted his horn for ever: and he gave him a testament of the kingdom, and the seat of glory in Israel.

text.KJV: 47:14 How wise wast thou in thy youth, & as a flood filled with vnderstanding.
text.NABRE: How wise you were when you were young, overflowing with instruction, like the Nile in flood!
text.NRSV: How wise you were when you were young! You overflowed like the Nile with understanding.
text.DRB: After him arose a wise son, and for him did he overthrow all the might of the enemies.
Salomon reigned in days of peace, to whom God subdued all his enemies, that he might build an house in his name, & prepare holiness for ever: as thou art instructed in thy youth.
text.NABRE: Your understanding covered the whole earth, and, like a sea, filled it with knowledge.
text.KJV: 47:15 Thy soule couered the whole earth, and thou filledst it with dark parables.

text.DRB: And thou art replenished as a river with wisdom, and thy soul discovered the earth.
text.NABRE: Your fame reached distant coasts, and you were beloved for your peaceful reign.
text.KJV: 47:16 Thy name went farre vnto the Ilands, and for thy peace thou wast beloued.

text.DRB: And thou didst multiply dark sayings in comparisons: thy name was bruited to the islands far off, and thou wast beloved in thy peace.
text.NABRE: With song and proverb and riddle, and with your answers, you astounded the nations.
text.KJV: 47:17 The countreys marueiled at thee for thy Songs, and Prouerbs, and Parables, and interpretations.

text.DRB: The lands marveled at the songs and proverbs, and comparisons, and interpretations,
text.NABRE: You were called by that glorious name which was conferred upon Israel. Gold you gathered like so much iron; you heaped up silver as though it were lead.
text.KJV: 47:18 By the Name of the Lord God, which is called the Lord God of Israel, thou didst gather gold as tinne, and didst multiply siluer as lead.

text.DRB: and at the name of our Lord God, whose name is, God of Israel.
text.NABRE: But you abandoned yourself to women and gave them dominion over your body.
text.KJV: 47:19 Thou didst bow thy loines vnto women, and by thy body thou wast brought into subiection.

text.DRB: Thou didst gather gold as copper, and filledst silver as lead,
text.NABRE: You brought a stain upon your glory, shame upon your marriage bed, Wrath upon your descendants, and groaning upon your deathbed.
text.KJV: 47:20 Thou dist staine thy honour, and pollute thy seed, so that thou broughtest wrath vpon thy children, and wast grieued for thy folly.

text.DRB: and bowedst thy thighs to women: thou hast had power on thy body.
text.NABRE: Thus two governments came into being, when in Ephraim kingship was usurped.
text.KJV: 47:21 So the kingdome was diuided, and out of Ephraim ruled a rebellious kingdome.

text.DRB: Thou hast made a blot in thy glory, & profaned thy seed to bring wrath to thy children, & thy folly to be kindled,
